665	## API Endpoints
666	
667	### Content API (`/api/content`)
668	| Method | Path | Description |
669	|--------|------|-------------|
670	| `GET` | `/` | List all content items (with filtering: status, type, channel) |
671	| `POST` | `/` | Create a new content item |
672	| `GET` | `/{id}` | Get a single content item |
673	| `PUT` | `/{id}` | Update a content item (body, title, status, channel) |
674	| `DELETE` | `/{id}` | Delete a content item |
675	| `GET` | `/{id}/versions` | Get version history for a content item |
676	| `GET` | `/{id}/versions/{version}` | Get a specific version snapshot |
677	| `POST` | `/{id}/status` | Transition content status (with validation) |
678	
679	### Generation API (`/api/generation`)
680	| Method | Path | Description |
681	|--------|------|-------------|
682	| `POST` | `/generate` | Generate content (full response) |
683	| `POST` | `/stream` | Generate content with SSE streaming |
684	| `GET` | `/models` | List available AI models and their status |
685	
686	### Validation API (`/api/validation`)
687	| Method | Path | Description |
688	|--------|------|-------------|
689	| `POST` | `/validate/{content_id}` | Trigger validation for a content item |
690	| `GET` | `/history/{content_id}` | Get validation history for a content item |
691	| `GET` | `/status` | Check validation service availability |
692	
693	### Schedule API (`/api/schedule`)
694	| Method | Path | Description |
695	|--------|------|-------------|
696	| `GET` | `/calendar` | Get scheduled items for a date range |
697	| `POST` | `/{content_id}/schedule` | Schedule a content item |
698	| `PUT` | `/{content_id}/reschedule` | Reschedule a content item |
699	| `GET` | `/export/ical` | Export calendar as .ics file |
700	
701	### Dashboard API (`/api/dashboard`)
702	| Method | Path | Description |
703	|--------|------|-------------|
704	| `GET` | `/summary` | Pipeline summary (counts per status, recent items) |
705	| `GET` | `/upcoming` | Next 7 days of scheduled content |
706	
707	### SEO API (`/api/seo`)
708	| Method | Path | Description |
709	|--------|------|-------------|
710	| `GET` | `/{content_id}` | Get SEO metadata for a content item |
711	| `PUT` | `/{content_id}` | Update SEO metadata |
712	| `POST` | `/{content_id}/analyze` | Run readability analysis |
713	| `POST` | `/{content_id}/keywords` | Generate keyword suggestions via AI |
714	
715	## Implementation Phases
716	
717	### Phase 1: Foundation (Backend + Frontend Skeleton)
718	1. Set up project structure (backend and frontend directories)
719	2. Create FastAPI app with CORS, lifespan, and router structure
720	3. Define SQLModel data models and database initialization
721	4. Create React app with routing, Tailwind, and UI component library
722	5. Implement content CRUD (backend API + frontend list/create pages)
723	6. **Milestone**: Can create, list, edit, and delete content items
724	
725	### Phase 2: AI Generation Integration
726	1. Implement Claude provider with streaming
727	2. Implement OpenAI provider with streaming
728	3. Create AI service abstraction layer
729	4. Build generation API endpoints (full + streaming)
730	5. Build frontend GenerationPanel with model selector and streaming display
731	6. Implement content type templates (system prompts per type)
732	7. **Milestone**: Can generate content via Claude or ChatGPT with real-time streaming
733	
734	### Phase 3: Theological Validation
735	1. Implement NotebookLM provider with error handling and retry
736	2. Implement AI-based fallback validation provider
737	3. Create validation service with automatic failover
738	4. Build validation API endpoints
739	5. Build frontend ValidationPanel with score, flags, citations
740	6. Implement validation history tracking
741	7. **Milestone**: Can validate content with citation display and fallback
742	
743	### Phase 4: Calendar, SEO, and Polish
744	1. Build editorial calendar UI (week/month views)
745	2. Implement scheduling API and status transitions
746	3. Add iCal export
747	4. Build SEO metadata panel for blog posts
748	5. Implement content versioning
749	6. Build dashboard with pipeline summary
750	7. Portuguese localization of interface elements
751	8. **Milestone**: Full content lifecycle operational
752	
753	## Success Criteria
754	
755	The task is complete when:
756	
757	1. [ ] Project structure is set up with backend (FastAPI/Python) and frontend (React/TypeScript) directories
758	2. [ ] SQLite database is created with all required tables (content_items, content_versions, validation_records, seo_metadata)
759	3. [ ] Content CRUD operations work end-to-end (create, read, update, delete, list with filtering)
760	4. [ ] AI generation works with both Claude and ChatGPT, including real-time streaming to the editor
761	5. [ ] Content type templates (short video, long video, blog post) produce appropriately structured output
762	6. [ ] Theological validation via NotebookLM returns score, flags, and traceable citations
763	7. [ ] Validation fallback to AI-based provider works when NotebookLM is unavailable
764	8. [ ] Content status lifecycle (draft → validated → scheduled → published) is enforced and tracked
765	9. [ ] Editorial calendar displays scheduled items in week and month views
766	10. [ ] Portuguese SEO metadata tools work for blog posts (meta fields, readability, keyword suggestions)
767	11. [ ] Content versioning tracks edit history with version snapshots
768	12. [ ] iCal export produces valid .ics files
769	13. [ ] Dashboard shows pipeline summary with status counts and upcoming items
770	14. [ ] All API keys are managed via environment variables — none exposed in frontend
771	15. [ ] Frontend is Portuguese-first with consistent, responsive design
772	16. [ ] No console errors in normal operation
773	17. [ ] All existing tests pass, new tests cover core functionality
774	
775	## QA Acceptance Criteria
776	
777	**CRITICAL**: These criteria must be verified by the QA Agent before sign-off.
778	
779	### Unit Tests
780	
781	| Test | File | What to Verify |
782	|------|------|----------------|
783	| Content CRUD | `backend/tests/test_content.py` | Create, read, update, delete content items; validate field defaults; enforce required fields |
784	| Status Transitions | `backend/tests/test_content.py` | Valid transitions (draft→validated→scheduled→published); reject invalid transitions |
785	| Claude Provider | `backend/tests/test_generation.py` | Mock Anthropic client; verify correct model/params; handle APIError, RateLimitError |
786	| OpenAI Provider | `backend/tests/test_generation.py` | Mock OpenAI client; verify Chat Completions format; handle errors |
787	| Validation Service | `backend/tests/test_validation.py` | Primary→fallback failover; score/flag/citation parsing; unavailability detection |
788	| NotebookLM Provider | `backend/tests/test_validation.py` | Mock notebooklm client; parse AskResult with citations; handle RPCError |
789	| Schedule Service | `backend/tests/test_schedule.py` | Schedule/reschedule items; iCal export format; date range queries |
790	| SEO Service | `backend/tests/test_seo.py` | Readability score calculation; keyword suggestion format; metadata validation |
791	| Content Versioning | `backend/tests/test_content.py` | Version increment on save; snapshot creation; version retrieval |
792	
793	### Integration Tests
794	
795	| Test | Services | What to Verify |
796	|------|----------|----------------|
797	| Content → Generation | backend API + AI service | POST to /api/generation/generate returns valid content; POST to /api/generation/stream returns SSE events |
798	| Content → Validation | backend API + validation service | POST to /api/validation/validate/{id} returns ValidationResult; GET /api/validation/history/{id} returns history |
799	| Content → Schedule | backend API + schedule service | POST schedule updates content status; GET /api/schedule/calendar returns items in date range |

800	| Validation Failover | validation service + providers | When primary provider raises, fallback is used; response includes `provider` field indicating fallback |
801	| Dashboard Aggregation | backend API + database | GET /api/dashboard/summary returns correct counts per status |
802	
803	### End-to-End Tests
804	
805	| Flow | Steps | Expected Outcome |
806	|------|-------|------------------|
807	| Content Creation | 1. Navigate to content list 2. Click "New Content" 3. Select "Short Video" type 4. Enter title | Content item created with status "draft", appears in list |
808	| AI Generation | 1. Open content in editor 2. Select "Claude" model 3. Enter prompt 4. Click "Generate" | Text streams into editor in real-time; content body is updated |
809	| Theological Validation | 1. Open content with body text 2. Click "Validate" | Validation panel shows score (0-100%), any flags, and expandable citations |
810	| Schedule Content | 1. Validate content first 2. Click "Schedule" 3. Select date and channel | Content appears on editorial calendar at selected date; status changes to "scheduled" |
811	| Full Pipeline | 1. Create → 2. Generate → 3. Validate → 4. Schedule → 5. Mark Published | Content item transitions through all statuses correctly; appears in dashboard counts |
812	| Validation Fallback | 1. Disable NotebookLM (env var) 2. Trigger validation | Validation completes via AI fallback; panel shows "fallback" indicator |
813	
814	### Browser Verification
815	
816	| Page/Component | URL | Checks |
817	|----------------|-----|--------|
818	| Dashboard | `http://localhost:5173/` | Pipeline status counts display; upcoming items list populates; mini-calendar renders |
819	| Content List | `http://localhost:5173/content` | Content items load; filtering by status/type works; create button navigates to editor |
820	| Content Editor | `http://localhost:5173/content/{id}` | Editor loads content body; right panel shows validation results; generation panel has model selector |
821	| Generation Panel | `http://localhost:5173/content/{id}` | Model dropdown lists Claude + ChatGPT; streaming text appears character-by-character |
822	| Validation Panel | `http://localhost:5173/content/{id}` | Score displays as percentage with color coding; flags listed; citations expandable |
823	| Editorial Calendar | `http://localhost:5173/calendar` | Week/month toggle works; scheduled items appear on correct dates; click opens content |
824	| SEO Panel | `http://localhost:5173/content/{id}` (blog type) | SEO fields visible for blog posts only; readability score updates; keyword suggestions generate |
825	
826	### Database Verification
827	
828	| Check | Query/Command | Expected |
829	|-------|---------------|----------|
830	| Tables exist | `sqlite3 verbum.db ".tables"` | `content_items content_versions validation_records seo_metadata` |
831	| Content creation | `sqlite3 verbum.db "SELECT count(*) FROM content_items"` | Count increments after creating content |
832	| Status tracking | `sqlite3 verbum.db "SELECT status, count(*) FROM content_items GROUP BY status"` | Correct distribution across statuses |
833	| Validation records | `sqlite3 verbum.db "SELECT count(*) FROM validation_records WHERE content_id='{id}'"` | Records created after each validation |
834	| Version history | `sqlite3 verbum.db "SELECT count(*) FROM content_versions WHERE content_id='{id}'"` | Versions increment on saves |
835	| UTF-8 Portuguese | `sqlite3 verbum.db "SELECT title FROM content_items WHERE title LIKE '%ção%'"` | Portuguese characters stored and retrieved correctly |
836	
837	### Security Verification
838	
839	| Check | How to Verify | Expected |
840	|-------|---------------|----------|
841	| No API keys in frontend | Inspect network requests in browser DevTools | No `sk-ant-*` or `sk-*` keys in any request or response |
842	| API keys in env only | `grep -r "sk-ant\|sk-" frontend/` | No matches (keys only in backend/.env) |
843	| CORS configured | `curl -H "Origin: http://evil.com" http://localhost:8000/api/content` | Request blocked (only localhost:5173 allowed) |
844	| Environment validation | Remove ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from .env and restart backend | Clear error message at startup, not a runtime crash |
845	
846	### Performance Verification
847	
848	| Check | How to Verify | Target |
849	|-------|---------------|--------|
850	| AI generation latency | Time from "Generate" click to first streamed token | < 3 seconds |
851	| Validation latency | Time from "Validate" click to score display | < 5 seconds (NotebookLM), < 3 seconds (fallback) |
852	| Content list load | Navigate to content list with 50+ items | < 1 second page load |
853	| Calendar render | Navigate to calendar with 20+ scheduled items | < 1 second render |
854	
855	### QA Sign-off Requirements
856	- [ ] All unit tests pass (`cd backend && pytest`)
857	- [ ] All integration tests pass
858	- [ ] All E2E tests pass
859	- [ ] Browser verification complete (all pages render correctly)
860	- [ ] Database state verified (tables, relationships, UTF-8)
861	- [ ] No regressions in existing functionality
862	- [ ] Code follows established patterns (async everywhere, Protocol interfaces, SQLModel)
863	- [ ] No security vulnerabilities introduced (API keys secure, CORS configured)
864	- [ ] Portuguese content renders correctly throughout
865	- [ ] Streaming AI generation works smoothly in the browser
866	- [ ] Validation fallback triggers correctly when NotebookLM is unavailable
867	- [ ] Editorial calendar displays items on correct dates
868	- [ ] Content status transitions are enforced (cannot skip validation)

211	```python
212	# backend/app/main.py
213	from contextlib import asynccontextmanager
214	from fastapi import FastAPI
215	from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware
216	from app.core.database import init_db
217	from app.api import content, generation, validation, schedule, seo, dashboard
218	
219	@asynccontextmanager
220	async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):
221	    await init_db()
222	    yield
223	
224	app = FastAPI(title="Verbum API", lifespan=lifespan)
225	app.add_middleware(
226	    CORSMiddleware,
227	    allow_origins=["http://localhost:5173"],
228	    allow_credentials=True,
229	    allow_methods=["*"],
230	    allow_headers=["*"],
231	)
232	app.include_router(content.router, prefix="/api/content", tags=["content"])
233	app.include_router(generation.router, prefix="/api/generation", tags=["generation"])
234	app.include_router(validation.router, prefix="/api/validation", tags=["validation"])
235	app.include_router(schedule.router, prefix="/api/schedule", tags=["schedule"])
236	app.include_router(seo.router, prefix="/api/seo", tags=["seo"])
237	app.include_router(dashboard.router, prefix="/api/dashboard", tags=["dashboard"])

246	### Pattern 2: SQLModel Data Models with Status Enum
247	
248	```python
249	# backend/app/models/content.py
250	from enum import Enum
251	from datetime import datetime, timezone
252	from typing import Optional
253	from sqlmodel import SQLModel, Field
254	from uuid import uuid4
255	
256	class ContentType(str, Enum):
257	    SHORT_VIDEO = "short_video"
258	    LONG_VIDEO = "long_video"
259	    BLOG_POST = "blog_post"
260	
261	class ContentStatus(str, Enum):
262	    DRAFT = "draft"
263	    VALIDATED = "validated"
264	    SCHEDULED = "scheduled"
265	    PUBLISHED = "published"
266	
267	class ContentItem(SQLModel, table=True):
268	    __tablename__ = "content_items"
269	
270	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid4()), primary_key=True)
271	    title: str
272	    body: str = ""
273	    content_type: ContentType
274	    status: ContentStatus = ContentStatus.DRAFT
275	    language: str = "pt"
276	    channels: str = "[]"  # JSON list of channel strings, e.g. '["youtube", "blog"]'
277	    ai_model_used: Optional[str] = None
278	    scheduled_date: Optional[datetime] = None
279	    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
280	    updated_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
281	    version: int = 1
282	```
283	
284	**Key Points:**
285	- Use string enums for readable database values

462	3. **Theological Validation via NotebookLM (High Priority)**
463	   - Description: One-click validation sends content to NotebookLM for doctrinal review against loaded Catholic sources. Returns a validation score (0-100%), flag list for issues, and source citations from the Catholic corpus (Catechism, Vatican II, etc.).
464	   - Acceptance: Validation completes in under 5 seconds (target; NotebookLM response times are unverified — see Performance Verification), displays score + flags + citations in the right panel. If NotebookLM is unavailable, falls back to AI-based validation with a clear indicator.

=== SPEC CRITIQUE COMPLETE ===

**Issues Found: 6**
- High severity: 0
- Medium severity: 5
- Low severity: 1

**Fixes Applied: 6**
**Confidence Level: high**

---

### Summary of Issues Found & Fixed

| # | Severity | Category | Issue | Fix |
|---|----------|----------|-------|-----|
| 1 | **MEDIUM** | Accuracy | `datetime.utcnow()` deprecated in Python 3.12 (system runs 3.12.3) — would produce runtime warnings | Replaced with `datetime.now(timezone.utc)` pattern |
| 2 | **LOW** | Accuracy | Comment falsely claimed Python 3.11+ is "required by" FastAPI/notebooklm-py (they require 3.10+) | Corrected to explain 3.10+ is the package requirement, 3.11+ is a project choice |
| 3 | **MEDIUM** | Completeness | `main.py` Pattern 1 omitted the SEO router — despite SEO API being documented and `seo.py` listed in files | Added `seo` import and `app.include_router(seo.router, ...)` |
| 4 | **MEDIUM** | Completeness | `test_seo.py` missing from Files to Create table — but referenced in QA Acceptance Criteria | Added to the backend test files table |
| 5 | **MEDIUM** | Consistency | Validation latency: Requirement #3 said "<2s" but Performance Verification said "<5s"; research flags <2s as **unverified** | Aligned to <5s with caveat noting unverified response times |
| 6 | **MEDIUM** | Consistency | Requirement #8 says "one or more channels" but data model had `channel: str` (single value) | Changed to `channels: JSON (list of strings)` in both model and data schema |

### What's Solid (No Issues)
- ✅ All package names match research exactly (`anthropic`, `openai`, `notebooklm-py`, `fastapi`, `sqlmodel`, `aiosqlite`, `icalendar`)
- ✅ API patterns correct (Chat Completions not Responses API, `model` always required for Anthropic, streaming via SSE)
- ✅ All 10 functional requirements from PRD are covered with acceptance criteria
- ✅ 10 edge cases thoroughly specified including NotebookLM failover
- ✅ Research recommendations followed (abstraction layer, async throughout, fallback validation provider)
- ✅ Existing prototype components verified to exist at `prototypes-magic/src/components/ui/`
- ✅ All environment variables match research (`ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `NOTEBOOKLM_AUTH_JSON`)
- ✅ Implementation phases logically ordered with clear milestones

entries.detail:
1	# Specification: Verbum — Catholic Content Management Platform
2	
3	## Overview
4	
5	Verbum is a greenfield, single-user Catholic content management platform for Ramir, a Portuguese-speaking Catholic content creator. The platform centralizes the full content lifecycle — from AI-assisted generation (via Anthropic Claude and OpenAI ChatGPT) through theological validation (via NotebookLM), editorial scheduling, and multi-channel delivery. It supports three content types: short-form video scripts (1 min), long-form video scripts (10 min), and SEO-optimized blog posts in Portuguese. The goal is to reduce time-to-publish by 50%, ensure 100% doctrinal accuracy through automated validation, and maintain a consistent publishing cadence of at least 3 pieces per week. The entire system is designed for a single user — no multi-tenancy, no scaling concerns, no monetization.
6	
7	## Workflow Type
8	
9	**Type**: feature
10	
11	**Rationale**: This is a brand-new full-stack application being built from scratch within an empty repository. There is no existing application logic to refactor or migrate — only a Vite/React/Tailwind prototype shell (`prototypes-magic/`) with reusable UI components. Every layer (database schema, backend API, service integrations, frontend pages) must be designed and implemented.
12	
13	## Task Scope
14	
15	### Services Involved
16	- **verbum-backend** (primary) — Python FastAPI backend handling API routing, AI integration orchestration, NotebookLM validation, database persistence, and scheduling logic
17	- **verbum-frontend** (primary) — React TypeScript SPA providing the dashboard, content editor, validation panel, editorial calendar, and SEO tools
18	- **SQLite database** (infrastructure) — Embedded file-based database for content items, validation records, scheduling metadata, and version history
19	
20	### This Task Will:
21	- [ ] Set up the full project structure (backend + frontend monorepo)
22	- [ ] Implement the Python FastAPI backend with async architecture
23	- [ ] Design and create the SQLite database schema via SQLModel
24	- [ ] Integrate Anthropic Claude API for AI content generation with streaming
25	- [ ] Integrate OpenAI ChatGPT API as an alternate drafting engine with streaming
26	- [ ] Integrate NotebookLM (via `notebooklm-py`) for theological validation with citation retrieval
27	- [ ] Build a validation abstraction layer to enable graceful degradation and provider swapping
28	- [ ] Create the React frontend with dashboard, content editor, validation panel, and calendar views
29	- [ ] Implement the full content lifecycle: draft → validated → scheduled → published
30	- [ ] Add Portuguese SEO metadata tools for blog post optimization
31	- [ ] Implement content versioning and validation history tracking
32	- [ ] Add iCal export for editorial calendar reminders
33	- [ ] Implement environment-based configuration with secure API key management
34	
35	### Out of Scope:
36	- Custom Catholic knowledge base or RAG pipeline (NotebookLM handles this)
37	- Social media analytics or community management
38	- Monetization or revenue features
39	- Multi-user authentication or multi-tenancy
40	- Direct Google Calendar API integration (using .ics export instead)
41	- Bulk social media publishing automation
42	- Mobile native app (responsive web only)
43	- Docker containerization (single-user local/hosted deployment)
44	
45	## Service Context
46	
47	### verbum-backend
48	
49	**Tech Stack:**
50	- Language: Python 3.11+
51	- Framework: FastAPI >= 0.135.0 (with `[standard]` extras for uvicorn)
52	- ORM: SQLModel >= 0.0.37 (Pydantic + SQLAlchemy combined)
53	- Database: SQLite (via aiosqlite for async operations)
54	- AI SDKs: `anthropic >= 0.88.0`, `openai >= 2.30.0`
55	- Validation: `notebooklm-py[browser] >= 0.3.4` (UNOFFICIAL — HIGH RISK)
56	- Calendar: `icalendar` for .ics export
57	- Config: `python-dotenv` for environment variable management
58	- Key directories: `backend/app/`, `backend/app/api/`, `backend/app/models/`, `backend/app/services/`, `backend/app/core/`
59	
60	**Entry Point:** `backend/app/main.py`
61	
62	**How to Run:**
63	```bash
64	cd backend
65	pip install -r requirements.txt
66	uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8000
67	```
68	
69	**Port:** 8000
70	
71	---
72	
73	### verbum-frontend
74	
75	**Tech Stack:**
76	- Language: TypeScript 5.5+
77	- Framework: React 18.3
78	- Bundler: Vite 5.2
79	- Styling: Tailwind CSS 3.4
80	- Icons: lucide-react 0.522
81	- UI Components: Reuse from `prototypes-magic/src/components/ui/` (Button, Input, Select, Card, Tabs, Badge, Table, Textarea, Progress, etc.)
82	- Charts: recharts (already in prototype dependencies)

2	  "task_description": "# Catholic Content Management Platform – PRD

### TL;DR

A personal platform for Ramir to manage the full lifecycle of Catholic content — from AI-assisted generation (Claude, ChatGPT) to theological validation via NotebookLM, editorial scheduling, and multi-channel delivery. It specifically supports short-form video scripts (1-min), long-form video scripts (10-min), and SEO-optimized blog posts in Portuguese, ensuring both speed and doctrinal accuracy for a Catholic content creator.

---

## Goals

### Business Goals

* Centralize all Catholic content production activities in a single platform.
* Reduce average time-to-publish for each piece of content by at least 50%.
* Increase consistency in publishing, achieving a minimum of 3 content pieces per week.
* Ensure 100% of published content is validated for theological accuracy.
* Minimize post-publication corrections by implementing robust doctrinal review.

### User Goals

* Rapidly generate content using AI while maintaining a uniquely Catholic voice.
* Validate content against authoritative Church sources (without building custom RAG pipelines).
* Organize content into an accessible editorial calendar with clear status tracking.
* Schedule delivery for each piece on appropriate channels (e.g., YouTube, Blog).
* Easily curate, update, and archive content with full version and validation history.

### Non-Goals

* Building or maintaining a custom Catholic knowledge base (handled entirely by NotebookLM).
* Undertaking broad social media management (e.g., analytics, community interaction) beyond basic scheduling/delivery reminders.
* Implementing monetization or revenue-generating features.

---

## User Stories

**Persona:** Ramir, Catholic Content Creator

* As Ramir, I want to create a new short-form video script using Claude or ChatGPT, so that I can generate engaging 1-min video content quickly.
* As Ramir, I want to validate my script against the full Catholic corpus available in NotebookLM, so that I am confident my content aligns with Church teaching.
* As Ramir, I want to schedule my validated content on an editorial calendar, so that I maintain a consistent publishing cadence across channels.
* As Ramir, I want to curate and edit blog posts for SEO (in Portuguese), so that my written content reaches a larger audience via search engines.
* As Ramir, I want to review the current status (draft, validated, scheduled, published) and destination (YouTube, Blog, etc.) of all my content, so that I’m always aware of what’s next.

---

## Functional Requirements

* **Content Creation (Priority: High)**

  * AI Drafting Integration: Connect to Claude and ChatGPT APIs for rapid content generation.
  * Content Type Templates: Pre-configured templates for short-form video, long-form video, and blog posts.

* **Theological Validation (Priority: High)**

  * NotebookLM Integration: Use a third-party library for doctrinal source validation.
  * Validation Score/Flag System: Visual feedback indicating theological alignment.
  * Source Citation Display: Display relevant citations from the Catholic corpus.

* **Editorial Calendar (Priority: Medium)**

  * Scheduling by Content Type and Channel: Assign publication dates and platforms.
  * Status Tracking: Transparently manage content states (draft, validated, scheduled, published).

* **SEO & Curation Tools (Priority: Medium)**

  * Portuguese SEO Metadata: Fields and checks for keywords, meta, and descriptions in Portuguese.
  * Readability Checks: Automated assessment of text clarity.
  * Keyword Suggestions: AI-powered prompts for blog post optimization.

* **Channel Management (Priority: Low)**

  * Channel Tagging: Associate content with YouTube, Blog, or other platforms.
  * Delivery Reminders: Notify when content is due to be published or reviewed.

---

## User Experience

**Entry Point & First-Time User Experience**

* User logs in and lands on a dashboard summarizing the content pipeline, showing draft/validated/scheduled/published items, and the editorial calendar.
* Optional onboarding walkthrough highlights how to create content, trigger validation, and use the editor.

**Core Experience**

* **Step 1:** Create a new content item.
  * Choose content type: short video, long video, or blog post.
  * Select language preference (default: Portuguese for blog).
  * Clear, minimal form – no unnecessary friction.
* **Step 2:** AI generation panel.
  * Choose AI model: Claude or ChatGPT.
  * Enter topic/prompt and generate draft.
  * Validate prompt entry; handle API errors gracefully.
  * Show generated draft in main editor.
* **Step 3:** Trigger NotebookLM validation.
  * One-click validation sends content to third-party NotebookLM library.
  * Return and display validation score/flags and doctrinal citations.
  * Validation panel anchored on the right side of the editor.
* **Step 4:** Content curation and editing.
  * Edit draft based on validation results.
  * Option to view citation sources directly.
  * Save drafts and track validation history.
* **Step 5:** Assign to channel and schedule.
  * Tag content to delivery platform.
  * Choose publication date via editorial calendar UI.
  * Status updates to “Scheduled.”
* **Step 6:** Mark as published and archive.
  * Mark content as published after delivery.
  * Optionally archive for future reference and analytics.

**Advanced Features & Edge Cases**

* Bulk scheduling: Assign dates and channels for multiple items at once.
* Validation history: View prior validation runs and responses.
* Content versioning: Track major edits and revert if necessary.
* Handle validation or AI API downtime gracefully (clear errors, retry options).

**UI/UX Highlights**

* Single-column editor central to workflow; validation feedback panel fixed to the right.
* Editorial calendar as a visual week/month view; click-in to schedule or review items.
* Portuguese-first interface and localization for SEO/blog workflows.
* Consistent, accessible color palette and responsive design for all device types.

---

## Narrative

Ramir sits down at his desk on a Tuesday morning with inspiration to create a 1-minute video script about the upcoming Feast of Corpus Christi. He opens his Catholic Content Management Platform and immediately sees his dashboard, which highlights content drafts and this week’s publishing schedule. Selecting the “short video” template, he enters a brief prompt for Claude, who quickly generates a concise, engaging script in less than a minute.

Before moving forward, Ramir clicks the “Validate with NotebookLM” button. Within seconds, the platform provides a score indicating strong doctrinal support but flags two statements for clarification. The tool seamlessly surfaces source citations from the Catechism and Vatican II documents, making it easy for Ramir to reference and refine his wording to ensure precise Catholic teaching.

Satisfied with the script, he assigns it to his short-video channel and schedules it for Thursday’s publication. Motivated by the smooth workflow, he turns to the blog post section—writes, validates, optimizes SEO in Portuguese, and schedules another article, all in one productive sitting. Ramir knows that every piece he publishes aligns with Church authority, publishes on time, and is organized for maximum reach—giving him both confidence and creative momentum.

---

## Success Metrics

### User-Centric Metrics

* Average time (in minutes) from content idea to scheduled item (tracked via timestamps).
* Number of validation cycles per content piece before approval.
* Number of content pieces published per week.
* User feedback on validation accuracy and workflow satisfaction.

### Business Metrics

* Consistency of weekly publishing cadence (minimum 3 items/week).
* Reduction in after-publication corrections or removals due to doctrinal issues.
* Adoption/continued use rate (e.g., items managed per week/month).

### Technical Metrics

* Average response time from NotebookLM API (<2 seconds per call).
* Average AI generation latency (<5 seconds per request).
* Content editor uptime (>99% during weekday business hours).
* Robust error-rate tracking on API integrations (<2% failure rate).

### Tracking Plan

* Content item created (event)
* AI generation triggered (event, with selected model)
* Validation initiated/completed (event, with score/result)
* Content scheduled (event, with channel/date)
* Marked as published (event)
* User edits/re-validation cycles (event)

---

## Technical Considerations

### Technical Needs

* Web application frontend with content editor, dashboard, and calendar views.
* API integrations for Anthropic (Claude), OpenAI (ChatGPT), and third-party NotebookLM library.
* Lightweight backend for routing requests, managing validation/scheduling logic, and storing user data.
* Database for persistent storage of content items, validation responses, scheduling metadata, and channel assignments.

### Integration Points

* Anthropic Claude API for content drafting.
* OpenAI ChatGPT API for alternate drafting option.
* NotebookLM third-party library for source validation and citation retrieval.
* Option for editorial calendar export (iCal or Google Calendar integration for reminders).

### Data Storage & Privacy

* All data (drafts, validation results, scheduling info) stored privately and securely for Ramir only.
* Ensure encryption at rest and in transit.
* No personal data shared externally or sold.
* Compliance with GDPR and local privacy standards as relevant.

### Scalability & Performance

* Designed for single-user, low daily volume (tens of content items per week).
* Minimal scaling risk.
* Prioritize fast response for workflow efficiency.

### Potential Challenges

* Reliability and rate limits of NotebookLM’s third-party library/API.
* Maintaining consistent, high-quality Portuguese output from AI models (especially for SEO).
* Managing failing API calls

* Designed for single-user, low daily volume (tens of content items per week).
* Minimal scaling risk.
* Prioritize fast response for workflow efficiency.

### Potential Challenges

* Reliability and rate limits of NotebookLM’s third-party library/API.
* Maintaining consistent, high-quality Portuguese output from AI models (especially for SEO).
* Managing failing API calls or downtime from AI or validation services.\

entries.detail:
200 | `prototypes-magic/src/components/ui/progress/Progress.tsx` | Progress bar — reuse for validation score display | 201 | `prototypes-magic/tailwind.config.js` | Tailwind theme configuration — extend for Verbum's color palette | 202 | `prototypes-magic/vite.config.ts` | Vite configuration — use as base, add API proxy | 203 | `prototypes-magic/package.json` | Dependency baseline — reuse core deps, add routing + calendar libs | 204 205 ## Patterns to Follow 206 207 ### Pattern 1: Async FastAPI with SQLModel 208 209 Industry best practice for Python async web applications: 210 211 ```python 212 # backend/app/main.py 213 from contextlib import asynccontextmanager 214 from fastapi import FastAPI 215 from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware 216 from app.core.database import init_db 217 from app.api import content, generation, validation, schedule, seo, dashboard 218 219 @asynccontextmanager 220 async def lifespan(app: FastAPI): 221 await init_db() 222 yield 223 224 app = FastAPI(title="Verbum API", lifespan=lifespan) 225 app.add_middleware( 226 CORSMiddleware, 227 allow_origins=["http://localhost:5173"], 228 allow_credentials=True, 229 allow_methods=["*"], 230 allow_headers=["*"], 231 ) 232 app.include_router(content.router, prefix="/api/content", tags=["content"]) 233 app.include_router(generation.router, prefix="/api/generation", tags=["generation"]) 234 app.include_router(validation.router, prefix="/api/validation", tags=["validation"]) 235 app.include_router(schedule.router, prefix="/api/schedule", tags=["schedule"]) 236 app.include_router(seo.router, prefix="/api/seo", tags=["seo"]) 237 app.include_router(dashboard.router, prefix="/api/dashboard", tags=["dashboard"]) 238 ``` 239 240 **Key Points:** 241 - Use `lifespan` context manager for database initialization (not deprecated `@app.on_event`) 242 - Configure CORS for frontend-backend communication 243 - Organize endpoints into routers with prefixes and tags 244 - All handlers should be `async def` for non-blocking I/O 245 246 ### Pattern 2: SQLModel Data Models with Status Enum 247 248 ```python 249 # backend/app/models/content.py 250 from enum import Enum 251 from datetime import datetime, timezone 252 from typing import Optional 253 from sqlmodel import SQLModel, Field 254 from uuid import uuid4 255 256 class ContentType(str, Enum): 257 SHORT_VIDEO = "short_video" 258 LONG_VIDEO = "long_video" 259 BLOG_POST = "blog_post" 260 261 class ContentStatus(str, Enum): 262 DRAFT = "draft" 263 VALIDATED = "validated" 264 SCHEDULED = "scheduled" 265 PUBLISHED = "published" 266 267 class ContentItem(SQLModel, table=True): 268 __tablename__ = "content_items" 269 270 id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid4()), primary_key=True) 271 title: str 272 body: str = "" 273 content_type: ContentType 274 status: ContentStatus = ContentStatus.DRAFT 275 language: str = "pt" 276 channels: str = "[]" # JSON list of channel strings, e.g. '["youtube", "blog"]' 277 ai_model_used: Optional[str] = None 278 scheduled_date: Optional[datetime] = None 279 created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc)) 280 updated_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc)) 281 version: int = 1 282 ``` 283 284 **Key Points:** 285 - Use string enums for readable database values 286 - UUID primary keys for content items 287 - Default to Portuguese language (`pt`) 288 - Track which AI model generated the draft 289 - Version counter for content versioning 290 291 ### Pattern 3: AI Service Abstraction with Streaming 292 293 ```python 294 # backend/app/services/ai_service.py 295 from typing import Protocol, AsyncIterator 296 297 class AIProvider(Protocol): 298 async def generate(self, prompt: str, system_prompt: str, max_tokens: int) -> str: ... 299 async def generate_stream(self, prompt: str, system_prompt: str, max_tokens: int) -> AsyncIterator[str]: ... 300 301 class AIService: 302 def __init__(self, providers: dict[str, AIProvider]): 303 self._providers = providers 304 305 async def generate(self, model_key: str, prompt: str, system_prompt: str, max_tokens: int = 2048) -> str: 306 provider = self._providers[model_key] 307 return await provider.generate(prompt, system_prompt, max_tokens) 308 309 async def generate_stream(self, model_key: str, prompt: str, system_prompt: str, max_tokens: int = 2048) -> AsyncIterator[str]: 310 provider = self._providers[model_key] 311 async for chunk in provider.generate_stream(prompt, system_prompt, max_tokens): 312 yield chunk 313 ``` 314 315 **Key Points:** 316 - Use Python `Protocol` for interface definition (structural typing) 317 - Support both full-response and streaming generation 318 - Provider pattern allows easy addition of new AI models 319 - The `model_key` selects between Claude and ChatGPT 320 321 ### Pattern 4: Validation Service with Fallback 322 323 ```python 324 # backend/app/services/validation_service.py 325 from typing import Protocol 326 from dataclasses import dataclass 327 328 @dataclass 329 class Citation: 330 citation_number: int 331 source_id: str 332 cited_text: str 333 334 @dataclass 335 class ValidationResult: 336 score: float # 0.0 to 1.0 337 flags: list[str] 338 citations: list[Citation] 339 provider: str # "notebooklm" or "ai_fallback" 340 raw_answer: str 341 342 class ValidationProvider(Protocol): 343 async def validate(self, content: str, content_type: str) -> ValidationResult: ... 344 async def is_available(self) -> bool: ... 345 346 class ValidationService: 347 def __init__(self, primary: ValidationProvider, fallback: ValidationProvider): 348 self._primary = primary 349 self._fallback = fallback 350 351 async def validate(self, content: str, content_type: str) -> ValidationResult: 352 if await self._primary.is_available(): 353 try: 354 return await self._primary.validate(content, content_type) 355 except Exception: 356 pass 357 return await self._fallback.validate(content, content_type) 358 ``` 359 360 **Key Points:** 361 - Abstraction layer critical for NotebookLM reliability concerns 362 - `is_available()` check before attempting validation 363 - Automatic fallback to AI-based validation if NotebookLM is down 364 - Structured `ValidationResult` with score, flags, and traceable citations 365 - The `provider` field tells the UI which backend produced the result 366 367 ### Pattern 5: SSE Streaming from FastAPI to React 368 369 ```python 370 # backend/app/api/generation.py 371 from fastapi import APIRouter 372 from fastapi.responses import StreamingResponse 373 374 router = APIRouter() 375 376 @router.post("/stream") 377 async def generate_stream(request: GenerationRequest): 378 async def event_generator(): 379 async for chunk in ai_service.generate_stream( 380 model_key=request.model, 381 prompt=request.prompt, 382 system_prompt=request.system_prompt, 383 ): 384 yield f"data: {json.dumps({'text': chunk})}\n\n" 385 yield f"data: {json.dumps({'done': True})}\n\n" 386 387 return StreamingResponse(event_generator(), media_type="text/event-stream") 388 ``` 389 390 ```typescript 391 // frontend/src/services/streaming.ts 392 export async function* streamGeneration(request: GenerationRequest): AsyncGenerator { 393 const response = await fetch('/api/generation/stream', { 394 method: 'POST', 395 headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }, 396 body: JSON.stringify(request), 397 }); 398 const reader = response.body!.getReader(); 399 const decoder = new TextDecoder(); 400 while (true) { 401 const { done, value } = await reader.read(); 402 if (done) break; 403 const text = decoder.decode(value); 404 for (const line of text.split('\n')) { 405 if (line.startsWith('data: ')) { 406 const data = JSON.parse(line.slice(6)); 407 if (data.done) return; 408 yield data.text; 409 } 410 } 411 } 412 } 413 ``` 414 415 **Key Points:** 416 - FastAPI `StreamingResponse` with SSE format (`data: ...\n\n`) 417 - React consumes via `ReadableStream` reader 418 - JSON-encoded chunks allow metadata alongside text 419 - Terminal `done` event signals completion 420 - Dramatically improves perceived performance for content generation 421 422 ### Pattern 6: React Page with Editor + Right Panel Layout 423 424 ```tsx 425 // frontend/src/pages/EditorPage.tsx 426 export function EditorPage() { 427 return ( 428
429 {/* Main Editor - Single Column */} 430
431 432 433 434
435 {/* Validation Panel - Fixed Right */} 436
437 438
439
440 ); 441 } 442 ``` 443 444 **Key Points:** 445 - Flexbox layout with fixed-width right panel (per UX requirements) 446 - Editor takes remaining space (`flex-1`) 447 - Validation panel is always visible alongside editor 448 - Both panels independently scrollable 449 450 ## Requirements 451 452 ### Functional Requirements 453 454 1. **AI Content Generation (High Priority)** 455 - Description: Connect to Anthropic Claude and OpenAI ChatGPT APIs to generate content drafts from user prompts. Support three content type templates: short-form video (1 min), long-form video (10 min), and SEO blog post. Stream AI responses in real-time to the editor. 456 - Acceptance: User can select an AI model (Claude or ChatGPT), enter a prompt, and see the generated draft stream into the editor in under 5 seconds. Content type template structures the system prompt appropriately. 457 458 2. **Content Type Templates (High Priority)** 459 - Description: Pre-configured templates that structure AI prompts ... 598	python3 --version  # must be >= 3.11
599	
600	# Node.js 18+ and npm
601	node --version
602	npm --version
603	
604	# Playwright + Chromium (only for initial NotebookLM auth setup)
605	# NOT required in production if using NOTEBOOKLM_AUTH_JSON
606	```
607	
608	### Install Dependencies
609	
610	```bash
611	# Backend
612	cd backend
613	python3 -m venv venv
614	source venv/bin/activate
615	pip install -r requirements.txt
616	
617	# Frontend
618	cd frontend
619	npm install
620	
621	# NotebookLM auth setup (one-time)
622	pip install 'notebooklm-py[browser]'
623	playwright install chromium
624	notebooklm login  # Opens browser for Google SSO
625	```
626	
627	### Start Services
628	
629	```bash
630	# Terminal 1: Backend
631	cd backend
632	source venv/bin/activate
633	uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8000
634	
635	# Terminal 2: Frontend
636	cd frontend
637	npm run dev
638	```
639	
640	### Service URLs
641	- Frontend: http://localhost:5173
642	- Backend API: http://localhost:8000
643	- API Documentation (Swagger): http://localhost:8000/docs
644	- API Documentation (ReDoc): http://localhost:8000/redoc
645	
646	### Required Environment Variables
647	
648	Create `backend/.env`:
649	
650	```bash
651	# Required
652	ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...          # Anthropic Claude API key
653	OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...                 # OpenAI API key
654	
655	# NotebookLM (one of these)
656	NOTEBOOKLM_AUTH_JSON={"..."}          # Inline auth for production (preferred)
657	# OR rely on ~/.notebooklm/auth.json from 'notebooklm login'
658	
659	# Optional
660	DATABASE_URL=sqlite+aiosqlite:///./verbum.db   # Default if not set
661	CLAUDE_MODEL=claude-sonnet-4-5-20250514            # Default Claude model
662	OPENAI_MODEL=gpt-4o-mini                        # Default OpenAI model
663	NOTEBOOKLM_NOTEBOOK_ID=                          # Pre-configured notebook with Catholic sources
664	LOG_LEVEL=INFO
665	```
666	
667	## API Endpoints
668	
669	### Content API (`/api/content`)
670	| Method | Path | Description |
671	|--------|------|-------------|
672	| `GET` | `/` | List all content items (with filtering: status, type, channel) |
673	| `POST` | `/` | Create a new content item |
674	| `GET` | `/{id}` | Get a single content item |
675	| `PUT` | `/{id}` | Update a content item (body, title, status, channel) |
676	| `DELETE` | `/{id}` | Delete a content item |
677	| `GET` | `/{id}/versions` | Get version history for a content item |
678	| `GET` | `/{id}/versions/{version}` | Get a specific version snapshot |
679	| `POST` | `/{id}/status` | Transition content status (with validation) |
680	
681	### Generation API (`/api/generation`)
682	| Method | Path | Description |
683	|--------|------|-------------|
684	| `POST` | `/generate` | Generate content (full response) |
685	| `POST` | `/stream` | Generate content with SSE streaming |
686	| `GET` | `/models` | List available AI models and their status |
687	
688	### Validation API (`/api/validation`)
689	| Method | Path | Description |
690	|--------|------|-------------|
691	| `POST` | `/validate/{content_id}` | Trigger validation for a content item |
692	| `GET` | `/history/{content_id}` | Get validation history for a content item |
693	| `GET` | `/status` | Check validation service availability |
694	
695	### Schedule API (`/api/schedule`)
696	| Method | Path | Description |
697	|--------|------|-------------|
698	| `GET` | `/calendar` | Get scheduled items for a date range |
699	| `POST` | `/{content_id}/schedule` | Schedule a content item |
700	| `PUT` | `/{content_id}/reschedule` | Reschedule a content item |
701	| `GET` | `/export/ical` | Export calendar as .ics file |
702	
703	### Dashboard API (`/api/dashboard`)
704	| Method | Path | Description |
705	|--------|------|-------------|
706	| `GET` | `/summary` | Pipeline summary (counts per status, recent items) |
707	| `GET` | `/upcoming` | Next 7 days of scheduled content |
708	
709	### SEO API (`/api/seo`)
710	| Method | Path | Description |
711	|--------|------|-------------|
712	| `GET` | `/{content_id}` | Get SEO metadata for a content item |
713	| `PUT` | `/{content_id}` | Update SEO metadata |
714	| `POST` | `/{content_id}/analyze` | Run readability analysis |
715	| `POST` | `/{content_id}/keywords` | Generate keyword suggestions via AI |
716	
717	## Implementation Phases
718	
719	### Phase 1: Foundation (Backend + Frontend Skeleton)
720	1. Set up project structure (backend and frontend directories)
721	2. Create FastAPI app with CORS, lifespan, and router structure
722	3. Define SQLModel data models and database initialization
723	4. Create React app with routing, Tailwind, and UI component library
724	5. Implement content CRUD (backend API + frontend list/create pages)
725	6. **Milestone**: Can create, list, edit, and delete content items
726	
727	### Phase 2: AI Generation Integration
728	1. Implement Claude provider with streaming
729	2. Implement OpenAI provider with streaming
730	3. Create AI service abstraction layer
731	4. Build generation API endpoints (full + streaming)
732	5. Build frontend GenerationPanel with model selector and streaming display
733	6. Implement content type templates (system prompts per type)
734	7. **Milestone**: Can generate content via Claude or ChatGPT with real-time streaming
735	
736	### Phase 3: Theological Validation
737	1. Implement NotebookLM provider with error handling and retry
738	2. Implement AI-based fallback validation provider
739	3. Create validation service with automatic failover
740	4. Build validation API endpoints
741	5. Build frontend ValidationPanel with score, flags, citations
742	6. Implement validation history tracking
743	7. **Milestone**: Can validate content with citation display and fallback
744	
745	### Phase 4: Calendar, SEO, and Polish
746	1. Build editorial calendar UI (week/month views)
747	2. Implement scheduling API and status transitions
748	3. Add iCal export
749	4. Build SEO metadata panel for blog posts
750	5. Implement content versioning
751	6. Build dashboard with pipeline summary
752	7. Portuguese localization of interface elements
753	8. **Milestone**: Full content lifecycle operational
754	
755	## Success Criteria
756	
757	The task is complete when:
758	
759	1. [ ] Project structure is set up with backend (FastAPI/Python) and frontend (React/TypeScript) directories
760	2. [ ] SQLite database is created with all required tables (content_items, content_versions, validation_records, seo_metadata)
761	3. [ ] Content CRUD operations work end-to-end (create, read, update, delete, list with filtering)
762	4. [ ] AI generation works with both Claude and ChatGPT, including real-time streaming to the editor
763	5. [ ] Content type templates (short video, long video, blog post) produce appropriately structured output
764	6. [ ] Theological validation via NotebookLM returns score, flags, and traceable citations
765	7. [ ] Validation fallback to AI-based provider works when NotebookLM is unavailable
766	8. [ ] Content status lifecycle (draft → validated → scheduled → published) is enforced and tracked
767	9. [ ] Editorial calendar displays scheduled items in week and month views
768	10. [ ] Portuguese SEO metadata tools work for blog posts (meta fields, readability, keyword suggestions)
769	11. [ ] Content versioning tracks edit history with version snapshots
770	12. [ ] iCal export produces valid .ics files
771	13. [ ] Dashboard shows pipeline summary with status counts and upcoming items
772	14. [ ] All API keys are managed via environment variables — none exposed in frontend
773	15. [ ] Frontend is Portuguese-first with consistent, responsive design
774	16. [ ] No console errors in normal operation
775	17. [ ] All existing tests pass, new tests cover core functionality
776	
777	## QA Acceptance Criteria
778	
779	**CRITICAL**: These criteria must be verified by the QA Agent before sign-off.
780	
781	### Unit Tests
782	
783	| Test | File | What to Verify |
784	|------|------|----------------|
785	| Content CRUD | `backend/tests/test_content.py` | Create, read, update, delete content items; validate field defaults; enforce required fields |
786	| Status Transitions | `backend/tests/test_content.py` | Valid transitions (draft→validated→scheduled→published); reject invalid transitions |
787	| Claude Provider | `backend/tests/test_generation.py` | Mock Anthropic client; verify correct model/params; handle APIError, RateLimitError |
788	| OpenAI Provider | `backend/tests/test_generation.py` | Mock OpenAI client; verify Chat Completions format; handle errors |
789	| Validation Service | `backend/tests/test_validation.py` | Primary→fallback failover; score/flag/citation parsing; unavailability detection |
790	| NotebookLM Provider | `backend/tests/test_validation.py` | Mock notebooklm client; parse AskResult with citations; handle RPCError |
791	| Schedule Service | `backend/tests/test_schedule.py` | Schedule/reschedule items; iCal export format; date range queries |
792	| SEO Service | `backend/tests/test_seo.py` | Readability score calculation; keyword suggestion format; metadata validation |
793	| Content Versioning | `backend/tests/test_content.py` | Version increment on save; snapshot creation; version retrieval |
794	
795	### Integration Tests
796	
797	| Test | Services | What to Verify |
798	|------|----------|----------------|
799	| Content → Generation | backend API + AI service | POST to /api/generation/generate returns valid content; POST to /api/generation/stream returns SSE events |
800	| Content → Validation | backend API + validation service | POST to /api/validation/validate/{id} returns ValidationResult; GET /api/validation/history/{id} returns history |
801	| Content → Schedule | backend API + schedule service | POST schedule updates content status; GET /api/schedule/calendar returns items in date range |
802	| Validation Failover | validation service + providers | When primary provider raises, fallback is used; response includes `provider` field indicating fallback |
803	| Dashboard Aggregation | backend API + database | GET /api/dashboard/summary returns correct counts per status |
804	
805	### End-to-End Tests
806	
807	| Flow | Steps | Expected Outcome |
808	|------|-------|------------------|

entries.content:
Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase. Let me document my findings and create all the planning artifacts. Let me start by creating the updated context files and the implementation plan.

## Phase 0 Findings Summary

**Existing codebase:**
- Root project: "musicalizer-app" — Next.js 14 + Prisma + PostgreSQL (music production tool)
- `prototypes-magic/`: Vite/React/Tailwind prototype with reusable UI components (CVA pattern, cn utility, dark mode, CSS variable theming)
- `src/components/ui/`: Already has copies of prototype UI components (for the Next.js app)
- Python 3.12.3 available; Node.js needs nvm activation
- No `backend/` or `frontend/` directories exist yet — Verbum is greenfield

**Key patterns found:**
- UI components: `ComponentName.tsx` + `index.tsx` re-export pattern, CVA for variants, `cn()` from clsx+twMerge
- Tailwind theme: CSS custom properties (`var(--primary)`, etc.), dark mode via `selector`
- Prototype deps: React 18.3, lucide-react 0.522, class-variance-authority, recharts, sonner

Now let me create all the planning artifacts:

All planning artifacts are validated and complete. Let me provide the final summary.

---

## Planning Session Complete — Verbum Implementation Plan

### What was created:

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `implementation_plan.json` | Complete 8-phase, 30-subtask implementation plan |
| `project_index.json` | Updated with Verbum backend/frontend service definitions |
| `context.json` | Updated with files to create/reference, patterns, and constraints |
| `init.sh` | Development environment startup script (backend + frontend) |
| `build-progress.txt` | Progress tracking document |

### Plan Structure (8 Phases, 30 Subtasks):

| Phase | Name | Subtasks | Depends On | Parallel-Safe |
|-------|------|----------|------------|---------------|
| 1 | **Backend Foundation** | 6 | — | ✅ |
| 2 | **Frontend Foundation** | 4 | — | ✅ |
| 3 | **AI Generation Backend** | 3 | Phase 1 | ✅ |
| 4 | **Validation Backend** | 3 | Phase 1 | ✅ |
| 5 | **Editor Frontend Integration** | 4 | Phases 2,3,4 | ❌ |
| 6 | **Calendar/SEO/Dashboard Backend** | 3 | Phase 1 | ✅ |
| 7 | **Calendar/SEO/Dashboard Frontend** | 3 | Phases 2,5,6 | ❌ |
| 8 | **Testing & Polish** | 4 | Phases 5,6,7 | ❌ |

### Parallel Groups:
- **Group A**: Phase 1 ‖ Phase 2 (backend vs frontend, zero overlap)
- **Group B**: Phase 3 ‖ Phase 4 ‖ Phase 6 (all backend, different service files)

### Key Decisions:
- **~71 files** to create across `backend/` and `frontend/` directories
- **Risk level: High** — 3 external API integrations, API key security, NotebookLM fragility
- **Recommended workers: 2** for ~1.3x speedup
- Tests are **post-implementation** (Phase 8)
- UI components **copied** from `prototypes-magic/` (not symlinked)
- Existing musicalizer Next.js app is **untouched**

### To start building:
```bash
source auto-claude/.venv/bin/activate && python auto-claude/run.py --spec 002 --parallel 2
```
Let me read the spec and plan first, then implement.

entries.detail:
1	# Specification: Verbum — Catholic Content Management Platform
2	
3	## Overview
4	
5	Verbum is a greenfield, single-user Catholic content management platform for Ramir, a Portuguese-speaking Catholic content creator. The platform centralizes the full content lifecycle — from AI-assisted generation (via Anthropic Claude and OpenAI ChatGPT) through theological validation (via NotebookLM), editorial scheduling, and multi-channel delivery. It supports three content types: short-form video scripts (1 min), long-form video scripts (10 min), and SEO-optimized blog posts in Portuguese. The goal is to reduce time-to-publish by 50%, ensure 100% doctrinal accuracy through automated validation, and maintain a consistent publishing cadence of at least 3 pieces per week. The entire system is designed for a single user — no multi-tenancy, no scaling concerns, no monetization.
6	
7	## Workflow Type
8	
9	**Type**: feature
10	
11	**Rationale**: This is a brand-new full-stack application being built from scratch within an empty repository. There is no existing application logic to refactor or migrate — only a Vite/React/Tailwind prototype shell (`prototypes-magic/`) with reusable UI components. Every layer (database schema, backend API, service integrations, frontend pages) must be designed and implemented.
12	
13	## Task Scope
14	
15	### Services Involved
16	- **verbum-backend** (primary) — Python FastAPI backend handling API routing, AI integration orchestration, NotebookLM validation, database persistence, and scheduling logic
17	- **verbum-frontend** (primary) — React TypeScript SPA providing the dashboard, content editor, validation panel, editorial calendar, and SEO tools
18	- **SQLite database** (infrastructure) — Embedded file-based database for content items, validation records, scheduling metadata, and version history
19	
20	### This Task Will:
21	- [ ] Set up the full project structure (backend + frontend monorepo)
22	- [ ] Implement the Python FastAPI backend with async architecture
23	- [ ] Design and create the SQLite database schema via SQLModel
24	- [ ] Integrate Anthropic Claude API for AI content generation with streaming
25	- [ ] Integrate OpenAI ChatGPT API as an alternate drafting engine with streaming
26	- [ ] Integrate NotebookLM (via `notebooklm-py`) for theological validation with citation retrieval
27	- [ ] Build a validation abstraction layer to enable graceful degradation and provider swapping
28	- [ ] Create the React frontend with dashboard, content editor, validation panel, and calendar views
29	- [ ] Implement the full content lifecycle: draft → validated → scheduled → published
30	- [ ] Add Portuguese SEO metadata tools for blog post optimization
31	- [ ] Implement content versioning and validation history tracking
32	- [ ] Add iCal export for editorial calendar reminders
33	- [ ] Implement environment-based configuration with secure API key management
34	
35	### Out of Scope:
36	- Custom Catholic knowledge base or RAG pipeline (NotebookLM handles this)
37	- Social media analytics or community management
38	- Monetization or revenue features
39	- Multi-user authentication or multi-tenancy
40	- Direct Google Calendar API integration (using .ics export instead)
41	- Bulk social media publishing automation
42	- Mobile native app (responsive web only)
43	- Docker containerization (single-user local/hosted deployment)
44	
45	## Service Context
46	
47	### verbum-backend
48	
49	**Tech Stack:**
50	- Language: Python 3.11+

entries.detail:
48	
49	**Tech Stack:**
50	- Language: Python 3.11+
51	- Framework: FastAPI >= 0.135.0 (with `[standard]` extras for uvicorn)
52	- ORM: SQLModel >= 0.0.37 (Pydantic + SQLAlchemy combined)
53	- Database: SQLite (via aiosqlite for async operations)
54	- AI SDKs: `anthropic >= 0.88.0`, `openai >= 2.30.0`
55	- Validation: `notebooklm-py[browser] >= 0.3.4` (UNOFFICIAL — HIGH RISK)
56	- Calendar: `icalendar` for .ics export
57	- Config: `python-dotenv` for environment variable management
58	- Key directories: `backend/app/`, `backend/app/api/`, `backend/app/models/`, `backend/app/services/`, `backend/app/core/`
59	
60	**Entry Point:** `backend/app/main.py`
61	
62	**How to Run:**
63	```bash
64	cd backend
65	pip install -r requirements.txt
66	uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8000
67	```
68	
69	**Port:** 8000
70	
71	---
72	
73	### verbum-frontend
74	
75	**Tech Stack:**
76	- Language: TypeScript 5.5+
77	- Framework: React 18.3

entries.detail:
1	# Specification: Verbum — Catholic Content Management Platform
2	
3	## Overview
4	
5	Verbum is a greenfield, single-user Catholic content management platform for Ramir, a Portuguese-speaking Catholic content creator. The platform centralizes the full content lifecycle — from AI-assisted generation (via Anthropic Claude and OpenAI ChatGPT) through theological validation (via NotebookLM), editorial scheduling, and multi-channel delivery. It supports three content types: short-form video scripts (1 min), long-form video scripts (10 min), and SEO-optimized blog posts in Portuguese. The goal is to reduce time-to-publish by 50%, ensure 100% doctrinal accuracy through automated validation, and maintain a consistent publishing cadence of at least 3 pieces per week. The entire system is designed for a single user — no multi-tenancy, no scaling concerns, no monetization.
6	
7	## Workflow Type
8	
9	**Type**: feature
10	
11	**Rationale**: This is a brand-new full-stack application being built from scratch within an empty repository. There is no existing application logic to refactor or migrate — only a Vite/React/Tailwind prototype shell (`prototypes-magic/`) with reusable UI components. Every layer (database schema, backend API, service integrations, frontend pages) must be designed and implemented.
12	
13	## Task Scope
14	
15	### Services Involved
16	- **verbum-backend** (primary) — Python FastAPI backend handling API routing, AI integration orchestration, NotebookLM validation, database persistence, and scheduling logic
17	- **verbum-frontend** (primary) — React TypeScript SPA providing the dashboard, content editor, validation panel, editorial calendar, and SEO tools
18	- **SQLite database** (infrastructure) — Embedded file-based database for content items, validation records, scheduling metadata, and version history
19	
20	### This Task Will:
21	- [ ] Set up the full project structure (backend + frontend monorepo)
22	- [ ] Implement the Python FastAPI backend with async architecture
23	- [ ] Design and create the SQLite database schema via SQLModel
24	- [ ] Integrate Anthropic Claude API for AI content generation with streaming
25	- [ ] Integrate OpenAI ChatGPT API as an alternate drafting engine with streaming
26	- [ ] Integrate NotebookLM (via `notebooklm-py`) for theological validation with citation retrieval
27	- [ ] Build a validation abstraction layer to enable graceful degradation and provider swapping
28	- [ ] Create the React frontend with dashboard, content editor, validation panel, and calendar views
29	- [ ] Implement the full content lifecycle: draft → validated → scheduled → published
30	- [ ] Add Portuguese SEO metadata tools for blog post optimization
31	- [ ] Implement content versioning and validation history tracking
32	- [ ] Add iCal export for editorial calendar reminders
33	- [ ] Implement environment-based configuration with secure API key management
34	
35	### Out of Scope:
36	- Custom Catholic knowledge base or RAG pipeline (NotebookLM handles this)
37	- Social media analytics or community management
38	- Monetization or revenue features
39	- Multi-user authentication or multi-tenancy
40	- Direct Google Calendar API integration (using .ics export instead)
41	- Bulk social media publishing automation
42	- Mobile native app (responsive web only)
43	- Docker containerization (single-user local/hosted deployment)
44	
45	## Service Context
46	
47	### verbum-backend
48	
49	**Tech Stack:**
50	- Language: Python 3.11+
51	- Framework: FastAPI >= 0.135.0 (with `[standard]` extras for uvicorn)
52	- ORM: SQLModel >= 0.0.37 (Pydantic + SQLAlchemy combined)
53	- Database: SQLite (via aiosqlite for async operations)
54	- AI SDKs: `anthropic >= 0.88.0`, `openai >= 2.30.0`
55	- Validation: `notebooklm-py[browser] >= 0.3.4` (UNOFFICIAL — HIGH RISK)
56	- Calendar: `icalendar` for .ics export
57	- Config: `python-dotenv` for environment variable management
58	- Key directories: `backend/app/`, `backend/app/api/`, `backend/app/models/`, `backend/app/services/`, `backend/app/core/`
59	
60	**Entry Point:** `backend/app/main.py`
61	
62	**How to Run:**
63	```bash
64	cd backend
65	pip install -r requirements.txt
66	uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8000
67	```
68	
69	**Port:** 8000
70	
71	---
72	
73	### verbum-frontend
74	
75	**Tech Stack:**
76	- Language: TypeScript 5.5+
77	- Framework: React 18.3
78	- Bundler: Vite 5.2
79	- Styling: Tailwind CSS 3.4
80	- Icons: lucide-react 0.522
81	- UI Components: Reuse from `prototypes-magic/src/components/ui/` (Button, Input, Select, Card, Tabs, Badge, Table, Textarea, Progress, etc.)
82	- Charts: recharts (already in prototype dependencies)
83	- Key directories: `frontend/src/`, `frontend/src/components/`, `frontend/src/pages/`, `frontend/src/services/`, `frontend/src/hooks/`
84	
85	**Entry Point:** `frontend/src/main.tsx`
86	
87	**How to Run:**
88	```bash
89	cd frontend
90	npm install
91	npm run dev
92	```
93	
94	**Port:** 5173 (Vite default)
95	
96	## Files to Create
97	
98	### Backend Structure
99	
100	| File | Service | Purpose |

207:### Pattern 1: Async FastAPI with SQLModel

1	# Anthropic Claude API
2	ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=your-anthropic-api-key-here
3	
4	# OpenAI ChatGPT API
5	OPENAI_API_KEY=your-openai-api-key-here
6	
7	# Database
8	DATABASE_URL=sqlite+aiosqlite:///./verbum.db
9	
10	# Application
11	APP_ENV=development
12	DEBUG=true
13

207	### Pattern 1: Async FastAPI with SQLModel
208	
209	Industry best practice for Python async web applications:
210	
211	```python
212	# backend/app/main.py
213	from contextlib import asynccontextmanager
214	from fastapi import FastAPI
215	from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware
216	from app.core.database import init_db
217	from app.api import content, generation, validation, schedule, seo, dashboard
218	
219	@asynccontextmanager
220	async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):
221	    await init_db()
222	    yield
223	
224	app = FastAPI(title="Verbum API", lifespan=lifespan)
225	app.add_middleware(
226	    CORSMiddleware,
227	    allow_origins=["http://localhost:5173"],
228	    allow_credentials=True,
229	    allow_methods=["*"],
230	    allow_headers=["*"],
231	)
232	app.include_router(content.router, prefix="/api/content", tags=["content"])
233	app.include_router(generation.router, prefix="/api/generation", tags=["generation"])
234	app.include_router(validation.router, prefix="/api/validation", tags=["validation"])
235	app.include_router(schedule.router, prefix="/api/schedule", tags=["schedule"])
236	app.include_router(seo.router, prefix="/api/seo", tags=["seo"])
237	app.include_router(dashboard.router, prefix="/api/dashboard", tags=["dashboard"])
238	```
239	
240	**Key Points:**
241	- Use `lifespan` context manager for database initialization (not deprecated `@app.on_event`)
242	- Configure CORS for frontend-backend communication
243	- Organize endpoints into routers with prefixes and tags
244	- All handlers should be `async def` for non-blocking I/O
245	
246	### Pattern 2: SQLModel Data Models with Status Enum
247	
248	```python
249	# backend/app/models/content.py
250	from enum import Enum
251	from datetime import datetime, timezone
252	from typing import Optional
253	from sqlmodel import SQLModel, Field
254	from uuid import uuid4
255	
256	class ContentType(str, Enum):
257	    SHORT_VIDEO = "short_video"
258	    LONG_VIDEO = "long_video"
259	    BLOG_POST = "blog_post"
260	
261	class ContentStatus(str, Enum):
262	    DRAFT = "draft"
263	    VALIDATED = "validated"
264	    SCHEDULED = "scheduled"
265	    PUBLISHED = "published"
266	
267	class ContentItem(SQLModel, table=True):
268	    __tablename__ = "content_items"
269	
270	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid4()), primary_key=True)
271	    title: str
272	    body: str = ""
273	    content_type: ContentType
274	    status: ContentStatus = ContentStatus.DRAFT
275	    language: str = "pt"
276	    channels: str = "[]"  # JSON list of channel strings, e.g. '["youtube", "blog"]'
277	    ai_model_used: Optional[str] = None
278	    scheduled_date: Optional[datetime] = None
279	    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
280	    updated_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
281	    version: int = 1
282	```
283	
284	**Key Points:**
285	- Use string enums for readable database values
286	- UUID primary keys for content items

Subtask-1-2 is complete. Here's what was done:

1. **`backend/app/core/config.py`** — `Settings` class using Pydantic `BaseSettings` with env vars for API keys, database URL, app env, and debug flag. `get_settings()` with `@lru_cache` for singleton pattern.

6	
7	## Workflow Type

9	**Type**: feature
10	
11	**Rationale**: This is a brand-new full-stack application being built from scratch within an empty repository. There is no existing application logic to refactor or migrate — only a Vite/React/Tailwind prototype shell (`prototypes-magic/`) with reusable UI components. Every layer (database schema, backend API, service integrations, frontend pages) must be designed and implemented.

16	- **verbum-backend** (primary) — Python FastAPI backend handling API routing, AI integration orchestration, NotebookLM validation, database persistence, and scheduling logic

18	- **SQLite database** (infrastructure) — Embedded file-based database for content items, validation records, scheduling metadata, and version history

248	```python
249	# backend/app/models/content.py
250	from enum import Enum
251	from datetime import datetime, timezone
252	from typing import Optional
253	from sqlmodel import SQLModel, Field
254	from uuid import uuid4
255	
256	class ContentType(str, Enum):
257	    SHORT_VIDEO = "short_video"
258	    LONG_VIDEO = "long_video"
259	    BLOG_POST = "blog_post"
260	
261	class ContentStatus(str, Enum):
262	    DRAFT = "draft"
263	    VALIDATED = "validated"
264	    SCHEDULED = "scheduled"
265	    PUBLISHED = "published"
266	
267	class ContentItem(SQLModel, table=True):
268	    __tablename__ = "content_items"
269	
270	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid4()), primary_key=True)
271	    title: str
272	    body: str = ""
273	    content_type: ContentType
274	    status: ContentStatus = ContentStatus.DRAFT
275	    language: str = "pt"
276	    channels: str = "[]"  # JSON list of channel strings, e.g. '["youtube", "blog"]'
277	    ai_model_used: Optional[str] = None
278	    scheduled_date: Optional[datetime] = None
279	    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
280	    updated_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
281	    version: int = 1
282	```
283	
284	**Key Points:**
285	- Use string enums for readable database values
286	- UUID primary keys for content items
287	- Default to Portuguese language (`pt`)
288	- Track which AI model generated the draft
289	- Version counter for content versioning
290	
291	### Pattern 3: AI Service Abstraction with Streaming
292	
293	```python
294	# backend/app/services/ai_service.py
295	from typing import Protocol, AsyncIterator
296	
297	class AIProvider(Protocol):
298	    async def generate(self, prompt: str, system_prompt: str, max_tokens: int) -> str: ...
299	    async def generate_stream(self, prompt: str, system_prompt: str, max_tokens: int) -> AsyncIterator[str]: ...
300	
301	class AIService:
302	    def __init__(self, providers: dict[str, AIProvider]):
303	        self._providers = providers
304	
305	    async def generate(self, model_key: str, prompt: str, system_prompt: str, max_tokens: int = 2048) -> str:
306	        provider = self._providers[model_key]
307	        return await provider.generate(prompt, system_prompt, max_tokens)
308	
309	    async def generate_stream(self, model_key: str, prompt: str, system_prompt: str, max_tokens: int = 2048) -> AsyncIterator[str]:
310	        provider = self._providers[model_key]
311	        async for chunk in provider.generate_stream(prompt, system_prompt, max_tokens):
312	            yield chunk
313	```
314	
315	**Key Points:**
316	- Use Python `Protocol` for interface definition (structural typing)
317	- Support both full-response and streaming generation
318	- Provider pattern allows easy addition of new AI models
319	- The `model_key` selects between Claude and ChatGPT
320	
321	### Pattern 4: Validation Service with Fallback
322	
323	```python
324	# backend/app/services/validation_service.py
325	from typing import Protocol
326	from dataclasses import dataclass
327

1	# Specification: Verbum — Catholic Content Management Platform

5	Verbum is a greenfield, single-user Catholic content management platform for Ramir, a Portuguese-speaking Catholic content creator. The platform centralizes the full content lifecycle — from AI-assisted generation (via Anthropic Claude and OpenAI ChatGPT) through theological validation (via NotebookLM), editorial scheduling, and multi-channel delivery. It supports three content types: short-form video scripts (1 min), long-form video scripts (10 min), and SEO-optimized blog posts in Portuguese. The goal is to reduce time-to-publish by 50%, ensure 100% doctrinal accuracy through automated validation, and maintain a consistent publishing cadence of at least 3 pieces per week.
