200 | `prototypes-magic/src/components/ui/progress/Progress.tsx` | Progress bar — reuse for validation score display | 201 | `prototypes-magic/tailwind.config.js` | Tailwind theme configuration — extend for Verbum's color palette | 202 | `prototypes-magic/vite.config.ts` | Vite configuration — use as base, add API proxy | 203 | `prototypes-magic/package.json` | Dependency baseline — reuse core deps, add routing + calendar libs | 204 205 ## Patterns to Follow 206 207 ### Pattern 1: Async FastAPI with SQLModel 208 209 Industry best practice for Python async web applications: 210 211 ```python 212 # backend/app/main.py 213 from contextlib import asynccontextmanager 214 from fastapi import FastAPI 215 from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware 216 from app.core.database import init_db 217 from app.api import content, generation, validation, schedule, seo, dashboard 218 219 @asynccontextmanager 220 async def lifespan(app: FastAPI): 221 await init_db() 222 yield 223 224 app = FastAPI(title="Verbum API", lifespan=lifespan) 225 app.add_middleware( 226 CORSMiddleware, 227 allow_origins=["http://localhost:5173"], 228 allow_credentials=True, 229 allow_methods=["*"], 230 allow_headers=["*"], 231 ) 232 app.include_router(content.router, prefix="/api/content", tags=["content"]) 233 app.include_router(generation.router, prefix="/api/generation", tags=["generation"]) 234 app.include_router(validation.router, prefix="/api/validation", tags=["validation"]) 235 app.include_router(schedule.router, prefix="/api/schedule", tags=["schedule"]) 236 app.include_router(seo.router, prefix="/api/seo", tags=["seo"]) 237 app.include_router(dashboard.router, prefix="/api/dashboard", tags=["dashboard"]) 238 ``` 239 240 **Key Points:** 241 - Use `lifespan` context manager for database initialization (not deprecated `@app.on_event`) 242 - Configure CORS for frontend-backend communication 243 - Organize endpoints into routers with prefixes and tags 244 - All handlers should be `async def` for non-blocking I/O 245 246 ### Pattern 2: SQLModel Data Models with Status Enum 247 248 ```python 249 # backend/app/models/content.py 250 from enum import Enum 251 from datetime import datetime, timezone 252 from typing import Optional 253 from sqlmodel import SQLModel, Field 254 from uuid import uuid4 255 256 class ContentType(str, Enum): 257 SHORT_VIDEO = "short_video" 258 LONG_VIDEO = "long_video" 259 BLOG_POST = "blog_post" 260 261 class ContentStatus(str, Enum): 262 DRAFT = "draft" 263 VALIDATED = "validated" 264 SCHEDULED = "scheduled" 265 PUBLISHED = "published" 266 267 class ContentItem(SQLModel, table=True): 268 __tablename__ = "content_items" 269 270 id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid4()), primary_key=True) 271 title: str 272 body: str = "" 273 content_type: ContentType 274 status: ContentStatus = ContentStatus.DRAFT 275 language: str = "pt" 276 channels: str = "[]" # JSON list of channel strings, e.g. '["youtube", "blog"]' 277 ai_model_used: Optional[str] = None 278 scheduled_date: Optional[datetime] = None 279 created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc)) 280 updated_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc)) 281 version: int = 1 282 ``` 283 284 **Key Points:** 285 - Use string enums for readable database values 286 - UUID primary keys for content items 287 - Default to Portuguese language (`pt`) 288 - Track which AI model generated the draft 289 - Version counter for content versioning 290 291 ### Pattern 3: AI Service Abstraction with Streaming 292 293 ```python 294 # backend/app/services/ai_service.py 295 from typing import Protocol, AsyncIterator 296 297 class AIProvider(Protocol): 298 async def generate(self, prompt: str, system_prompt: str, max_tokens: int) -> str: ... 299 async def generate_stream(self, prompt: str, system_prompt: str, max_tokens: int) -> AsyncIterator[str]: ... 300 301 class AIService: 302 def __init__(self, providers: dict[str, AIProvider]): 303 self._providers = providers 304 305 async def generate(self, model_key: str, prompt: str, system_prompt: str, max_tokens: int = 2048) -> str: 306 provider = self._providers[model_key] 307 return await provider.generate(prompt, system_prompt, max_tokens) 308 309 async def generate_stream(self, model_key: str, prompt: str, system_prompt: str, max_tokens: int = 2048) -> AsyncIterator[str]: 310 provider = self._providers[model_key] 311 async for chunk in provider.generate_stream(prompt, system_prompt, max_tokens): 312 yield chunk 313 ``` 314 315 **Key Points:** 316 - Use Python `Protocol` for interface definition (structural typing) 317 - Support both full-response and streaming generation 318 - Provider pattern allows easy addition of new AI models 319 - The `model_key` selects between Claude and ChatGPT 320 321 ### Pattern 4: Validation Service with Fallback 322 323 ```python 324 # backend/app/services/validation_service.py 325 from typing import Protocol 326 from dataclasses import dataclass 327 328 @dataclass 329 class Citation: 330 citation_number: int 331 source_id: str 332 cited_text: str 333 334 @dataclass 335 class ValidationResult: 336 score: float # 0.0 to 1.0 337 flags: list[str] 338 citations: list[Citation] 339 provider: str # "notebooklm" or "ai_fallback" 340 raw_answer: str 341 342 class ValidationProvider(Protocol): 343 async def validate(self, content: str, content_type: str) -> ValidationResult: ... 344 async def is_available(self) -> bool: ... 345 346 class ValidationService: 347 def __init__(self, primary: ValidationProvider, fallback: ValidationProvider): 348 self._primary = primary 349 self._fallback = fallback 350 351 async def validate(self, content: str, content_type: str) -> ValidationResult: 352 if await self._primary.is_available(): 353 try: 354 return await self._primary.validate(content, content_type) 355 except Exception: 356 pass 357 return await self._fallback.validate(content, content_type) 358 ``` 359 360 **Key Points:** 361 - Abstraction layer critical for NotebookLM reliability concerns 362 - `is_available()` check before attempting validation 363 - Automatic fallback to AI-based validation if NotebookLM is down 364 - Structured `ValidationResult` with score, flags, and traceable citations 365 - The `provider` field tells the UI which backend produced the result 366 367 ### Pattern 5: SSE Streaming from FastAPI to React 368 369 ```python 370 # backend/app/api/generation.py 371 from fastapi import APIRouter 372 from fastapi.responses import StreamingResponse 373 374 router = APIRouter() 375 376 @router.post("/stream") 377 async def generate_stream(request: GenerationRequest): 378 async def event_generator(): 379 async for chunk in ai_service.generate_stream( 380 model_key=request.model, 381 prompt=request.prompt, 382 system_prompt=request.system_prompt, 383 ): 384 yield f"data: {json.dumps({'text': chunk})}\n\n" 385 yield f"data: {json.dumps({'done': True})}\n\n" 386 387 return StreamingResponse(event_generator(), media_type="text/event-stream") 388 ``` 389 390 ```typescript 391 // frontend/src/services/streaming.ts 392 export async function* streamGeneration(request: GenerationRequest): AsyncGenerator { 393 const response = await fetch('/api/generation/stream', { 394 method: 'POST', 395 headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }, 396 body: JSON.stringify(request), 397 }); 398 const reader = response.body!.getReader(); 399 const decoder = new TextDecoder(); 400 while (true) { 401 const { done, value } = await reader.read(); 402 if (done) break; 403 const text = decoder.decode(value); 404 for (const line of text.split('\n')) { 405 if (line.startsWith('data: ')) { 406 const data = JSON.parse(line.slice(6)); 407 if (data.done) return; 408 yield data.text; 409 } 410 } 411 } 412 } 413 ``` 414 415 **Key Points:** 416 - FastAPI `StreamingResponse` with SSE format (`data: ...\n\n`) 417 - React consumes via `ReadableStream` reader 418 - JSON-encoded chunks allow metadata alongside text 419 - Terminal `done` event signals completion 420 - Dramatically improves perceived performance for content generation 421 422 ### Pattern 6: React Page with Editor + Right Panel Layout 423 424 ```tsx 425 // frontend/src/pages/EditorPage.tsx 426 export function EditorPage() { 427 return ( 428
429 {/* Main Editor - Single Column */} 430
431 432 433 434
435 {/* Validation Panel - Fixed Right */} 436
437 438
439
440 ); 441 } 442 ``` 443 444 **Key Points:** 445 - Flexbox layout with fixed-width right panel (per UX requirements) 446 - Editor takes remaining space (`flex-1`) 447 - Validation panel is always visible alongside editor 448 - Both panels independently scrollable 449 450 ## Requirements 451 452 ### Functional Requirements 453 454 1. **AI Content Generation (High Priority)** 455 - Description: Connect to Anthropic Claude and OpenAI ChatGPT APIs to generate content drafts from user prompts. Support three content type templates: short-form video (1 min), long-form video (10 min), and SEO blog post. Stream AI responses in real-time to the editor. 456 - Acceptance: User can select an AI model (Claude or ChatGPT), enter a prompt, and see the generated draft stream into the editor in under 5 seconds. Content type template structures the system prompt appropriately. 457 458 2. **Content Type Templates (High Priority)** 459 - Description: Pre-configured templates that structure AI prompts ... 132:| `backend/app/api/seo.py` | backend | SEO analysis endpoints |

243:- Organize endpoints into routers with prefixes and tags
510:- Use `async def` for ALL FastAPI endpoints and service methods — the entire backend must be non-blocking
667:## API Endpoints
731:4. Build generation API endpoints (full + streaming)
740:4. Build validation API endpoints

667	## API Endpoints
668	
669	### Content API (`/api/content`)
670	| Method | Path | Description |
671	|--------|------|-------------|
672	| `GET` | `/` | List all content items (with filtering: status, type, channel) |
673	| `POST` | `/` | Create a new content item |
674	| `GET` | `/{id}` | Get a single content item |
675	| `PUT` | `/{id}` | Update a content item (body, title, status, channel) |
676	| `DELETE` | `/{id}` | Delete a content item |
677	| `GET` | `/{id}/versions` | Get version history for a content item |
678	| `GET` | `/{id}/versions/{version}` | Get a specific version snapshot |
679	| `POST` | `/{id}/status` | Transition content status (with validation) |
680	
681	### Generation API (`/api/generation`)
682	| Method | Path | Description |
683	|--------|------|-------------|
684	| `POST` | `/generate` | Generate content (full response) |
685	| `POST` | `/stream` | Generate content with SSE streaming |
686	| `GET` | `/models` | List available AI models and their status |
687	
688	### Validation API (`/api/validation`)
689	| Method | Path | Description |
690	|--------|------|-------------|
691	| `POST` | `/validate/{content_id}` | Trigger validation for a content item |
692	| `GET` | `/history/{content_id}` | Get validation history for a content item |
693	| `GET` | `/status` | Check validation service availability |
694	
695	### Schedule API (`/api/schedule`)
696	| Method | Path | Description |
697	|--------|------|-------------|
698	| `GET` | `/calendar` | Get scheduled items for a date range |
699	| `POST` | `/{content_id}/schedule` | Schedule a content item |
700	| `PUT` | `/{content_id}/reschedule` | Reschedule a content item |
701	| `GET` | `/export/ical` | Export calendar as .ics file |
702	
703	### Dashboard API (`/api/dashboard`)
704	| Method | Path | Description |
705	|--------|------|-------------|
706	| `GET` | `/summary` | Pipeline summary (counts per status, recent items) |
707	| `GET` | `/upcoming` | Next 7 days of scheduled content |
708	
709	### SEO API (`/api/seo`)
710	| Method | Path | Description |
711	|--------|------|-------------|
712	| `GET` | `/{content_id}` | Get SEO metadata for a content item |
713	| `PUT` | `/{content_id}` | Update SEO metadata |
714	| `POST` | `/{content_id}/analyze` | Run readability analysis |
715	| `POST` | `/{content_id}/keywords` | Generate keyword suggestions via AI |
716	
717	## Implementation Phases
718	
719	### Phase 1: Foundation (Backend + Frontend Skeleton)
720	1. Set up project structure (backend and frontend directories)
721	2. Create FastAPI app with CORS, lifespan, and router structure
722	3. Define SQLModel data models and database initialization
723	4. Create React app with routing, Tailwind, and UI component library
724	5. Implement content CRUD (backend API + frontend list/create pages)
725	6. **Milestone**: Can create, list, edit, and delete content items
726	
727	### Phase 2: AI Generation Integration
728	1. Implement Claude provider with streaming
729	2. Implement OpenAI provider with streaming
730	3. Create AI service abstraction layer
731	4. Build generation API endpoints (full + streaming)
732	5. Build frontend GenerationPanel with model selector and streaming display
733	6. Implement content type templates (system prompts per type)
734	7. **Milestone**: Can generate content via Claude or ChatGPT with real-time streaming
735	
736	### Phase 3: Theological Validation
737	1. Implement NotebookLM provider with error handling and retry
738	2. Implement AI-based fallback validation provider
739	3. Create validation service with automatic failover
740	4. Build validation API endpoints
741	5. Build frontend ValidationPanel with score, flags, citations
742	6. Implement validation history tracking
743	7. **Milestone**: Can validate content with citation display and fallback
744	
745	### Phase 4: Calendar, SEO, and Polish
746	1. Build editorial calendar UI (week/month views)

entries.detail:
1	# Specification: Verbum — Catholic Content Management Platform
2	
3	## Overview
4	
5	Verbum is a greenfield, single-user Catholic content management platform for Ramir, a Portuguese-speaking Catholic content creator. The platform centralizes the full content lifecycle — from AI-assisted generation (via Anthropic Claude and OpenAI ChatGPT) through theological validation (via NotebookLM), editorial scheduling, and multi-channel delivery. It supports three content types: short-form video scripts (1 min), long-form video scripts (10 min), and SEO-optimized blog posts in Portuguese. The goal is to reduce time-to-publish by 50%, ensure 100% doctrinal accuracy through automated validation, and maintain a consistent publishing cadence of at least 3 pieces per week. The entire system is designed for a single user — no multi-tenancy, no scaling concerns, no monetization.
6	
7	## Workflow Type
8	
9	**Type**: feature
10	
11	**Rationale**: This is a brand-new full-stack application being built from scratch within an empty repository. There is no existing application logic to refactor or migrate — only a Vite/React/Tailwind prototype shell (`prototypes-magic/`) with reusable UI components. Every layer (database schema, backend API, service integrations, frontend pages) must be designed and implemented.
12	
13	## Task Scope
14	
15	### Services Involved
16	- **verbum-backend** (primary) — Python FastAPI backend handling API routing, AI integration orchestration, NotebookLM validation, database persistence, and scheduling logic
17	- **verbum-frontend** (primary) — React TypeScript SPA providing the dashboard, content editor, validation panel, editorial calendar, and SEO tools
18	- **SQLite database** (infrastructure) — Embedded file-based database for content items, validation records, scheduling metadata, and version history
19	
20	### This Task Will:
21	- [ ] Set up the full project structure (backend + frontend monorepo)
22	- [ ] Implement the Python FastAPI backend with async architecture
23	- [ ] Design and create the SQLite database schema via SQLModel
24	- [ ] Integrate Anthropic Claude API for AI content generation with streaming
25	- [ ] Integrate OpenAI ChatGPT API as an alternate drafting engine with streaming
26	- [ ] Integrate NotebookLM (via `notebooklm-py`) for theological validation with citation retrieval
27	- [ ] Build a validation abstraction layer to enable graceful degradation and provider swapping
28	- [ ] Create the React frontend with dashboard, content editor, validation panel, and calendar views
29	- [ ] Implement the full content lifecycle: draft → validated → scheduled → published
30	- [ ] Add Portuguese SEO metadata tools for blog post optimization
31	- [ ] Implement content versioning and validation history tracking
32	- [ ] Add iCal export for editorial calendar reminders
33	- [ ] Implement environment-based configuration with secure API key management
34	
35	### Out of Scope:
36	- Custom Catholic knowledge base or RAG pipeline (NotebookLM handles this)
37	- Social media analytics or community management
38	- Monetization or revenue features
39	- Multi-user authentication or multi-tenancy
40	- Direct Google Calendar API integration (using .ics export instead)
41	- Bulk social media publishing automation
42	- Mobile native app (responsive web only)
43	- Docker containerization (single-user local/hosted deployment)
44	
45	## Service Context
46	
47	### verbum-backend
48	
49	**Tech Stack:**
50	- Language: Python 3.11+
51	- Framework: FastAPI >= 0.135.0 (with `[standard]` extras for uvicorn)
52	- ORM: SQLModel >= 0.0.37 (Pydantic + SQLAlchemy combined)
53	- Database: SQLite (via aiosqlite for async operations)
54	- AI SDKs: `anthropic >= 0.88.0`, `openai >= 2.30.0`
55	- Validation: `notebooklm-py[browser] >= 0.3.4` (UNOFFICIAL — HIGH RISK)
56	- Calendar: `icalendar` for .ics export
57	- Config: `python-dotenv` for environment variable management
58	- Key directories: `backend/app/`, `backend/app/api/`, `backend/app/models/`, `backend/app/services/`, `backend/app/core/`
59	
60	**Entry Point:** `backend/app/main.py`
61	
62	**How to Run:**
63	```bash
64	cd backend
65	pip install -r requirements.txt
66	uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8000
67	```
68	
69	**Port:** 8000
70	
71	---
72	
73	### verbum-frontend
74	
75	**Tech Stack:**
76	- Language: TypeScript 5.5+
77	- Framework: React 18.3
78	- Bundler: Vite 5.2
79	- Styling: Tailwind CSS 3.4
80	- Icons: lucide-react 0.522
81	- UI Components: Reuse from `prototypes-magic/src/components/ui/` (Button, Input, Select, Card, Tabs, Badge, Table, Textarea, Progress, etc.)
82	- Charts: recharts (already in prototype dependencies)
83	- Key directories: `frontend/src/`, `frontend/src/components/`, `frontend/src/pages/`, `frontend/src/services/`, `frontend/src/hooks/`
84	
85	**Entry Point:** `frontend/src/main.tsx`
86	
87	**How to Run:**
88	```bash
89	cd frontend
90	npm install
91	npm run dev
92	```
93	
94	**Port:** 5173 (Vite default)
95	
96	## Files to Create

202	| `prototypes-magic/vite.config.ts` | Vite configuration — use as base, add API proxy |
203	| `prototypes-magic/package.json` | Dependency baseline — reuse core deps, add routing + calendar libs |
204	
205	## Patterns to Follow
206	
207	### Pattern 1: Async FastAPI with SQLModel
208	
209	Industry best practice for Python async web applications:
210	
211	```python
212	# backend/app/main.py
213	from contextlib import asynccontextmanager
214	from fastapi import FastAPI
215	from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware
216	from app.core.database import init_db
217	from app.api import content, generation, validation, schedule, seo, dashboard
218	
219	@asynccontextmanager
220	async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):
221	    await init_db()
222	    yield
223	
224	app = FastAPI(title="Verbum API", lifespan=lifespan)
225	app.add_middleware(
226	    CORSMiddleware,
227	    allow_origins=["http://localhost:5173"],
228	    allow_credentials=True,
229	    allow_methods=["*"],
230	    allow_headers=["*"],
231	)
232	app.include_router(content.router, prefix="/api/content", tags=["content"])
233	app.include_router(generation.router, prefix="/api/generation", tags=["generation"])
234	app.include_router(validation.router, prefix="/api/validation", tags=["validation"])
235	app.include_router(schedule.router, prefix="/api/schedule", tags=["schedule"])
236	app.include_router(seo.router, prefix="/api/seo", tags=["seo"])
237	app.include_router(dashboard.router, prefix="/api/dashboard", tags=["dashboard"])
238	```
239	
240	**Key Points:**
241	- Use `lifespan` context manager for database initialization (not deprecated `@app.on_event`)
242	- Configure CORS for frontend-backend communication
243	- Organize endpoints into routers with prefixes and tags
244	- All handlers should be `async def` for non-blocking I/O
245

entries.content:
Subtask-1-5 is complete. The `ContentService` implements:

- **CRUD**: `create`, `get`, `list` (with filtering by status/type/channel), `update`, `delete`
- **Version snapshots**: Automatically created before each update via `_create_version_snapshot`
- **Status transitions**: Enforced via `VALID_TRANSITIONS` map (draft→validated→scheduled→published, with backward transitions for validated→draft and scheduled→validated)
- **All async**: Every method is `async def` using the async SQLAlchemy session

entries.detail:
1	# Specification: Verbum — Catholic Content Management Platform
2	
3	## Overview
4	
5	Verbum is a greenfield, single-user Catholic content management platform for Ramir, a Portuguese-speaking Catholic content creator. The platform centralizes the full content lifecycle — from AI-assisted generation (via Anthropic Claude and OpenAI ChatGPT) through theological validation (via NotebookLM), editorial scheduling, and multi-channel delivery. It supports three content types: short-form video scripts (1 min), long-form video scripts (10 min), and SEO-optimized blog posts in Portuguese. The goal is to reduce time-to-publish by 50%, ensure 100% doctrinal accuracy through automated validation, and maintain a consistent publishing cadence of at least 3 pieces per week. The entire system is designed for a single user — no multi-tenancy, no scaling concerns, no monetization.
6	
7	## Workflow Type
8	
9	**Type**: feature
10	
11	**Rationale**: This is a brand-new full-stack application being built from scratch within an empty repository. There is no existing application logic to refactor or migrate — only a Vite/React/Tailwind prototype shell (`prototypes-magic/`) with reusable UI components. Every layer (database schema, backend API, service integrations, frontend pages) must be designed and implemented.
12	
13	## Task Scope
14	
15	### Services Involved
16	- **verbum-backend** (primary) — Python FastAPI backend handling API routing, AI integration orchestration, NotebookLM validation, database persistence, and scheduling logic
17	- **verbum-frontend** (primary) — React TypeScript SPA providing the dashboard, content editor, validation panel, editorial calendar, and SEO tools
18	- **SQLite database** (infrastructure) — Embedded file-based database for content items, validation records, scheduling metadata, and version history
19	
20	### This Task Will:
21	- [ ] Set up the full project structure (backend + frontend monorepo)
22	- [ ] Implement the Python FastAPI backend with async architecture
23	- [ ] Design and create the SQLite database schema via SQLModel
24	- [ ] Integrate Anthropic Claude API for AI content generation with streaming
25	- [ ] Integrate OpenAI ChatGPT API as an alternate drafting engine with streaming
26	- [ ] Integrate NotebookLM (via `notebooklm-py`) for theological validation with citation retrieval
27	- [ ] Build a validation abstraction layer to enable graceful degradation and provider swapping
28	- [ ] Create the React frontend with dashboard, content editor, validation panel, and calendar views
29	- [ ] Implement the full content lifecycle: draft → validated → scheduled → published
30	- [ ] Add Portuguese SEO metadata tools for blog post optimization
31	- [ ] Implement content versioning and validation history tracking
32	- [ ] Add iCal export for editorial calendar reminders
33	- [ ] Implement environment-based configuration with secure API key management
34	
35	### Out of Scope:
36	- Custom Catholic knowledge base or RAG pipeline (NotebookLM handles this)
37	- Social media analytics or community management
38	- Monetization or revenue features
39	- Multi-user authentication or multi-tenancy
40	- Direct Google Calendar API integration (using .ics export instead)
41	- Bulk social media publishing automation
42	- Mobile native app (responsive web only)
43	- Docker containerization (single-user local/hosted deployment)
44	
45	## Service Context
46	
47	### verbum-backend
48	
49	**Tech Stack:**
50	- Language: Python 3.11+
51	- Framework: FastAPI >= 0.135.0 (with `[standard]` extras for uvicorn)
52	- ORM: SQLModel >= 0.0.37 (Pydantic + SQLAlchemy combined)
53	- Database: SQLite (via aiosqlite for async operations)
54	- AI SDKs: `anthropic >= 0.88.0`, `openai >= 2.30.0`
55	- Validation: `notebooklm-py[browser] >= 0.3.4` (UNOFFICIAL — HIGH RISK)
56	- Calendar: `icalendar` for .ics export
57	- Config: `python-dotenv` for environment variable management
58	- Key directories: `backend/app/`, `backend/app/api/`, `backend/app/models/`, `backend/app/services/`, `backend/app/core/`
59	
60	**Entry Point:** `backend/app/main.py`
61	
62	**How to Run:**
63	```bash
64	cd backend
65	pip install -r requirements.txt
66	uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8000
67	```
68	
69	**Port:** 8000
70	
71	---
72	
73	### verbum-frontend
74	
75	**Tech Stack:**
76	- Language: TypeScript 5.5+
77	- Framework: React 18.3
78	- Bundler: Vite 5.2
79	- Styling: Tailwind CSS 3.4
80	- Icons: lucide-react 0.522
81	- UI Components: Reuse from `prototypes-magic/src/components/ui/` (Button, Input, Select, Card, Tabs, Badge, Table, Textarea, Progress, etc.)
82	- Charts: recharts (already in prototype dependencies)
83	- Key directories: `frontend/src/`, `frontend/src/components/`, `frontend/src/pages/`, `frontend/src/services/`, `frontend/src/hooks/`
84	
85	**Entry Point:** `frontend/src/main.tsx`
86	
87	**How to Run:**
88	```bash
89	cd frontend
90	npm install
91	npm run dev
92	```
93	
94	**Port:** 5173 (Vite default)
95	
96	## Files to Create
97	
98	### Backend Structure
99	
100	| File | Service | Purpose |

177	| `frontend/src/components/ui/` | frontend | Copy/adapt UI primitives from `prototypes-magic/src/components/ui/` |
178	
179	### Root Files
180	
181	| File | Purpose |
182	|------|---------|
183	| `.env.example` | Root-level environment variable template |
184	| `README.md` | Project overview and setup instructions |
185	
186	## Files to Reference (Existing Patterns)
187	
188	These files from `prototypes-magic/` demonstrate patterns to reuse:
189	
190	| File | Pattern to Copy |
191	|------|----------------|
192	| `prototypes-magic/src/components/ui/button/Button.tsx` | Button component with CVA variants — reuse for all interactive elements |
193	| `prototypes-magic/src/components/ui/card/Card.tsx` | Card layout component — reuse for content cards, dashboard cards |
194	| `prototypes-magic/src/components/ui/input/Input.tsx` | Input component styling — reuse for forms |
195	| `prototypes-magic/src/components/ui/select/Select.tsx` | Select dropdown component — reuse for model selector, content type picker |
196	| `prototypes-magic/src/components/ui/tabs/Tabs.tsx` | Tabs component — reuse for editor/validation/SEO tab switching |
197	| `prototypes-magic/src/components/ui/badge/Badge.tsx` | Badge component — adapt for status badges |
198	| `prototypes-magic/src/components/ui/textarea/Textarea.tsx` | Textarea styling — reuse for prompt input, editor |
199	| `prototypes-magic/src/components/ui/table/Table.tsx` | Table component — reuse for content list, validation history |
200	| `prototypes-magic/src/components/ui/progress/Progress.tsx` | Progress bar — reuse for validation score display |
201	| `prototypes-magic/tailwind.config.js` | Tailwind theme configuration — extend for Verbum's color palette |
202	| `prototypes-magic/vite.config.ts` | Vite configuration — use as base, add API proxy |
203	| `prototypes-magic/package.json` | Dependency baseline — reuse core deps, add routing + calendar libs |
204	
205	## Patterns to Follow
206	
207	### Pattern 1: Async FastAPI with SQLModel
208	
209	Industry best practice for Python async web applications:
210	
211	```python
212	# backend/app/main.py
213	from contextlib import asynccontextmanager
214	from fastapi import FastAPI
215	from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware
216	from app.core.database import init_db
217	from app.api import content, generation, validation, schedule, seo, dashboard
218	
219	@asynccontextmanager
220	async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):
221	    await init_db()
222	    yield
223	
224	app = FastAPI(title="Verbum API", lifespan=lifespan)
225	app.add_middleware(
226	    CORSMiddleware,
227	    allow_origins=["http://localhost:5173"],
228	    allow_credentials=True,
229	    allow_methods=["*"],
230	    allow_headers=["*"],
231	)
232	app.include_router(content.router, prefix="/api/content", tags=["content"])
233	app.include_router(generation.router, prefix="/api/generation", tags=["generation"])
234	app.include_router(validation.router, prefix="/api/validation", tags=["validation"])
235	app.include_router(schedule.router, prefix="/api/schedule", tags=["schedule"])
236	app.include_router(seo.router, prefix="/api/seo", tags=["seo"])
237	app.include_router(dashboard.router, prefix="/api/dashboard", tags=["dashboard"])
238	```
239	
240	**Key Points:**
241	- Use `lifespan` context manager for database initialization (not deprecated `@app.on_event`)
242	- Configure CORS for frontend-backend communication
243	- Organize endpoints into routers with prefixes and tags
244	- All handlers should be `async def` for non-blocking I/O
245

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	import json
4	from datetime import datetime, timezone
5	from typing import Optional
6	
7	from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession
8	from sqlmodel import select
9	
10	from app.models.content import ContentItem, ContentStatus, ContentType, ContentVersion
11	from app.schemas.content import ContentCreate, ContentListParams, ContentUpdate
12	
13	
14	# Valid status transitions: draft→validated→scheduled→published
15	VALID_TRANSITIONS: dict[ContentStatus, list[ContentStatus]] = {
16	    ContentStatus.DRAFT: [ContentStatus.VALIDATED],
17	    ContentStatus.VALIDATED: [ContentStatus.SCHEDULED, ContentStatus.DRAFT],
18	    ContentStatus.SCHEDULED: [ContentStatus.PUBLISHED, ContentStatus.VALIDATED],
19	    ContentStatus.PUBLISHED: [],
20	}
21	
22	
23	class ContentService:
24	    def __init__(self, session: AsyncSession):
25	        self.session = session
26	
27	    async def create(self, data: ContentCreate) -> ContentItem:
28	        item = ContentItem(
29	            title=data.title,
30	            body=data.body,
31	            content_type=data.content_type,
32	            language=data.language,
33	            channels=json.dumps(data.channels),
34	            ai_model_used=data.ai_model_used,
35	        )
36	        self.session.add(item)
37	        await self.session.commit()
38	        await self.session.refresh(item)
39	        return item
40	
41	    async def get(self, content_id: str) -> Optional[ContentItem]:
42	        return await self.session.get(ContentItem, content_id)
43	
44	    async def list(self, params: ContentListParams) -> tuple[list[ContentItem], int]:
45	        statement = select(ContentItem)
46	
47	        if params.status is not None:
48	            statement = statement.where(ContentItem.status == params.status)
49	        if params.content_type is not None:
50	            statement = statement.where(ContentItem.content_type == params.content_type)
51	        if params.channel is not None:
52	            statement = statement.where(ContentItem.channels.contains(params.channel))
53	
54	        statement = statement.order_by(ContentItem.updated_at.desc())
55	
56	        result = await self.session.exec(statement)
57	        items = list(result.all())
58	        return items, len(items)
59	
60	    async def update(self, content_id: str, data: ContentUpdate) -> Optional[ContentItem]:
61	        item = await self.get(content_id)
62	        if item is None:
63	            return None
64	
65	        # Create version snapshot before updating
66	        await self._create_version_snapshot(item)
67	
68	        if data.title is not None:
69	            item.title = data.title
70	        if data.body is not None:
71	            item.body = data.body
72	        if data.channels is not None:
73	            item.channels = json.dumps(data.channels)
74	        if data.ai_model_used is not None:
75	            item.ai_model_used = data.ai_model_used
76	
77	        item.version += 1
78	        item.updated_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
79	
80	        self.session.add(item)
81	        await self.session.commit()
82	        await self.session.refresh(item)
83	        return item
84	
85	    async def delete(self, content_id: str) -> bool:
86	        item = await self.get(content_id)
87	        if item is None:
88	            return False
89	        await self.session.delete(item)
90	        await self.session.commit()
91	        return True
92	
93	    async def transition_status(
94	        self, content_id: str, new_status: ContentStatus
95	    ) -> ContentItem:
96	        item = await self.get(content_id)
97	        if item is None:
98	            raise ValueError(f"Content item '{content_id}' not found")
99	
100	        allowed = VALID_TRANSITIONS.get(item.status, [])
101	        if new_status not in allowed:
102	            raise ValueError(
103	                f"Invalid status transition from '{item.status.value}' to '{new_status.value}'. "
104	                f"Allowed transitions: {[s.value for s in allowed]}"
105	            )
106	
107	        item.status = new_status
108	        item.updated_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
109	
110	        self.session.add(item)
111	        await self.session.commit()
112	        await self.session.refresh(item)
113	        return item
114	
115	    async def get_versions(self, content_id: str) -> list[ContentVersion]:
116	        statement = (
117	            select(ContentVersion)
118	            .where(ContentVersion.content_id == content_id)
119	            .order_by(ContentVersion.version_number.desc())
120	        )
121	        result = await self.session.exec(statement)
122	        return list(result.all())
123	
124	    async def _create_version_snapshot(self, item: ContentItem) -> ContentVersion:
125	        version = ContentVersion(
126	            content_id=item.id,
127	            version_number=item.version,
128	            title=item.title,
129	            body=item.body,
130	            ai_model_used=item.ai_model_used,
131	        )
132	        self.session.add(version)
133	        return version
134

1	from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession, create_async_engine
2	from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker
3	from sqlmodel import SQLModel
4	
5	from app.core.config import get_settings
6	
7	settings = get_settings()
8	
9	engine = create_async_engine(
10	    settings.database_url,
11	    echo=settings.debug,
12	    future=True,
13	)
14	
15	async_session_factory = sessionmaker(
16	    engine,
17	    class_=AsyncSession,
18	    expire_on_commit=False,
19	)
20	
21	
22	async def init_db() -> None:
23	    async with engine.begin() as conn:
24	        await conn.run_sync(SQLModel.metadata.create_all)
25	
26	
27	async def get_session() -> AsyncSession:
28	    async with async_session_factory() as session:
29	        yield session

- **`backend/app/api/validation.py`** — Stub router with validate and results endpoints
- **`backend/app/api/schedule.py`** — Stub router with list, schedule, and calendar export endpoints
- **`backend/app/api/seo.py`** — Stub router with get, update, and analyze endpoints
- **`backend/app/api/dashboard.py`** — Stub router with summary and recent activity endpoints
- **`backend/app/main.py`** — FastAPI app with CORS middleware, lifespan for DB init, all 6 routers registered

1	# Specification: Verbum — Catholic Content Management Platform
2	
3	## Overview
4	
5	Verbum is a greenfield, single-user Catholic content management platform for Ramir, a Portuguese-speaking Catholic content creator. The platform centralizes the full content lifecycle — from AI-assisted generation (via Anthropic Claude and OpenAI ChatGPT) through theological validation (via NotebookLM), editorial scheduling, and multi-channel delivery. It supports three content types: short-form video scripts (1 min), long-form video scripts (10 min), and SEO-optimized blog posts in Portuguese. The goal is to reduce time-to-publish by 50%, ensure 100% doctrinal accuracy through automated validation, and maintain a consistent publishing cadence of at least 3 pieces per week. The entire system is designed for a single user — no multi-tenancy, no scaling concerns, no monetization.
6	
7	## Workflow Type
8	
9	**Type**: feature
10	
11	**Rationale**: This is a brand-new full-stack application being built from scratch within an empty repository. There is no existing application logic to refactor or migrate — only a Vite/React/Tailwind prototype shell (`prototypes-magic/`) with reusable UI components. Every layer (database schema, backend API, service integrations, frontend pages) must be designed and implemented.
12	
13	## Task Scope
14	
15	### Services Involved
16	- **verbum-backend** (primary) — Python FastAPI backend handling API routing, AI integration orchestration, NotebookLM validation, database persistence, and scheduling logic
17	- **verbum-frontend** (primary) — React TypeScript SPA providing the dashboard, content editor, validation panel, editorial calendar, and SEO tools
18	- **SQLite database** (infrastructure) — Embedded file-based database for content items, validation records, scheduling metadata, and version history
19	
20	### This Task Will:
21	- [ ] Set up the full project structure (backend + frontend monorepo)
22	- [ ] Implement the Python FastAPI backend with async architecture
23	- [ ] Design and create the SQLite database schema via SQLModel
24	- [ ] Integrate Anthropic Claude API for AI content generation with streaming
25	- [ ] Integrate OpenAI ChatGPT API as an alternate drafting engine with streaming
26	- [ ] Integrate NotebookLM (via `notebooklm-py`) for theological validation with citation retrieval
27	- [ ] Build a validation abstraction layer to enable graceful degradation and provider swapping
28	- [ ] Create the React frontend with dashboard, content editor, validation panel, and calendar views
29	- [ ] Implement the full content lifecycle: draft → validated → scheduled → published
30	- [ ] Add Portuguese SEO metadata tools for blog post optimization
31	- [ ] Implement content versioning and validation history tracking
32	- [ ] Add iCal export for editorial calendar reminders
33	- [ ] Implement environment-based configuration with secure API key management
34	
35	### Out of Scope:
36	- Custom Catholic knowledge base or RAG pipeline (NotebookLM handles this)
37	- Social media analytics or community management
38	- Monetization or revenue features
39	- Multi-user authentication or multi-tenancy
40	- Direct Google Calendar API integration (using .ics export instead)
41	- Bulk social media publishing automation
42	- Mobile native app (responsive web only)
43	- Docker containerization (single-user local/hosted deployment)
44	
45	## Service Context
46	
47	### verbum-backend
48	
49	**Tech Stack:**
50	- Language: Python 3.11+
51	- Framework: FastAPI >= 0.135.0 (with `[standard]` extras for uvicorn)
52	- ORM: SQLModel >= 0.0.37 (Pydantic + SQLAlchemy combined)
53	- Database: SQLite (via aiosqlite for async operations)
54	- AI SDKs: `anthropic >= 0.88.0`, `openai >= 2.30.0`
55	- Validation: `notebooklm-py[browser] >= 0.3.4` (UNOFFICIAL — HIGH RISK)
56	- Calendar: `icalendar` for .ics export
57	- Config: `python-dotenv` for environment variable management
58	- Key directories: `backend/app/`, `backend/app/api/`, `backend/app/models/`, `backend/app/services/`, `backend/app/core/`
59	
60	**Entry Point:** `backend/app/main.py`
61	
62	**How to Run:**
63	```bash
64	cd backend
65	pip install -r requirements.txt
66	uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8000
67	```
68	
69	**Port:** 8000
70	
71	---
72	
73	### verbum-frontend
74	
75	**Tech Stack:**
76	- Language: TypeScript 5.5+
77	- Framework: React 18.3
78	- Bundler: Vite 5.2
79	- Styling: Tailwind CSS 3.4
80	- Icons: lucide-react 0.522
81	- UI Components: Reuse from `prototypes-magic/src/components/ui/` (Button, Input, Select, Card, Tabs, Badge, Table, Textarea, Progress, etc.)
82	- Charts: recharts (already in prototype dependencies)
83	- Key directories: `frontend/src/`, `frontend/src/components/`, `frontend/src/pages/`, `frontend/src/services/`, `frontend/src/hooks/`
84	
85	**Entry Point:** `frontend/src/main.tsx`
86	
87	**How to Run:**
88	```bash
89	cd frontend
90	npm install
91	npm run dev
92	```
93	
94	**Port:** 5173 (Vite default)
95	
96	## Files to Create

1	# Specification: Verbum — Catholic Content Management Platform
2	
3	## Overview
4	
5	Verbum is a greenfield, single-user Catholic content management platform for Ramir, a Portuguese-speaking Catholic content creator. The platform centralizes the full content lifecycle — from AI-assisted generation (via Anthropic Claude and OpenAI ChatGPT) through theological validation (via NotebookLM), editorial scheduling, and multi-channel delivery. It supports three content types: short-form video scripts (1 min), long-form video scripts (10 min), and SEO-optimized blog posts in Portuguese. The goal is to reduce time-to-publish by 50%, ensure 100% doctrinal accuracy through automated validation, and maintain a consistent publishing cadence of at least 3 pieces per week. The entire system is designed for a single user — no multi-tenancy, no scaling concerns, no monetization.
6	
7	## Workflow Type
8	
9	**Type**: feature
10	
11	**Rationale**: This is a brand-new full-stack application being built from scratch within an empty repository. There is no existing application logic to refactor or migrate — only a Vite/React/Tailwind prototype shell (`prototypes-magic/`) with reusable UI components. Every layer (database schema, backend API, service integrations, frontend pages) must be designed and implemented.
12	
13	## Task Scope
14	
15	### Services Involved
16	- **verbum-backend** (primary) — Python FastAPI backend handling API routing, AI integration orchestration, NotebookLM validation, database persistence, and scheduling logic
17	- **verbum-frontend** (primary) — React TypeScript SPA providing the dashboard, content editor, validation panel, editorial calendar, and SEO tools
18	- **SQLite database** (infrastructure) — Embedded file-based database for content items, validation records, scheduling metadata, and version history
19	
20	### This Task Will:
21	- [ ] Set up the full project structure (backend + frontend monorepo)
22	- [ ] Implement the Python FastAPI backend with async architecture
23	- [ ] Design and create the SQLite database schema via SQLModel
24	- [ ] Integrate Anthropic Claude API for AI content generation with streaming
25	- [ ] Integrate OpenAI ChatGPT API as an alternate drafting engine with streaming
26	- [ ] Integrate NotebookLM (via `notebooklm-py`) for theological validation with citation retrieval
27	- [ ] Build a validation abstraction layer to enable graceful degradation and provider swapping
28	- [ ] Create the React frontend with dashboard, content editor, validation panel, and calendar views
29	- [ ] Implement the full content lifecycle: draft → validated → scheduled → published
30	- [ ] Add Portuguese SEO metadata tools for blog post optimization
31	- [ ] Implement content versioning and validation history tracking
32	- [ ] Add iCal export for editorial calendar reminders
33	- [ ] Implement environment-based configuration with secure API key management
34	
35	### Out of Scope:
36	- Custom Catholic knowledge base or RAG pipeline (NotebookLM handles this)
37	- Social media analytics or community management
38	- Monetization or revenue features
39	- Multi-user authentication or multi-tenancy
40	- Direct Google Calendar API integration (using .ics export instead)
41	- Bulk social media publishing automation
42	- Mobile native app (responsive web only)
43	- Docker containerization (single-user local/hosted deployment)
44	
45	## Service Context
46	
47	### verbum-backend
48	
49	**Tech Stack:**
50	- Language: Python 3.11+
51	- Framework: FastAPI >= 0.135.0 (with `[standard]` extras for uvicorn)
52	- ORM: SQLModel >= 0.0.37 (Pydantic + SQLAlchemy combined)
53	- Database: SQLite (via aiosqlite for async operations)
54	- AI SDKs: `anthropic >= 0.88.0`, `openai >= 2.30.0`
55	- Validation: `notebooklm-py[browser] >= 0.3.4` (UNOFFICIAL — HIGH RISK)
56	- Calendar: `icalendar` for .ics export
57	- Config: `python-dotenv` for environment variable management
58	- Key directories: `backend/app/`, `backend/app/api/`, `backend/app/models/`, `backend/app/services/`, `backend/app/core/`
59	
60	**Entry Point:** `backend/app/main.py`
61	
62	**How to Run:**
63	```bash
64	cd backend
65	pip install -r requirements.txt
66	uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8000
67	```
68	
69	**Port:** 8000
70	
71	---
72	
73	### verbum-frontend
74	
75	**Tech Stack:**
76	- Language: TypeScript 5.5+
77	- Framework: React 18.3
78	- Bundler: Vite 5.2
79	- Styling: Tailwind CSS 3.4
80	- Icons: lucide-react 0.522
81	- UI Components: Reuse from `prototypes-magic/src/components/ui/` (Button, Input, Select, Card, Tabs, Badge, Table, Textarea, Progress, etc.)
82	- Charts: recharts (already in prototype dependencies)
83	- Key directories: `frontend/src/`, `frontend/src/components/`, `frontend/src/pages/`, `frontend/src/services/`, `frontend/src/hooks/`
84	
85	**Entry Point:** `frontend/src/main.tsx`
86	
87	**How to Run:**
88	```bash
89	cd frontend
90	npm install
91	npm run dev
92	```
93	
94	**Port:** 5173 (Vite default)
95	
96	## Files to Create
97

196	| `prototypes-magic/src/components/ui/tabs/Tabs.tsx` | Tabs component — reuse for editor/validation/SEO tab switching |
197	| `prototypes-magic/src/components/ui/badge/Badge.tsx` | Badge component — adapt for status badges |

200	| `prototypes-magic/src/components/ui/progress/Progress.tsx` | Progress bar — reuse for validation score display |
201	| `prototypes-magic/tailwind.config.js` | Tailwind theme configuration — extend for Verbum's color palette |
202	| `prototypes-magic/vite.config.ts` | Vite configuration — use as base, add API proxy |
203	| `prototypes-magic/package.json` | Dependency baseline — reuse core deps, add routing + calendar libs |
204	
205	## Patterns to Follow
206	
207	### Pattern 1: Async FastAPI with SQLModel
208	
209	Industry best practice for Python async web applications:
210	
211	```python
212	# backend/app/main.py
213	from contextlib import asynccontextmanager
214	from fastapi import FastAPI
215	from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware
216	from app.core.database import init_db
217	from app.api import content, generation, validation, schedule, seo, dashboard
218	
219	@asynccontextmanager
220	async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):
221	    await init_db()
222	    yield
223	
224	app = FastAPI(title="Verbum API", lifespan=lifespan)
225	app.add_middleware(
226	    CORSMiddleware,
227	    allow_origins=["http://localhost:5173"],
228	    allow_credentials=True,
229	    allow_methods=["*"],
230	    allow_headers=["*"],
231	)
232	app.include_router(content.router, prefix="/api/content", tags=["content"])
233	app.include_router(generation.router, prefix="/api/generation", tags=["generation"])
234	app.include_router(validation.router, prefix="/api/validation", tags=["validation"])
235	app.include_router(schedule.router, prefix="/api/schedule", tags=["schedule"])
236	app.include_router(seo.router, prefix="/api/seo", tags=["seo"])
237	app.include_router(dashboard.router, prefix="/api/dashboard", tags=["dashboard"])
238	```
239	
240	**Key Points:**
241	- Use `lifespan` context manager for database initialization (not deprecated `@app.on_event`)
242	- Configure CORS for frontend-backend communication
243	- Organize endpoints into routers with prefixes and tags
244	- All handlers should be `async def` for non-blocking I/O
245

entries.detail:
1	# Specification: Verbum — Catholic Content Management Platform
2	
3	## Overview
4	
5	Verbum is a greenfield, single-user Catholic content management platform for Ramir, a Portuguese-speaking Catholic content creator. The platform centralizes the full content lifecycle — from AI-assisted generation (via Anthropic Claude and OpenAI ChatGPT) through theological validation (via NotebookLM), editorial scheduling, and multi-channel delivery. It supports three content types: short-form video scripts (1 min), long-form video scripts (10 min), and SEO-optimized blog posts in Portuguese. The goal is to reduce time-to-publish by 50%, ensure 100% doctrinal accuracy through automated validation, and maintain a consistent publishing cadence of at least 3 pieces per week. The entire system is designed for a single user — no multi-tenancy, no scaling concerns, no monetization.
6	
7	## Workflow Type
8	
9	**Type**: feature
10	
11	**Rationale**: This is a brand-new full-stack application being built from scratch within an empty repository. There is no existing application logic to refactor or migrate — only a Vite/React/Tailwind prototype shell (`prototypes-magic/`) with reusable UI components. Every layer (database schema, backend API, service integrations, frontend pages) must be designed and implemented.
12	
13	## Task Scope
14	
15	### Services Involved
16	- **verbum-backend** (primary) — Python FastAPI backend handling API routing, AI integration orchestration, NotebookLM validation, database persistence, and scheduling logic
17	- **verbum-frontend** (primary) — React TypeScript SPA providing the dashboard, content editor, validation panel, editorial calendar, and SEO tools
18	- **SQLite database** (infrastructure) — Embedded file-based database for content items, validation records, scheduling metadata, and version history
19	
20	### This Task Will:
21	- [ ] Set up the full project structure (backend + frontend monorepo)
22	- [ ] Implement the Python FastAPI backend with async architecture
23	- [ ] Design and create the SQLite database schema via SQLModel
24	- [ ] Integrate Anthropic Claude API for AI content generation with streaming
25	- [ ] Integrate OpenAI ChatGPT API as an alternate drafting engine with streaming
26	- [ ] Integrate NotebookLM (via `notebooklm-py`) for theological validation with citation retrieval
27	- [ ] Build a validation abstraction layer to enable graceful degradation and provider swapping
28	- [ ] Create the React frontend with dashboard, content editor, validation panel, and calendar views
29	- [ ] Implement the full content lifecycle: draft → validated → scheduled → published
30	- [ ] Add Portuguese SEO metadata tools for blog post optimization
31	- [ ] Implement content versioning and validation history tracking
32	- [ ] Add iCal export for editorial calendar reminders
33	- [ ] Implement environment-based configuration with secure API key management
34	
35	### Out of Scope:
36	- Custom Catholic knowledge base or RAG pipeline (NotebookLM handles this)
37	- Social media analytics or community management
38	- Monetization or revenue features
39	- Multi-user authentication or multi-tenancy
40	- Direct Google Calendar API integration (using .ics export instead)
41	- Bulk social media publishing automation
42	- Mobile native app (responsive web only)
43	- Docker containerization (single-user local/hosted deployment)
44	
45	## Service Context
46	
47	### verbum-backend
48	
49	**Tech Stack:**
50	- Language: Python 3.11+
51	- Framework: FastAPI >= 0.135.0 (with `[standard]` extras for uvicorn)
52	- ORM: SQLModel >= 0.0.37 (Pydantic + SQLAlchemy combined)
53	- Database: SQLite (via aiosqlite for async operations)
54	- AI SDKs: `anthropic >= 0.88.0`, `openai >= 2.30.0`
55	- Validation: `notebooklm-py[browser] >= 0.3.4` (UNOFFICIAL — HIGH RISK)
56	- Calendar: `icalendar` for .ics export
57	- Config: `python-dotenv` for environment variable management
58	- Key directories: `backend/app/`, `backend/app/api/`, `backend/app/models/`, `backend/app/services/`, `backend/app/core/`
59	
60	**Entry Point:** `backend/app/main.py`
61	
62	**How to Run:**
63	```bash
64	cd backend
65	pip install -r requirements.txt
66	uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8000
67	```
68	
69	**Port:** 8000
70	
71	---
72	
73	### verbum-frontend
74	
75	**Tech Stack:**
76	- Language: TypeScript 5.5+
77	- Framework: React 18.3
78	- Bundler: Vite 5.2
79	- Styling: Tailwind CSS 3.4
80	- Icons: lucide-react 0.522
81	- UI Components: Reuse from `prototypes-magic/src/components/ui/` (Button, Input, Select, Card, Tabs, Badge, Table, Textarea, Progress, etc.)
82	- Charts: recharts (already in prototype dependencies)
83	- Key directories: `frontend/src/`, `frontend/src/components/`, `frontend/src/pages/`, `frontend/src/services/`, `frontend/src/hooks/`
84	
85	**Entry Point:** `frontend/src/main.tsx`
86	
87	**How to Run:**
88	```bash
89	cd frontend
90	npm install
91	npm run dev
92	```
93	
94	**Port:** 5173 (Vite default)
95	
96	## Files to Create
97	
98	### Backend Structure
99	
100	| File | Service | Purpose |
101	|------|---------|---------|
102	| `backend/requirements.txt` | backend | Python dependencies |
103	| `backend/app/__init__.py` | backend | Package init |
104	| `backend/app/main.py` | backend | FastAPI app entry point, middleware, CORS, lifespan |
105	| `backend/app/core/__init__.py` | backend | Core package init |
106	| `backend/app/core/config.py` | backend | Settings class with env var loading (Pydantic BaseSettings) |
107	| `backend/app/core/database.py` | backend | Async SQLite engine, session factory, DB initialization |
108	| `backend/app/core/deps.py` | backend | FastAPI dependency injection (DB sessions, service instances) |
109	| `backend/app/models/__init__.py` | backend | Models package init |
110	| `backend/app/models/content.py` | backend | Content SQLModel (type, status, language, channel, versions) |
111	| `backend/app/models/validation.py` | backend | ValidationRecord SQLModel (score, flags, citations, history) |
112	| `backend/app/models/schedule.py` | backend | Schedule SQLModel (date, channel, status, reminders) |
113	| `backend/app/schemas/__init__.py` | backend | Schemas package init |
114	| `backend/app/schemas/content.py` | backend | Pydantic request/response schemas for content endpoints |
115	| `backend/app/schemas/validation.py` | backend | Pydantic schemas for validation responses |
116	| `backend/app/schemas/generation.py` | backend | Pydantic schemas for AI generation requests/responses |
117	| `backend/app/services/__init__.py` | backend | Services package init |
118	| `backend/app/services/ai_service.py` | backend | Abstraction layer for Claude + ChatGPT with streaming |
119	| `backend/app/services/claude_provider.py` | backend | Anthropic Claude API implementation |
120	| `backend/app/services/openai_provider.py` | backend | OpenAI ChatGPT API implementation |
121	| `backend/app/services/validation_service.py` | backend | Validation abstraction layer (interface/protocol) |
122	| `backend/app/services/notebooklm_provider.py` | backend | NotebookLM implementation with retry + error handling |
123	| `backend/app/services/fallback_validation_provider.py` | backend | AI-based fallback validation if NotebookLM is down |
124	| `backend/app/services/content_service.py` | backend | Content CRUD, versioning, status transitions |
125	| `backend/app/services/schedule_service.py` | backend | Scheduling logic, calendar export |
126	| `backend/app/services/seo_service.py` | backend | Portuguese SEO metadata, readability, keyword suggestions |
127	| `backend/app/api/__init__.py` | backend | API package init |
128	| `backend/app/api/content.py` | backend | Content CRUD endpoints |
129	| `backend/app/api/generation.py` | backend | AI generation endpoints (with SSE streaming) |
130	| `backend/app/api/validation.py` | backend | Theological validation endpoints |
131	| `backend/app/api/schedule.py` | backend | Scheduling and calendar endpoints |
132	| `backend/app/api/seo.py` | backend | SEO analysis endpoints |
133	| `backend/app/api/dashboard.py` | backend | Dashboard aggregation endpoints |
134	| `backend/.env.example` | backend | Environment variable template |
135	| `backend/tests/__init__.py` | backend | Tests package init |
136	| `backend/tests/conftest.py` | backend | Pytest fixtures (test DB, mock services) |
137	| `backend/tests/test_content.py` | backend | Content CRUD tests |
138	| `backend/tests/test_generation.py` | backend | AI generation tests (mocked) |
139	| `backend/tests/test_validation.py` | backend | Validation service tests (mocked) |
140	| `backend/tests/test_schedule.py` | backend | Schedule and calendar export tests |
141	| `backend/tests/test_seo.py` | backend | SEO service tests (readability, keywords, metadata) |
142	
143	### Frontend Structure
144	
145	| File | Service | Purpose |
146	|------|---------|---------|
147	| `frontend/package.json` | frontend | Node dependencies and scripts |
148	| `frontend/tsconfig.json` | frontend | TypeScript configuration |
149	| `frontend/tsconfig.node.json` | frontend | TypeScript config for Vite |
150	| `frontend/vite.config.ts` | frontend | Vite configuration with proxy to backend |
151	| `frontend/tailwind.config.js` | frontend | Tailwind CSS configuration |

153	| `frontend/index.html` | frontend | HTML entry point |

158	| `frontend/src/services/api.ts` | frontend | API client (fetch wrapper with error handling) |
159	| `frontend/src/services/streaming.ts` | frontend | SSE streaming client for AI generation |

161	| `frontend/src/hooks/useGeneration.ts` | frontend | AI generation hook with streaming state |
162	| `frontend/src/hooks/useValidation.ts` | frontend | Validation trigger + result hook |

200	| `prototypes-magic/src/components/ui/progress/Progress.tsx` | Progress bar — reuse for validation score display |
201	| `prototypes-magic/tailwind.config.js` | Tailwind theme configuration — extend for Verbum's color palette |

203	| `prototypes-magic/package.json` | Dependency baseline — reuse core deps, add routing + calendar libs |
204	
205	## Patterns to Follow
206	
207	### Pattern 1: Async FastAPI with SQLModel
208	
209	Industry best practice for Python async web applications:
210	
211	```python
212	# backend/app/main.py
213	from contextlib import asynccontextmanager
214	from fastapi import FastAPI
215	from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware
216	from app.core.database import init_db
217	from app.api import content, generation, validation, schedule, seo, dashboard
218	
219	@asynccontextmanager
220	async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):
221	    await init_db()
222	    yield
223	
224	app = FastAPI(title="Verbum API", lifespan=lifespan)
225	app.add_middleware(
226	    CORSMiddleware,
227	    allow_origins=["http://localhost:5173"],
228	    allow_credentials=True,
229	    allow_methods=["*"],
230	    allow_headers=["*"],
231	)
232	app.include_router(content.router, prefix="/api/content", tags=["content"])
233	app.include_router(generation.router, prefix="/api/generation", tags=["generation"])
234	app.include_router(validation.router, prefix="/api/validation", tags=["validation"])
235	app.include_router(schedule.router, prefix="/api/schedule", tags=["schedule"])
236	app.include_router(seo.router, prefix="/api/seo", tags=["seo"])
237	app.include_router(dashboard.router, prefix="/api/dashboard", tags=["dashboard"])
238	```
239	
240	**Key Points:**
241	- Use `lifespan` context manager for database initialization (not deprecated `@app.on_event`)
242	- Configure CORS for frontend-backend communication
243	- Organize endpoints into routers with prefixes and tags
244	- All handlers should be `async def` for non-blocking I/O
245	
246	### Pattern 2: SQLModel Data Models with Status Enum
247	
248	```python
249	# backend/app/models/content.py
250	from enum import Enum
251	from datetime import datetime, timezone
252	from typing import Optional
253	from sqlmodel import SQLModel, Field
254	from uuid import uuid4
255	
256	class ContentType(str, Enum):
257	    SHORT_VIDEO = "short_video"
258	    LONG_VIDEO = "long_video"
259	    BLOG_POST = "blog_post"
260	
261	class ContentStatus(str, Enum):
262	    DRAFT = "draft"
263	    VALIDATED = "validated"
264	    SCHEDULED = "scheduled"
265	    PUBLISHED = "published"
266	
267	class ContentItem(SQLModel, table=True):
268	    __tablename__ = "content_items"
269	
270	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid4()), primary_key=True)
271	    title: str
272	    body: str = ""
273	    content_type: ContentType
274	    status: ContentStatus = ContentStatus.DRAFT
275	    language: str = "pt"
276	    channels: str = "[]"  # JSON list of channel strings, e.g. '["youtube", "blog"]'
277	    ai_model_used: Optional[str] = None
278	    scheduled_date: Optional[datetime] = None
279	    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
280	    updated_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
281	    version: int = 1
282	```
283	
284	**Key Points:**
285	- Use string enums for readable database values
286	- UUID primary keys for content items
287	- Default to Portuguese language (`pt`)
288	- Track which AI model generated the draft
289	- Version counter for content versioning
290	
291	### Pattern 3: AI Service Abstraction with Streaming
292	
293	```python
294	# backend/app/services/ai_service.py
295	from typing import Protocol, AsyncIterator
296	
297	class AIProvider(Protocol):
298	    async def generate(self, prompt: str, system_prompt: str, max_tokens: int) -> str: ...
299	    async def generate_stream(self, prompt: str, system_prompt: str, max_tokens: int) -> AsyncIterator[str]: ...
300	
301	class AIService:
302	    def __init__(self, providers: dict[str, AIProvider]):
303	        self._providers = providers
304	
305	    async def generate(self, model_key: str, prompt: str, system_prompt: str, max_tokens: int = 2048) -> str:
306	        provider = self._providers[model_key]
307	        return await provider.generate(prompt, system_prompt, max_tokens)
308	
309	    async def generate_stream(self, model_key: str, prompt: str, system_prompt: str, max_tokens: int = 2048) -> AsyncIterator[str]:
310	        provider = self._providers[model_key]
311	        async for chunk in provider.generate_stream(prompt, system_prompt, max_tokens):
312	            yield chunk
313	```
314	
315	**Key Points:**
316	- Use Python `Protocol` for interface definition (structural typing)
317	- Support both full-response and streaming generation
318	- Provider pattern allows easy addition of new AI models
319	- The `model_key` selects between Claude and ChatGPT
320	
321	### Pattern 4: Validation Service with Fallback
322	
323	```python
324	# backend/app/services/validation_service.py
325	from typing import Protocol
326	from dataclasses import dataclass
327	
328	@dataclass
329	class Citation:
330	    citation_number: int
331	    source_id: str
332	    cited_text: str
333	
334	@dataclass
335	class ValidationResult:
336	    score: float  # 0.0 to 1.0
337	    flags: list[str]
338	    citations: list[Citation]
339	    provider: str  # "notebooklm" or "ai_fallback"
340	    raw_answer: str
341	
342	class ValidationProvider(Protocol):
343	    async def validate(self, content: str, content_type: str) -> ValidationResult: ...
344	    async def is_available(self) -> bool: ...
345	
346	class ValidationService:
347	    def __init__(self, primary: ValidationProvider, fallback: ValidationProvider):
348	        self._primary = primary
349	        self._fallback = fallback

340 raw_answer: str 341 342 class ValidationProvider(Protocol): 343 async def validate(self, content: str, content_type: str) -> ValidationResult: ... 344 async def is_available(self) -> bool: ... 345 346 class ValidationService: 347 def __init__(self, primary: ValidationProvider, fallback: ValidationProvider): 348 self._primary = primary 349 self._fallback = fallback 350 351 async def validate(self, content: str, content_type: str) -> ValidationResult: 352 if await self._primary.is_available(): 353 try: 354 return await self._primary.validate(content, content_type) 355 except Exception: 356 pass 357 return await self._fallback.validate(content, content_type) 358 ``` 359 360 **Key Points:** 361 - Abstraction layer critical for NotebookLM reliability concerns 362 - `is_available()` check before attempting validation 363 - Automatic fallback to AI-based validation if NotebookLM is down 364 - Structured `ValidationResult` with score, flags, and traceable citations 365 - The `provider` field tells the UI which backend produced the result 366 367 ### Pattern 5: SSE Streaming from FastAPI to React 368 369 ```python 370 # backend/app/api/generation.py 371 from fastapi import APIRouter 372 from fastapi.responses import StreamingResponse 373 374 router = APIRouter() 375 376 @router.post("/stream") 377 async def generate_stream(request: GenerationRequest): 378 async def event_generator(): 379 async for chunk in ai_service.generate_stream( 380 model_key=request.model, 381 prompt=request.prompt, 382 system_prompt=request.system_prompt, 383 ): 384 yield f"data: {json.dumps({'text': chunk})}\n\n" 385 yield f"data: {json.dumps({'done': True})}\n\n" 386 387 return StreamingResponse(event_generator(), media_type="text/event-stream") 388 ``` 389 390 ```typescript 391 // frontend/src/services/streaming.ts 392 export async function* streamGeneration(request: GenerationRequest): AsyncGenerator { 393 const response = await fetch('/api/generation/stream', { 394 method: 'POST', 395 headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }, 396 body: JSON.stringify(request), 397 }); 398 const reader = response.body!.getReader(); 399 const decoder = new TextDecoder(); 400 while (true) { 401 const { done, value } = await reader.read(); 402 if (done) break; 403 const text = decoder.decode(value); 404 for (const line of text.split('\n')) { 405 if (line.startsWith('data: ')) { 406 const data = JSON.parse(line.slice(6)); 407 if (data.done) return; 408 yield data.text; 409 } 410 } 411 } 412 } 413 ``` 414 415 **Key Points:** 416 - FastAPI `StreamingResponse` with SSE format (`data: ...\n\n`) 417 - React consumes via `ReadableStream` reader 418 - JSON-encoded chunks allow metadata alongside text 419 - Terminal `done` event signals completion 420 - Dramatically improves perceived performance for content generation 421 422 ### Pattern 6: React Page with Editor + Right Panel Layout 423 424 ```tsx 425 // frontend/src/pages/EditorPage.tsx 426 export function EditorPage() { 427 return ( 428
429 {/* Main Editor - Single Column */} 430
431 432 433 434
435 {/* Validation Panel - Fixed Right */} 436
437 438
439
440 ); 441 } 442 ``` 443 444 **Key Points:** 445 - Flexbox layout with fixed-width right panel (per UX requirements) 446 - Editor takes remaining space (`flex-1`) 447 - Validation panel is always visible alongside editor 448 - Both panels independently scrollable 449 450 ## Requirements 451 452 ### Functional Requirements 453 454 1. **AI Content Generation (High Priority)** 455 - Description: Connect to Anthropic Claude and OpenAI ChatGPT APIs to generate content drafts from user prompts. Support three content type templates: short-form video (1 min), long-form video (10 min), and SEO blog post. Stream AI responses in real-time to the editor. 456 - Acceptance: User can select an AI model (Claude or ChatGPT), enter a prompt, and see the generated draft stream into the editor in under 5 seconds. Content type template structures the system prompt appropriately. 457 458 2. **Content Type Templates (High Priority)** 459 - Description: Pre-configured templates that structure AI prompts and editor layouts for each content type. Short video templates target 150-200 words; long video templates target 1500-2000 words; blog post templates include SEO fields. 460 - Acceptance: Each content type produces appropriately structured output with correct length targets and format (script format for videos, article format for blogs). 461 462 3. **Theological Validation via NotebookLM (High Priority)** 463 - Description: One-click validation sends content to NotebookLM for doctrinal review against loaded Catholic sources. Returns a validation score (0-100%), flag list for issues, and source citations from the Catholic corpus (Catechism, Vatican II, etc.). 464 - Acceptance: Validation completes in under 5 seconds (target; NotebookLM response times are unverified — see Performance Verification), displays score + flags + citations in the right panel. If NotebookLM is unavailable, falls back to AI-based validation with a clear indicator. 465 466 4. **Source Citation Display (High Priority)** 467 - Description: Display traceable citations from NotebookLM validation, showing the specific text passages from Catholic sources that support or contradict the content. 468 - Acceptance: Each citation shows the citation number, source name, and exact quoted text. Citations are expandable/collapsible. The user can see which parts of their content are supported by which sources. 469 470 5. **Content Lifecycle Status Tracking (Medium Priority)** 471 - Description: Each content item moves through a linear status pipeline: draft → validated → scheduled → published. Status transitions are tracked with timestamps. Dashboard shows counts per status. 472 - Acceptance: Status is visible on all content views (list, editor, calendar). Only valid transitions are allowed (e.g., cannot schedule without validation). Dashboard shows accurate pipeline counts. 473 474 6. **Editorial Calendar (Medium Priority)** 475 - Description: Visual week/month calendar view showing scheduled content items. Click to schedule, reschedule, or view content details. Supports filtering by content type and channel. 476 - Acceptance: Calendar displays all scheduled items on their publication dates. User can drag/click to schedule items. Week and month views both work. Items show content type icon, title, and channel. 477 478 7. **Portuguese SEO Metadata (Medium Priority)** 479 - Description: For blog posts, provide fields for Portuguese meta title, meta description, keywords, and slug. Include automated readability assessment and AI-powered keyword suggestions in Portuguese. 480 - Acceptance: SEO panel shows all metadata fields when editing a blog post. Readability score is calculated automatically. Keyword suggestions can be triggered via AI. 481 482 8. **Channel Management (Low Priority)** 483 - Description: Tag content with delivery channels (YouTube, Blog, other). Each content item can be assigned to one or more channels. Channels appear on calendar and list views. 484 - Acceptance: User can tag content with channels, filter by channel, and see channel assignments on calendar. 485 486 9. **iCal Export (Low Priority)** 487 - Description: Export the editorial calendar as an .ics file compatible with Google Calendar, Apple Calendar, and Outlook. 488 - Acceptance: Exported .ics file contains all scheduled items with correct dates, titles, and channel info. File imports successfully into Google Calendar. 489

530	- Don't build multi-user auth — this is a single-user personal tool
531	- Don't use Playwright/Chromium in production for NotebookLM auth — use `NOTEBOOKLM_AUTH_JSON` env var with pre-captured tokens
532	- Don't block the event loop with `time.sleep()` or synchronous HTTP calls — use `asyncio.sleep()` and async clients
533	
534	## Data Model
535	
536	### Core Tables
537	
538	```
539	content_items
540	├── id: UUID (PK)
541	├── title: str
542	├── body: text
543	├── content_type: enum (short_video | long_video | blog_post)
544	├── status: enum (draft | validated | scheduled | published)
545	├── language: str (default: "pt")
546	├── channels: JSON (list of strings, default: []) — e.g. ["youtube", "blog"]
547	├── ai_model_used: str (nullable) — "claude" or "chatgpt"
548	├── prompt_used: text (nullable) — the user's original prompt
549	├── scheduled_date: datetime (nullable)
550	├── published_date: datetime (nullable)
551	├── version: int (default: 1)
552	├── created_at: datetime
553	└── updated_at: datetime
554	
555	content_versions
556	├── id: UUID (PK)
557	├── content_id: UUID (FK → content_items.id)
558	├── version_number: int
559	├── title: str
560	├── body: text
561	├── created_at: datetime
562	└── snapshot_reason: str — "manual_save", "pre_validation", "ai_generation"
563	
564	validation_records
565	├── id: UUID (PK)
566	├── content_id: UUID (FK → content_items.id)
567	├── content_version: int — which version was validated
568	├── score: float (0.0 to 1.0)
569	├── flags: JSON (list of flag strings)
570	├── citations: JSON (list of citation objects)
571	├── provider: str — "notebooklm" or "ai_fallback"
572	├── raw_response: text
573	├── created_at: datetime
574	└── duration_ms: int — validation response time
575	
576	seo_metadata
577	├── id: UUID (PK)
578	├── content_id: UUID (FK → content_items.id, unique)
579	├── meta_title: str
580	├── meta_description: text
581	├── keywords: JSON (list of strings)
582	├── slug: str
583	├── readability_score: float (nullable)
584	└── updated_at: datetime
585	```
586	
587	### Relationships
588	- `content_items` 1:N `content_versions` — full version history
589	- `content_items` 1:N `validation_records` — multiple validations over time
590	- `content_items` 1:1 `seo_metadata` — one SEO record per content item (blog posts)
591	
592	## Development Environment
593	
594	### Prerequisites
595	
596	```bash
597	# Python 3.11+ (FastAPI >= 0.130.0 and notebooklm-py require 3.10+; we target 3.11+ for best async performance)
598	python3 --version  # must be >= 3.11
599	
600	# Node.js 18+ and npm
601	node --version
602	npm --version
603	
604	# Playwright + Chromium (only for initial NotebookLM auth setup)
605	# NOT required in production if using NOTEBOOKLM_AUTH_JSON
606	```
607	
608	### Install Dependencies
609	
610	```bash
611	# Backend
612	cd backend
613	python3 -m venv venv
614	source venv/bin/activate
615	pip install -r requirements.txt
616	
617	# Frontend
618	cd frontend
619	npm install
620	
621	# NotebookLM auth setup (one-time)
622	pip install 'notebooklm-py[browser]'
623	playwright install chromium
624	notebooklm login  # Opens browser for Google SSO
625	```
626	
627	### Start Services
628	
629	```bash

1	# Specification: Verbum — Catholic Content Management Platform
2	
3	## Overview
4	
5	Verbum is a greenfield, single-user Catholic content management platform for Ramir, a Portuguese-speaking Catholic content creator. The platform centralizes the full content lifecycle — from AI-assisted generation (via Anthropic Claude and OpenAI ChatGPT) through theological validation (via NotebookLM), editorial scheduling, and multi-channel delivery. It supports three content types: short-form video scripts (1 min), long-form video scripts (10 min), and SEO-optimized blog posts in Portuguese. The goal is to reduce time-to-publish by 50%, ensure 100% doctrinal accuracy through automated validation, and maintain a consistent publishing cadence of at least 3 pieces per week. The entire system is designed for a single user — no multi-tenancy, no scaling concerns, no monetization.
6	
7	## Workflow Type
8	
9	**Type**: feature
10	
11	**Rationale**: This is a brand-new full-stack application being built from scratch within an empty repository. There is no existing application logic to refactor or migrate — only a Vite/React/Tailwind prototype shell (`prototypes-magic/`) with reusable UI components. Every layer (database schema, backend API, service integrations, frontend pages) must be designed and implemented.
12	
13	## Task Scope
14	
15	### Services Involved
16	- **verbum-backend** (primary) — Python FastAPI backend handling API routing, AI integration orchestration, NotebookLM validation, database persistence, and scheduling logic
17	- **verbum-frontend** (primary) — React TypeScript SPA providing the dashboard, content editor, validation panel, editorial calendar, and SEO tools
18	- **SQLite database** (infrastructure) — Embedded file-based database for content items, validation records, scheduling metadata, and version history
19	
20	### This Task Will:
21	- [ ] Set up the full project structure (backend + frontend monorepo)
22	- [ ] Implement the Python FastAPI backend with async architecture
23	- [ ] Design and create the SQLite database schema via SQLModel
24	- [ ] Integrate Anthropic Claude API for AI content generation with streaming
25	- [ ] Integrate OpenAI ChatGPT API as an alternate drafting engine with streaming
26	- [ ] Integrate NotebookLM (via `notebooklm-py`) for theological validation with citation retrieval
27	- [ ] Build a validation abstraction layer to enable graceful degradation and provider swapping
28	- [ ] Create the React frontend with dashboard, content editor, validation panel, and calendar views
29	- [ ] Implement the full content lifecycle: draft → validated → scheduled → published
30	- [ ] Add Portuguese SEO metadata tools for blog post optimization
31	- [ ] Implement content versioning and validation history tracking
32	- [ ] Add iCal export for editorial calendar reminders
33	- [ ] Implement environment-based configuration with secure API key management
34	
35	### Out of Scope:
36	- Custom Catholic knowledge base or RAG pipeline (NotebookLM handles this)
37	- Social media analytics or community management
38	- Monetization or revenue features
39	- Multi-user authentication or multi-tenancy
40	- Direct Google Calendar API integration (using .ics export instead)
41	- Bulk social media publishing automation
42	- Mobile native app (responsive web only)
43	- Docker containerization (single-user local/hosted deployment)
44	
45	## Service Context
46	
47	### verbum-backend
48	
49	**Tech Stack:**
50	- Language: Python 3.11+
51	- Framework: FastAPI >= 0.135.0 (with `[standard]` extras for uvicorn)
52	- ORM: SQLModel >= 0.0.37 (Pydantic + SQLAlchemy combined)
53	- Database: SQLite (via aiosqlite for async operations)
54	- AI SDKs: `anthropic >= 0.88.0`, `openai >= 2.30.0`
55	- Validation: `notebooklm-py[browser] >= 0.3.4` (UNOFFICIAL — HIGH RISK)
56	- Calendar: `icalendar` for .ics export
57	- Config: `python-dotenv` for environment variable management
58	- Key directories: `backend/app/`, `backend/app/api/`, `backend/app/models/`, `backend/app/services/`, `backend/app/core/`
59	
60	**Entry Point:** `backend/app/main.py`
61	
62	**How to Run:**
63	```bash
64	cd backend
65	pip install -r requirements.txt
66	uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8000
67	```
68	
69	**Port:** 8000
70	
71	---
72	
73	### verbum-frontend
74	
75	**Tech Stack:**
76	- Language: TypeScript 5.5+
77	- Framework: React 18.3
78	- Bundler: Vite 5.2
79	- Styling: Tailwind CSS 3.4
80	- Icons: lucide-react 0.522
81	- UI Components: Reuse from `prototypes-magic/src/components/ui/` (Button, Input, Select, Card, Tabs, Badge, Table, Textarea, Progress, etc.)
82	- Charts: recharts (already in prototype dependencies)
83	- Key directories: `frontend/src/`, `frontend/src/components/`, `frontend/src/pages/`, `frontend/src/services/`, `frontend/src/hooks/`
84	
85	**Entry Point:** `frontend/src/main.tsx`
86	
87	**How to Run:**
88	```bash
89	cd frontend
90	npm install
91	npm run dev
92	```
93	
94	**Port:** 5173 (Vite default)
95	
96	## Files to Create
97	
98	### Backend Structure
99	
100	| File | Service | Purpose |

App.tsx
components
index.css
main.tsx
services
