0	# Specification: Verbum — Catholic Content Management Platform
1	
2	## Overview
3	
4	Verbum is a greenfield, single-user Catholic content management platform for Ramir, a Portuguese-speaking Catholic content creator. The platform centralizes the full content lifecycle — from AI-assisted generation (via Anthropic Claude and OpenAI ChatGPT) through theological validation (via NotebookLM), editorial scheduling, and multi-channel delivery. It supports three content types: short-form video scripts (1 min), long-form video scripts (10 min), and SEO-optimized blog posts in Portuguese. The goal is to reduce time-to-publish by 50%, ensure 100% doctrinal accuracy through automated validation, and maintain a consistent publishing cadence of at least 3 pieces per week. The entire system is designed for a single user — no multi-tenancy, no scaling concerns, no monetization.
5	
6	## Workflow Type
7	
8	**Type**: feature
9	
10	**Rationale**: This is a brand-new full-stack application being built from scratch within an empty repository. There is no existing application logic to refactor or migrate — only a Vite/React/Tailwind prototype shell (`prototypes-magic/`) with reusable UI components. Every layer (database schema, backend API, service integrations, frontend pages) must be designed and implemented.
11	
12	## Task Scope
13	
14	### Services Involved
15	- **verbum-backend** (primary) — Python FastAPI backend handling API routing, AI integration orchestration, NotebookLM validation, database persistence, and scheduling logic
16	- **verbum-frontend** (primary) — React TypeScript SPA providing the dashboard, content editor, validation panel, editorial calendar, and SEO tools
17	- **SQLite database** (infrastructure) — Embedded file-based database for content items, validation records, scheduling metadata, and version history
18	
19	### This Task Will:
20	- [ ] Set up the full project structure (backend + frontend monorepo)
21	- [ ] Implement the Python FastAPI backend with async architecture
22	- [ ] Design and create the SQLite database schema via SQLModel
23	- [ ] Integrate Anthropic Claude API for AI content generation with streaming
24	- [ ] Integrate OpenAI ChatGPT API as an alternate drafting engine with streaming
25	- [ ] Integrate NotebookLM (via `notebooklm-py`) for theological validation with citation retrieval
26	- [ ] Build a validation abstraction layer to enable graceful degradation and provider swapping
27	- [ ] Create the React frontend with dashboard, content editor, validation panel, and calendar views
28	- [ ] Implement the full content lifecycle: draft → validated → scheduled → published
29	- [ ] Add Portuguese SEO metadata tools for blog post optimization
30	- [ ] Implement content versioning and validation history tracking
31	- [ ] Add iCal export for editorial calendar reminders
32	- [ ] Implement environment-based configuration with secure API key management
33	
34	### Out of Scope:
35	- Custom Catholic knowledge base or RAG pipeline (NotebookLM handles this)
36	- Social media analytics or community management
37	- Monetization or revenue features
38	- Multi-user authentication or multi-tenancy
39	- Direct Google Calendar API integration (using .ics export instead)
40	- Bulk social media publishing automation
41	- Mobile native app (responsive web only)
42	- Docker containerization (single-user local/hosted deployment)
43	
44	## Service Context
45	
46	### verbum-backend
47	
48	**Tech Stack:**
49	- Language: Python 3.11+
50	- Framework: FastAPI >= 0.135.0 (with `[standard]` extras for uvicorn)
51	- ORM: SQLModel >= 0.0.37 (Pydantic + SQLAlchemy combined)
52	- Database: SQLite (via aiosqlite for async operations)
53	- AI SDKs: `anthropic >= 0.88.0`, `openai >= 2.30.0`
54	- Validation: `notebooklm-py[browser] >= 0.3.4` (UNOFFICIAL — HIGH RISK)
55	- Calendar: `icalendar` for .ics export
56	- Config: `python-dotenv` for environment variable management
57	- Key directories: `backend/app/`, `backend/app/api/`, `backend/app/models/`, `backend/app/services/`, `backend/app/core/`
58	
59	**Entry Point:** `backend/app/main.py`
60	
61	**How to Run:**
62	```bash
63	cd backend
64	pip install -r requirements.txt
65	uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8000
66	```
67	
68	**Port:** 8000
69	
70	---
71	
72	### verbum-frontend
73	
74	**Tech Stack:**
75	- Language: TypeScript 5.5+
76	- Framework: React 18.3
77	- Bundler: Vite 5.2
78	- Styling: Tailwind CSS 3.4
79	- Icons: lucide-react 0.522
80	- UI Components: Reuse from `prototypes-magic/src/components/ui/` (Button, Input, Select, Card, Tabs, Badge, Table, Textarea, Progress, etc.)
81	- Charts: recharts (already in prototype dependencies)
82	- Key directories: `frontend/src/`, `frontend/src/components/`, `frontend/src/pages/`, `frontend/src/services/`, `frontend/src/hooks/`
83	
84	**Entry Point:** `frontend/src/main.tsx`
85	
86	**How to Run:**
87	```bash
88	cd frontend
89	npm install
90	npm run dev
91	```
92	
93	**Port:** 5173 (Vite default)
94	
95	## Files to Create
96	
97	### Backend Structure
98	
99	| File | Service | Purpose |
100	|------|---------|---------|
101	| `backend/requirements.txt` | backend | Python dependencies |
102	| `backend/app/__init__.py` | backend | Package init |
103	| `backend/app/main.py` | backend | FastAPI app entry point, middleware, CORS, lifespan |
104	| `backend/app/core/__init__.py` | backend | Core package init |
105	| `backend/app/core/config.py` | backend | Settings class with env var loading (Pydantic BaseSettings) |
106	| `backend/app/core/database.py` | backend | Async SQLite engine, session factory, DB initialization |
107	| `backend/app/core/deps.py` | backend | FastAPI dependency injection (DB sessions, service instances) |
108	| `backend/app/models/__init__.py` | backend | Models package init |
109	| `backend/app/models/content.py` | backend | Content SQLModel (type, status, language, channel, versions) |
110	| `backend/app/models/validation.py` | backend | ValidationRecord SQLModel (score, flags, citations, history) |
111	| `backend/app/models/schedule.py` | backend | Schedule SQLModel (date, channel, status, reminders) |
112	| `backend/app/schemas/__init__.py` | backend | Schemas package init |
113	| `backend/app/schemas/content.py` | backend | Pydantic request/response schemas for content endpoints |
114	| `backend/app/schemas/validation.py` | backend | Pydantic schemas for validation responses |
115	| `backend/app/schemas/generation.py` | backend | Pydantic schemas for AI generation requests/responses |
116	| `backend/app/services/__init__.py` | backend | Services package init |
117	| `backend/app/services/ai_service.py` | backend | Abstraction layer for Claude + ChatGPT with streaming |
118	| `backend/app/services/claude_provider.py` | backend | Anthropic Claude API implementation |
119	| `backend/app/services/openai_provider.py` | backend | OpenAI ChatGPT API implementation |
120	| `backend/app/services/validation_service.py` | backend | Validation abstraction layer (interface/protocol) |
121	| `backend/app/services/notebooklm_provider.py` | backend | NotebookLM implementation with retry + error handling |
122	| `backend/app/services/fallback_validation_provider.py` | backend | AI-based fallback validation if NotebookLM is down |
123	| `backend/app/services/content_service.py` | backend | Content CRUD, versioning, status transitions |
124	| `backend/app/services/schedule_service.py` | backend | Scheduling logic, calendar export |
125	| `backend/app/services/seo_service.py` | backend | Portuguese SEO metadata, readability, keyword suggestions |
126	| `backend/app/api/__init__.py` | backend | API package init |
127	| `backend/app/api/content.py` | backend | Content CRUD endpoints |
128	| `backend/app/api/generation.py` | backend | AI generation endpoints (with SSE streaming) |
129	| `backend/app/api/validation.py` | backend | Theological validation endpoints |
130	| `backend/app/api/schedule.py` | backend | Scheduling and calendar endpoints |
131	| `backend/app/api/seo.py` | backend | SEO analysis endpoints |
132	| `backend/app/api/dashboard.py` | backend | Dashboard aggregation endpoints |
133	| `backend/.env.example` | backend | Environment variable template |
134	| `backend/tests/__init__.py` | backend | Tests package init |
135	| `backend/tests/conftest.py` | backend | Pytest fixtures (test DB, mock services) |
136	| `backend/tests/test_content.py` | backend | Content CRUD tests |
137	| `backend/tests/test_generation.py` | backend | AI generation tests (mocked) |
138	| `backend/tests/test_validation.py` | backend | Validation service tests (mocked) |
139	| `backend/tests/test_schedule.py` | backend | Schedule and calendar export tests |
140	| `backend/tests/test_seo.py` | backend | SEO service tests (readability, keywords, metadata) |
141	
142	### Frontend Structure
143	
144	| File | Service | Purpose |
145	|------|---------|---------|
146	| `frontend/package.json` | frontend | Node dependencies and scripts |
147	| `frontend/tsconfig.json` | frontend | TypeScript configuration |
148	| `frontend/tsconfig.node.json` | frontend | TypeScript config for Vite |
149	| `frontend/vite.config.ts` | frontend | Vite configuration with proxy to backend |
150	| `frontend/tailwind.config.js` | frontend | Tailwind CSS configuration |
151	| `frontend/postcss.config.js` | frontend | PostCSS configuration |
152	| `frontend/index.html` | frontend | HTML entry point |
153	| `frontend/src/main.tsx` | frontend | React app entry point |
154	| `frontend/src/App.tsx` | frontend | Root component with routing |
155	| `frontend/src/index.css` | frontend | Global styles + Tailwind imports |
156	| `frontend/src/types/content.ts` | frontend | TypeScript types for content, validation, schedule |
157	| `frontend/src/services/api.ts` | frontend | API client (fetch wrapper with error handling) |
158	| `frontend/src/services/streaming.ts` | frontend | SSE streaming client for AI generation |
159	| `frontend/src/hooks/useContent.ts` | frontend | Content CRUD hook |
160	| `frontend/src/hooks/useGeneration.ts` | frontend | AI generation hook with streaming state |
161	| `frontend/src/hooks/useValidation.ts` | frontend | Validation trigger + result hook |
162	| `frontend/src/hooks/useSchedule.ts`

entries.content:
Subtask 5-2 is complete. I created three files:

1. **`frontend/src/services/streaming.ts`** - SSE streaming client using `fetch` + `ReadableStream` to consume server-sent events, parsing `data:` lines into `GenerationResponse` objects with callbacks for token/done/error.

2. **`frontend/src/hooks/useGeneration.ts`** - `useGeneration` hook managing streaming state (`idle`/`generating`/`error`), accumulated output text, and providing `generate`/`cancel`/`reset` controls with `AbortController` for cancellation.

3. **`frontend/src/components/GenerationPanel.tsx`** - UI component with AI model selector (Claude/ChatGPT), prompt textarea, generate/stop buttons, error display, streaming output with spinner, and "Accept & Insert" button. Uses existing UI primitives (Button, Card, Textarea).

TypeScript compilation passes with zero errors.

entries.detail:
0	# Specification: Verbum — Catholic Content Management Platform
1	
2	## Overview
3	
4	Verbum is a greenfield, single-user Catholic content management platform for Ramir, a Portuguese-speaking Catholic content creator. The platform centralizes the full content lifecycle — from AI-assisted generation (via Anthropic Claude and OpenAI ChatGPT) through theological validation (via NotebookLM), editorial scheduling, and multi-channel delivery. It supports three content types: short-form video scripts (1 min), long-form video scripts (10 min), and SEO-optimized blog posts in Portuguese. The goal is to reduce time-to-publish by 50%, ensure 100% doctrinal accuracy through automated validation, and maintain a consistent publishing cadence of at least 3 pieces per week. The entire system is designed for a single user — no multi-tenancy, no scaling concerns, no monetization.
5	
6	## Workflow Type
7	
8	**Type**: feature
9	
10	**Rationale**: This is a brand-new full-stack application being built from scratch within an empty repository. There is no existing application logic to refactor or migrate — only a Vite/React/Tailwind prototype shell (`prototypes-magic/`) with reusable UI components. Every layer (database schema, backend API, service integrations, frontend pages) must be designed and implemented.
11	
12	## Task Scope
13	
14	### Services Involved
15	- **verbum-backend** (primary) — Python FastAPI backend handling API routing, AI integration orchestration, NotebookLM validation, database persistence, and scheduling logic
16	- **verbum-frontend** (primary) — React TypeScript SPA providing the dashboard, content editor, validation panel, editorial calendar, and SEO tools
17	- **SQLite database** (infrastructure) — Embedded file-based database for content items, validation records, scheduling metadata, and version history
18	
19	### This Task Will:
20	- [ ] Set up the full project structure (backend + frontend monorepo)
21	- [ ] Implement the Python FastAPI backend with async architecture
22	- [ ] Design and create the SQLite database schema via SQLModel
23	- [ ] Integrate Anthropic Claude API for AI content generation with streaming
24	- [ ] Integrate OpenAI ChatGPT API as an alternate drafting engine with streaming
25	- [ ] Integrate NotebookLM (via `notebooklm-py`) for theological validation with citation retrieval
26	- [ ] Build a validation abstraction layer to enable graceful degradation and provider swapping
27	- [ ] Create the React frontend with dashboard, content editor, validation panel, and calendar views
28	- [ ] Implement the full content lifecycle: draft → validated → scheduled → published
29	- [ ] Add Portuguese SEO metadata tools for blog post optimization
30	- [ ] Implement content versioning and validation history tracking
31	- [ ] Add iCal export for editorial calendar reminders
32	- [ ] Implement environment-based configuration with secure API key management
33	
34	### Out of Scope:
35	- Custom Catholic knowledge base or RAG pipeline (NotebookLM handles this)
36	- Social media analytics or community management
37	- Monetization or revenue features
38	- Multi-user authentication or multi-tenancy
39	- Direct Google Calendar API integration (using .ics export instead)
40	- Bulk social media publishing automation
41	- Mobile native app (responsive web only)
42	- Docker containerization (single-user local/hosted deployment)
43	
44	## Service Context
45	
46	### verbum-backend
47	
48	**Tech Stack:**
49	- Language: Python 3.11+
50	- Framework: FastAPI >= 0.135.0 (with `[standard]` extras for uvicorn)
51	- ORM: SQLModel >= 0.0.37 (Pydantic + SQLAlchemy combined)
52	- Database: SQLite (via aiosqlite for async operations)
53	- AI SDKs: `anthropic >= 0.88.0`, `openai >= 2.30.0`
54	- Validation: `notebooklm-py[browser] >= 0.3.4` (UNOFFICIAL — HIGH RISK)
55	- Calendar: `icalendar` for .ics export
56	- Config: `python-dotenv` for environment variable management
57	- Key directories: `backend/app/`, `backend/app/api/`, `backend/app/models/`, `backend/app/services/`, `backend/app/core/`
58	
59	**Entry Point:** `backend/app/main.py`
60	
61	**How to Run:**
62	```bash
63	cd backend
64	pip install -r requirements.txt
65	uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8000
66	```
67	
68	**Port:** 8000
69	
70	---
71	
72	### verbum-frontend
73	
74	**Tech Stack:**
75	- Language: TypeScript 5.5+
76	- Framework: React 18.3
77	- Bundler: Vite 5.2
78	- Styling: Tailwind CSS 3.4
79	- Icons: lucide-react 0.522
80	- UI Components: Reuse from `prototypes-magic/src/components/ui/` (Button, Input, Select, Card, Tabs, Badge, Table, Textarea, Progress, etc.)
81	- Charts: recharts (already in prototype dependencies)
82	- Key directories: `frontend/src/`, `frontend/src/components/`, `frontend/src/pages/`, `frontend/src/services/`, `frontend/src/hooks/`
83	
84	**Entry Point:** `frontend/src/main.tsx`
85	
86	**How to Run:**
87	```bash
88	cd frontend
89	npm install
90	npm run dev
91	```
92	
93	**Port:** 5173 (Vite default)
94	
95	## Files to Create
96	
97	### Backend Structure
98	
99	| File | Service | Purpose |
100	|------|---------|---------|
101	| `backend/requirements.txt` | backend | Python dependencies |
102	| `backend/app/__init__.py` | backend | Package init |
103	| `backend/app/main.py` | backend | FastAPI app entry point, middleware, CORS, lifespan |
104	| `backend/app/core/__init__.py` | backend | Core package init |
105	| `backend/app/core/config.py` | backend | Settings class with env var loading (Pydantic BaseSettings) |
106	| `backend/app/core/database.py` | backend | Async SQLite engine, session factory, DB initialization |
107	| `backend/app/core/deps.py` | backend | FastAPI dependency injection (DB sessions, service instances) |
108	| `backend/app/models/__init__.py` | backend | Models package init |
109	| `backend/app/models/content.py` | backend | Content SQLModel (type, status, language, channel, versions) |
110	| `backend/app/models/validation.py` | backend | ValidationRecord SQLModel (score, flags, citations, history) |
111	| `backend/app/models/schedule.py` | backend | Schedule SQLModel (date, channel, status, reminders) |
112	| `backend/app/schemas/__init__.py` | backend | Schemas package init |
113	| `backend/app/schemas/content.py` | backend | Pydantic request/response schemas for content endpoints |
114	| `backend/app/schemas/validation.py` | backend | Pydantic schemas for validation responses |
115	| `backend/app/schemas/generation.py` | backend | Pydantic schemas for AI generation requests/responses |
116	| `backend/app/services/__init__.py` | backend | Services package init |
117	| `backend/app/services/ai_service.py` | backend | Abstraction layer for Claude + ChatGPT with streaming |
118	| `backend/app/services/claude_provider.py` | backend | Anthropic Claude API implementation |
119	| `backend/app/services/openai_provider.py` | backend | OpenAI ChatGPT API implementation |
120	| `backend/app/services/validation_service.py` | backend | Validation abstraction layer (interface/protocol) |
121	| `backend/app/services/notebooklm_provider.py` | backend | NotebookLM implementation with retry + error handling |
122	| `backend/app/services/fallback_validation_provider.py` | backend | AI-based fallback validation if NotebookLM is down |
123	| `backend/app/services/content_service.py` | backend | Content CRUD, versioning, status transitions |
124	| `backend/app/services/schedule_service.py` | backend | Scheduling logic, calendar export |
125	| `backend/app/services/seo_service.py` | backend | Portuguese SEO metadata, readability, keyword suggestions |
126	| `backend/app/api/__init__.py` | backend | API package init |
127	| `backend/app/api/content.py` | backend | Content CRUD endpoints |
128	| `backend/app/api/generation.py` | backend | AI generation endpoints (with SSE streaming) |

Done. Here's what was implemented:

**ContentEditor component** (`frontend/src/components/ContentEditor.tsx`):
- Title input, content type selector (short video/long video/blog post), and a large textarea for the body
- Save button with loading state
- Loads existing content data when editing

**EditorPage** (`frontend/src/pages/EditorPage.tsx`):
- **Main area**: ContentEditor + GenerationPanel (with "Accept & Insert" wiring to append generated text to body)
- **Right panel (w-96)**: ValidationPanel (which includes CitationList internally)
- Content loading from API for existing items, creation for new items
- URL updates after creating new content

TypeScript compiles cleanly with no errors in the modified files.

entries.detail:
0	# Specification: Verbum — Catholic Content Management Platform
1	
2	## Overview
3	
4	Verbum is a greenfield, single-user Catholic content management platform for Ramir, a Portuguese-speaking Catholic content creator. The platform centralizes the full content lifecycle — from AI-assisted generation (via Anthropic Claude and OpenAI ChatGPT) through theological validation (via NotebookLM), editorial scheduling, and multi-channel delivery. It supports three content types: short-form video scripts (1 min), long-form video scripts (10 min), and SEO-optimized blog posts in Portuguese. The goal is to reduce time-to-publish by 50%, ensure 100% doctrinal accuracy through automated validation, and maintain a consistent publishing cadence of at least 3 pieces per week. The entire system is designed for a single user — no multi-tenancy, no scaling concerns, no monetization.
5	
6	## Workflow Type
7	
8	**Type**: feature
9	
10	**Rationale**: This is a brand-new full-stack application being built from scratch within an empty repository. There is no existing application logic to refactor or migrate — only a Vite/React/Tailwind prototype shell (`prototypes-magic/`) with reusable UI components. Every layer (database schema, backend API, service integrations, frontend pages) must be designed and implemented.
11	
12	## Task Scope
13	
14	### Services Involved
15	- **verbum-backend** (primary) — Python FastAPI backend handling API routing, AI integration orchestration, NotebookLM validation, database persistence, and scheduling logic
16	- **verbum-frontend** (primary) — React TypeScript SPA providing the dashboard, content editor, validation panel, editorial calendar, and SEO tools
17	- **SQLite database** (infrastructure) — Embedded file-based database for content items, validation records, scheduling metadata, and version history
18	
19	### This Task Will:
20	- [ ] Set up the full project structure (backend + frontend monorepo)
21	- [ ] Implement the Python FastAPI backend with async architecture
22	- [ ] Design and create the SQLite database schema via SQLModel
23	- [ ] Integrate Anthropic Claude API for AI content generation with streaming
24	- [ ] Integrate OpenAI ChatGPT API as an alternate drafting engine with streaming
25	- [ ] Integrate NotebookLM (via `notebooklm-py`) for theological validation with citation retrieval
26	- [ ] Build a validation abstraction layer to enable graceful degradation and provider swapping
27	- [ ] Create the React frontend with dashboard, content editor, validation panel, and calendar views
28	- [ ] Implement the full content lifecycle: draft → validated → scheduled → published
29	- [ ] Add Portuguese SEO metadata tools for blog post optimization
30	- [ ] Implement content versioning and validation history tracking
31	- [ ] Add iCal export for editorial calendar reminders
32	- [ ] Implement environment-based configuration with secure API key management
33	
34	### Out of Scope:
35	- Custom Catholic knowledge base or RAG pipeline (NotebookLM handles this)
36	- Social media analytics or community management
37	- Monetization or revenue features
38	- Multi-user authentication or multi-tenancy
39	- Direct Google Calendar API integration (using .ics export instead)
40	- Bulk social media publishing automation
41	- Mobile native app (responsive web only)
42	- Docker containerization (single-user local/hosted deployment)
43	
44	## Service Context
45	
46	### verbum-backend
47	
48	**Tech Stack:**
49	- Language: Python 3.11+
50	- Framework: FastAPI >= 0.135.0 (with `[standard]` extras for uvicorn)
51	- ORM: SQLModel >= 0.0.37 (Pydantic + SQLAlchemy combined)
52	- Database: SQLite (via aiosqlite for async operations)
53	- AI SDKs: `anthropic >= 0.88.0`, `openai >= 2.30.0`
54	- Validation: `notebooklm-py[browser] >= 0.3.4` (UNOFFICIAL — HIGH RISK)
55	- Calendar: `icalendar` for .ics export
56	- Config: `python-dotenv` for environment variable management
57	- Key directories: `backend/app/`, `backend/app/api/`, `backend/app/models/`, `backend/app/services/`, `backend/app/core/`
58	
59	**Entry Point:** `backend/app/main.py`
60	
61	**How to Run:**
62	```bash
63	cd backend
64	pip install -r requirements.txt
65	uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8000
66	```
67	
68	**Port:** 8000
69	
70	---
71	
72	### verbum-frontend
73	
74	**Tech Stack:**
75	- Language: TypeScript 5.5+
76	- Framework: React 18.3
77	- Bundler: Vite 5.2
78	- Styling: Tailwind CSS 3.4
79	- Icons: lucide-react 0.522
80	- UI Components: Reuse from `prototypes-magic/src/components/ui/` (Button, Input, Select, Card, Tabs, Badge, Table, Textarea, Progress, etc.)
81	- Charts: recharts (already in prototype dependencies)
82	- Key directories: `frontend/src/`, `frontend/src/components/`, `frontend/src/pages/`, `frontend/src/services/`, `frontend/src/hooks/`
83	
84	**Entry Point:** `frontend/src/main.tsx`
85	
86	**How to Run:**
87	```bash
88	cd frontend
89	npm install
90	npm run dev
91	```
92	
93	**Port:** 5173 (Vite default)
94	
95	## Files to Create
96	
97	### Backend Structure
98	
99	| File | Service | Purpose |
100	|------|---------|---------|
101	| `backend/requirements.txt` | backend | Python dependencies |
102	| `backend/app/__init__.py` | backend | Package init |
103	| `backend/app/main.py` | backend | FastAPI app entry point, middleware, CORS, lifespan |
104	| `backend/app/core/__init__.py` | backend | Core package init |
105	| `backend/app/core/config.py` | backend | Settings class with env var loading (Pydantic BaseSettings) |
106	| `backend/app/core/database.py` | backend | Async SQLite engine, session factory, DB initialization |
107	| `backend/app/core/deps.py` | backend | FastAPI dependency injection (DB sessions, service instances) |
108	| `backend/app/models/__init__.py` | backend | Models package init |
109	| `backend/app/models/content.py` | backend | Content SQLModel (type, status, language, channel, versions) |
110	| `backend/app/models/validation.py` | backend | ValidationRecord SQLModel (score, flags, citations, history) |
111	| `backend/app/models/schedule.py` | backend | Schedule SQLModel (date, channel, status, reminders) |
112	| `backend/app/schemas/__init__.py` | backend | Schemas package init |
113	| `backend/app/schemas/content.py` | backend | Pydantic request/response schemas for content endpoints |
114	| `backend/app/schemas/validation.py` | backend | Pydantic schemas for validation responses |
115	| `backend/app/schemas/generation.py` | backend | Pydantic schemas for AI generation requests/responses |
116	| `backend/app/services/__init__.py` | backend | Services package init |
117	| `backend/app/services/ai_service.py` | backend | Abstraction layer for Claude + ChatGPT with streaming |
118	| `backend/app/services/claude_provider.py` | backend | Anthropic Claude API implementation |
119	| `backend/app/services/openai_provider.py` | backend | OpenAI ChatGPT API implementation |
120	| `backend/app/services/validation_service.py` | backend | Validation abstraction layer (interface/protocol) |
121	| `backend/app/services/notebooklm_provider.py` | backend | NotebookLM implementation with retry + error handling |
122	| `backend/app/services/fallback_validation_provider.py` | backend | AI-based fallback validation if NotebookLM is down |
123	| `backend/app/services/content_service.py` | backend | Content CRUD, versioning, status transitions |
124	| `backend/app/services/schedule_service.py` | backend | Scheduling logic, calendar export |
125	| `backend/app/services/seo_service.py` | backend | Portuguese SEO metadata, readability, keyword suggestions |
126	| `backend/app/api/__init__.py` | backend | API package init |
127	| `backend/app/api/content.py` | backend | Content CRUD endpoints |
128	| `backend/app/api/generation.py` | backend | AI generation endpoints (with SSE streaming) |
129	| `backend/app/api/validation.py` | backend | Theological validation endpoints |

37	
38	            providers["openai"] = OpenAIProvider(api_key=settings.openai_api_key)
39	            logger.info("OpenAI provider registered")

46	AIServiceDep = Annotated[AIService, Depends(get_ai_service)]

49	@lru_cache
50	def get_validation_service() -> ValidationService:
51	    """Create and cache the ValidationService with primary and fallback providers."""
52	    settings = get_settings()
53	
54	    # Primary provider: NotebookLM
55	    try:
56	        from app.services.notebooklm_provider import NotebookLMProvider
57	
58	        primary = NotebookLMProvider(api_key=getattr(settings, "notebooklm_api_key", "") or "")
59	        logger.info("NotebookLM validation provider registered")
60	    except Exception as exc:
61	        logger.warning("Failed to initialize NotebookLM provider: %s", exc)
62	        primary = None
63	
64	    # Fallback provider: AI-based validation
65	    try:
66	        from app.services.fallback_validation_provider import FallbackValidationProvider
67	
68	        fallback = FallbackValidationProvider()
69	        logger.info("Fallback validation provider registered")
70	    except Exception as exc:
71	        logger.warning("Failed to initialize fallback validation provider: %s", exc)
72	        fallback = None
73	
74	    if primary is None and fallback is None:
75	        raise RuntimeError("No validation providers available")
76	
77	    # If primary is missing, use fallback as both
78	    if primary is None:
79	        primary = fallback
80	    if fallback is None:
81	        fallback = primary
82	
83	    return ValidationService(primary=primary, fallback=fallback)
84	
85	
86	ValidationServiceDep = Annotated[ValidationService, Depends(get_validation_service)]

462:3. **Theological Validation via NotebookLM (High Priority)**
471:   - Description: Each content item moves through a linear status pipeline: draft → validated → scheduled → published. Status transitions are tracked with timestamps. Dashboard shows counts per status.
472:   - Acceptance: Status is visible on all content views (list, editor, calendar). Only valid transitions are allowed (e.g., cannot schedule without validation). Dashboard shows accurate pipeline counts.
474:6. **Editorial Calendar (Medium Priority)**
475:   - Description: Visual week/month calendar view showing scheduled content items. Click to schedule, reschedule, or view content details. Supports filtering by content type and channel.
476:   - Acceptance: Calendar displays all scheduled items on their publication dates. User can drag/click to schedule items. Week and month views both work. Items show content type icon, title, and channel.
480:   - Acceptance: SEO panel shows all metadata fields when editing a blog post. Readability score is calculated automatically. Keyword suggestions can be triggered via AI.
483:   - Description: Tag content with delivery channels (YouTube, Blog, other). Each content item can be assigned to one or more channels. Channels appear on calendar and list views.
484:   - Acceptance: User can tag content with channels, filter by channel, and see channel assignments on calendar.
486:9. **iCal Export (Low Priority)**
487:   - Description: Export the editorial calendar as an .ics file compatible with Google Calendar, Apple Calendar, and Outlook.
488:   - Acceptance: Exported .ics file contains all scheduled items with correct dates, titles, and channel info. File imports successfully into Google Calendar.
503:8. **NotebookLM Auth Token Expiry** — Handle token refresh automatically. If refresh fails, show re-authentication instructions.
504:9. **Calendar Date in the Past** — Allow scheduling in the past (for backdating published content) but show a warning.

0	# Specification: Verbum — Catholic Content Management Platform
1	
2	## Overview
3	
4	Verbum is a greenfield, single-user Catholic content management platform for Ramir, a Portuguese-speaking Catholic content creator. The platform centralizes the full content lifecycle — from AI-assisted generation (via Anthropic Claude and OpenAI ChatGPT) through theological validation (via NotebookLM), editorial scheduling, and multi-channel delivery. It supports three content types: short-form video scripts (1 min), long-form video scripts (10 min), and SEO-optimized blog posts in Portuguese. The goal is to reduce time-to-publish by 50%, ensure 100% doctrinal accuracy through automated validation, and maintain a consistent publishing cadence of at least 3 pieces per week. The entire system is designed for a single user — no multi-tenancy, no scaling concerns, no monetization.
5	
6	## Workflow Type
7	
8	**Type**: feature
9	
10	**Rationale**: This is a brand-new full-stack application being built from scratch within an empty repository. There is no existing application logic to refactor or migrate — only a Vite/React/Tailwind prototype shell (`prototypes-magic/`) with reusable UI components. Every layer (database schema, backend API, service integrations, frontend pages) must be designed and implemented.
11	
12	## Task Scope
13	
14	### Services Involved
15	- **verbum-backend** (primary) — Python FastAPI backend handling API routing, AI integration orchestration, NotebookLM validation, database persistence, and scheduling logic
16	- **verbum-frontend** (primary) — React TypeScript SPA providing the dashboard, content editor, validation panel, editorial calendar, and SEO tools
17	- **SQLite database** (infrastructure) — Embedded file-based database for content items, validation records, scheduling metadata, and version history
18	
19	### This Task Will:
20	- [ ] Set up the full project structure (backend + frontend monorepo)
21	- [ ] Implement the Python FastAPI backend with async architecture
22	- [ ] Design and create the SQLite database schema via SQLModel
23	- [ ] Integrate Anthropic Claude API for AI content generation with streaming
24	- [ ] Integrate OpenAI ChatGPT API as an alternate drafting engine with streaming
25	- [ ] Integrate NotebookLM (via `notebooklm-py`) for theological validation with citation retrieval
26	- [ ] Build a validation abstraction layer to enable graceful degradation and provider swapping
27	- [ ] Create the React frontend with dashboard, content editor, validation panel, and calendar views
28	- [ ] Implement the full content lifecycle: draft → validated → scheduled → published
29	- [ ] Add Portuguese SEO metadata tools for blog post optimization
30	- [ ] Implement content versioning and validation history tracking
31	- [ ] Add iCal export for editorial calendar reminders
32	- [ ] Implement environment-based configuration with secure API key management
33	
34	### Out of Scope:
35	- Custom Catholic knowledge base or RAG pipeline (NotebookLM handles this)
36	- Social media analytics or community management
37	- Monetization or revenue features
38	- Multi-user authentication or multi-tenancy
39	- Direct Google Calendar API integration (using .ics export instead)
40	- Bulk social media publishing automation
41	- Mobile native app (responsive web only)
42	- Docker containerization (single-user local/hosted deployment)
43	
44	## Service Context
45	
46	### verbum-backend
47	
48	**Tech Stack:**
49	- Language: Python 3.11+
50	- Framework: FastAPI >= 0.135.0 (with `[standard]` extras for uvicorn)
51	- ORM: SQLModel >= 0.0.37 (Pydantic + SQLAlchemy combined)
52	- Database: SQLite (via aiosqlite for async operations)
53	- AI SDKs: `anthropic >= 0.88.0`, `openai >= 2.30.0`
54	- Validation: `notebooklm-py[browser] >= 0.3.4` (UNOFFICIAL — HIGH RISK)
55	- Calendar: `icalendar` for .ics export
56	- Config: `python-dotenv` for environment variable management
57	- Key directories: `backend/app/`, `backend/app/api/`, `backend/app/models/`, `backend/app/services/`, `backend/app/core/`
58	
59	**Entry Point:** `backend/app/main.py`
60	
61	**How to Run:**
62	```bash
63	cd backend
64	pip install -r requirements.txt
65	uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8000
66	```
67	
68	**Port:** 8000
69	
70	---
71	
72	### verbum-frontend
73	
74	**Tech Stack:**
75	- Language: TypeScript 5.5+
76	- Framework: React 18.3
77	- Bundler: Vite 5.2
78	- Styling: Tailwind CSS 3.4
79	- Icons: lucide-react 0.522
80	- UI Components: Reuse from `prototypes-magic/src/components/ui/` (Button, Input, Select, Card, Tabs, Badge, Table, Textarea, Progress, etc.)
81	- Charts: recharts (already in prototype dependencies)
82	- Key directories: `frontend/src/`, `frontend/src/components/`, `frontend/src/pages/`, `frontend/src/services/`, `frontend/src/hooks/`
83	
84	**Entry Point:** `frontend/src/main.tsx`
85	
86	**How to Run:**
87	```bash
88	cd frontend
89	npm install
90	npm run dev
91	```
92	
93	**Port:** 5173 (Vite default)
94	
95	## Files to Create
96	
97	### Backend Structure
98	
99	| File | Service | Purpose |

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	import json
4	from datetime import datetime, timezone
5	from typing import Optional
6	
7	from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession
8	from sqlmodel import select
9	
10	from app.models.content import ContentItem, ContentStatus, ContentType, ContentVersion
11	from app.schemas.content import ContentCreate, ContentListParams, ContentUpdate
12	
13	
14	# Valid status transitions: draft→validated→scheduled→published
15	VALID_TRANSITIONS: dict[ContentStatus, list[ContentStatus]] = {
16	    ContentStatus.DRAFT: [ContentStatus.VALIDATED],
17	    ContentStatus.VALIDATED: [ContentStatus.SCHEDULED, ContentStatus.DRAFT],
18	    ContentStatus.SCHEDULED: [ContentStatus.PUBLISHED, ContentStatus.VALIDATED],
19	    ContentStatus.PUBLISHED: [],
20	}
21	
22	
23	class ContentService:
24	    def __init__(self, session: AsyncSession):
25	        self.session = session
26	
27	    async def create(self, data: ContentCreate) -> ContentItem:
28	        item = ContentItem(
29	            title=data.title,
30	            body=data.body,
31	            content_type=data.content_type,
32	            language=data.language,
33	            channels=json.dumps(data.channels),
34	            ai_model_used=data.ai_model_used,
35	        )
36	        self.session.add(item)
37	        await self.session.commit()
38	        await self.session.refresh(item)
39	        return item
40	
41	    async def get(self, content_id: str) -> Optional[ContentItem]:
42	        return await self.session.get(ContentItem, content_id)
43	
44	    async def list(self, params: ContentListParams) -> tuple[list[ContentItem], int]:
45	        statement = select(ContentItem)
46	
47	        if params.status is not None:
48	            statement = statement.where(ContentItem.status == params.status)
49	        if params.content_type is not None:
50	            statement = statement.where(ContentItem.content_type == params.content_type)
51	        if params.channel is not None:
52	            statement = statement.where(ContentItem.channels.contains(params.channel))
53	
54	        statement = statement.order_by(ContentItem.updated_at.desc())
55	
56	        result = await self.session.exec(statement)
57	        items = list(result.all())
58	        return items, len(items)
59	
60	    async def update(self, content_id: str, data: ContentUpdate) -> Optional[ContentItem]:
61	        item = await self.get(content_id)
62	        if item is None:
63	            return None
64	
65	        # Create version snapshot before updating
66	        await self._create_version_snapshot(item)
67	
68	        if data.title is not None:
69	            item.title = data.title
70	        if data.body is not None:
71	            item.body = data.body
72	        if data.channels is not None:
73	            item.channels = json.dumps(data.channels)
74	        if data.ai_model_used is not None:
75	            item.ai_model_used = data.ai_model_used
76	
77	        item.version += 1
78	        item.updated_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
79	
80	        self.session.add(item)
81	        await self.session.commit()
82	        await self.session.refresh(item)
83	        return item
84	
85	    async def delete(self, content_id: str) -> bool:
86	        item = await self.get(content_id)
87	        if item is None:
88	            return False
89	        await self.session.delete(item)
90	        await self.session.commit()
91	        return True
92	
93	    async def transition_status(
94	        self, content_id: str, new_status: ContentStatus
95	    ) -> ContentItem:
96	        item = await self.get(content_id)
97	        if item is None:
98	            raise ValueError(f"Content item '{content_id}' not found")
99	
100	        allowed = VALID_TRANSITIONS.get(item.status, [])
101	        if new_status not in allowed:
102	            raise ValueError(
103	                f"Invalid status transition from '{item.status.value}' to '{new_status.value}'. "
104	                f"Allowed transitions: {[s.value for s in allowed]}"
105	            )
106	
107	        item.status = new_status
108	        item.updated_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
109	
110	        self.session.add(item)
111	        await self.session.commit()
112	        await self.session.refresh(item)
113	        return item
114	
115	    async def get_versions(self, content_id: str) -> list[ContentVersion]:
116	        statement = (
117	            select(ContentVersion)
118	            .where(ContentVersion.content_id == content_id)
119	            .order_by(ContentVersion.version_number.desc())
120	        )
121	        result = await self.session.exec(statement)
122	        return list(result.all())
123	
124	    async def _create_version_snapshot(self, item: ContentItem) -> ContentVersion:
125	        version = ContentVersion(
126	            content_id=item.id,
127	            version_number=item.version,
128	            title=item.title,
129	            body=item.body,
130	            ai_model_used=item.ai_model_used,
131	        )
132	        self.session.add(version)
133	        return version
134

1	from datetime import datetime, timezone
2	from enum import Enum
3	from typing import Optional
4	from uuid import uuid4
5	
6	from sqlmodel import Field, SQLModel
7	
8	
9	class ContentType(str, Enum):
10	    SHORT_VIDEO = "short_video"
11	    LONG_VIDEO = "long_video"
12	    BLOG_POST = "blog_post"
13	
14	
15	class ContentStatus(str, Enum):
16	    DRAFT = "draft"
17	    VALIDATED = "validated"
18	    SCHEDULED = "scheduled"
19	    PUBLISHED = "published"
20	
21	
22	class ContentItem(SQLModel, table=True):
23	    __tablename__ = "content_items"
24	
25	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid4()), primary_key=True)
26	    title: str
27	    body: str = ""
28	    content_type: ContentType
29	    status: ContentStatus = ContentStatus.DRAFT
30	    language: str = "pt"
31	    channels: str = "[]"  # JSON list of channel strings, e.g. '["youtube", "blog"]'
32	    ai_model_used: Optional[str] = None
33	    scheduled_date: Optional[datetime] = None
34	    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
35	    updated_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
36	    version: int = 1
37	
38	
39	class ContentVersion(SQLModel, table=True):
40	    __tablename__ = "content_versions"
41	
42	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid4()), primary_key=True)
43	    content_id: str = Field(foreign_key="content_items.id", index=True)
44	    version_number: int
45	    title: str
46	    body: str = ""
47	    ai_model_used: Optional[str] = None
48	    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
49	
50	
51	class SEOMetadata(SQLModel, table=True):
52	    __tablename__ = "seo_metadata"
53	
54	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid4()), primary_key=True)
55	    content_id: str = Field(foreign_key="content_items.id", unique=True, index=True)
56	    meta_title: str = ""
57	    meta_description: str = ""
58	    keywords: str = "[]"  # JSON list of keyword strings
59	    slug: str = ""
60	    readability_score: Optional[float] = None
61	    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
62	    updated_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))

1	import json
2	from typing import Optional
3	
4	from fastapi import APIRouter, HTTPException, Query
5	
6	from app.core.deps import SessionDep
7	from app.models.content import ContentStatus, ContentType
8	from app.schemas.content import (
9	    ContentCreate,
10	    ContentListParams,
11	    ContentListResponse,
12	    ContentResponse,
13	    ContentUpdate,
14	    ContentVersionResponse,
15	    StatusTransition,
16	)
17	from app.services.content_service import ContentService
18	
19	router = APIRouter()
20	
21	
22	def _to_response(item) -> ContentResponse:
23	    return ContentResponse(
24	        id=item.id,
25	        title=item.title,
26	        body=item.body,
27	        content_type=item.content_type,
28	        status=item.status,
29	        language=item.language,
30	        channels=json.loads(item.channels),
31	        ai_model_used=item.ai_model_used,
32	        scheduled_date=item.scheduled_date,
33	        created_at=item.created_at,
34	        updated_at=item.updated_at,
35	        version=item.version,
36	    )
37	
38	
39	@router.get("", response_model=ContentListResponse)
40	async def list_content(
41	    session: SessionDep,
42	    status: Optional[ContentStatus] = Query(None),
43	    content_type: Optional[ContentType] = Query(None),
44	    channel: Optional[str] = Query(None),
45	):
46	    service = ContentService(session)
47	    params = ContentListParams(status=status, content_type=content_type, channel=channel)
48	    items, total = await service.list(params)
49	    return ContentListResponse(
50	        items=[_to_response(item) for item in items],
51	        total=total,
52	    )
53	
54	
55	@router.post("", response_model=ContentResponse, status_code=201)
56	async def create_content(data: ContentCreate, session: SessionDep):
57	    service = ContentService(session)
58	    item = await service.create(data)
59	    return _to_response(item)
60	
61	
62	@router.get("/{content_id}", response_model=ContentResponse)
63	async def get_content(content_id: str, session: SessionDep):
64	    service = ContentService(session)
65	    item = await service.get(content_id)
66	    if item is None:
67	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Content item not found")
68	    return _to_response(item)
69	
70	
71	@router.put("/{content_id}", response_model=ContentResponse)
72	async def update_content(content_id: str, data: ContentUpdate, session: SessionDep):
73	    service = ContentService(session)
74	    item = await service.update(content_id, data)
75	    if item is None:
76	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Content item not found")
77	    return _to_response(item)
78	
79	
80	@router.delete("/{content_id}", status_code=204)
81	async def delete_content(content_id: str, session: SessionDep):
82	    service = ContentService(session)
83	    deleted = await service.delete(content_id)
84	    if not deleted:
85	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Content item not found")
86	
87	
88	@router.get("/{content_id}/versions", response_model=list[ContentVersionResponse])
89	async def get_versions(content_id: str, session: SessionDep):
90	    service = ContentService(session)
91	    item = await service.get(content_id)
92	    if item is None:
93	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Content item not found")
94	    versions = await service.get_versions(content_id)
95	    return versions
96	
97	
98	@router.post("/{content_id}/status", response_model=ContentResponse)
99	async def transition_status(
100	    content_id: str, data: StatusTransition, session: SessionDep
101	):
102	    service = ContentService(session)
103	    try:
104	        item = await service.transition_status(content_id, data.new_status)
105	    except ValueError as e:
106	        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail=str(e))
107	    return _to_response(item)
108

1	import logging
2	from functools import lru_cache
3	from typing import Annotated
4	
5	from fastapi import Depends
6	from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession
7	
8	from app.core.config import Settings, get_settings
9	from app.core.database import get_session
10	from app.services.ai_service import AIService
11	from app.services.validation_service import ValidationService
12	
13	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
14	
15	SessionDep = Annotated[AsyncSession, Depends(get_session)]
16	SettingsDep = Annotated[Settings, Depends(get_settings)]
17	
18	
19	@lru_cache
20	def get_ai_service() -> AIService:
21	    """Create and cache the AIService with available providers."""
22	    settings = get_settings()
23	    providers: dict = {}
24	
25	    if settings.anthropic_api_key:
26	        try:
27	            from app.services.claude_provider import ClaudeProvider
28	
29	            providers["claude"] = ClaudeProvider(api_key=settings.anthropic_api_key)
30	            logger.info("Claude provider registered")
31	        except Exception as exc:
32	            logger.warning("Failed to initialize Claude provider: %s", exc)
33	
34	    if settings.openai_api_key:
35	        try:
36	            from app.services.openai_provider import OpenAIProvider
37	
38	            providers["openai"] = OpenAIProvider(api_key=settings.openai_api_key)
39	            logger.info("OpenAI provider registered")
40	        except Exception as exc:
41	            logger.warning("Failed to initialize OpenAI provider: %s", exc)
42	
43	    return AIService(providers=providers)
44	
45	
46	AIServiceDep = Annotated[AIService, Depends(get_ai_service)]
47	
48	
49	@lru_cache
50	def get_validation_service() -> ValidationService:
51	    """Create and cache the ValidationService with primary and fallback providers."""
52	    settings = get_settings()
53	
54	    # Primary provider: NotebookLM
55	    try:
56	        from app.services.notebooklm_provider import NotebookLMProvider
57	
58	        primary = NotebookLMProvider(api_key=getattr(settings, "notebooklm_api_key", "") or "")
59	        logger.info("NotebookLM validation provider registered")
60	    except Exception as exc:

entries.content:
Subtask-6-2 is complete. Here's what was implemented:

**SEO Service** (`backend/app/services/seo_service.py`):
- `calculate_readability_score()` — Portuguese readability scoring (0-100) based on sentence length and word complexity (syllable counting for Portuguese words)
- `SEOService.get_metadata()` — fetch SEO metadata by content_id
- `SEOService.upsert_metadata()` — create/update SEO metadata (meta_title, meta_description, keywords, slug)
- `SEOService.analyze()` — analyze content readability and persist score
- `SEOService.suggest_keywords()` — AI-powered keyword suggestions using Portuguese SEO prompt

**SEO API** (`backend/app/api/seo.py`) — 4 routes:
- `GET /{content_id}` — get SEO metadata
- `PUT /{content_id}` — update SEO metadata
- `POST /{content_id}/analyze` — analyze readability
- `POST /{content_id}/keywords` — AI keyword suggestions

667	## API Endpoints
668	
669	### Content API (`/api/content`)
670	| Method | Path | Description |
671	|--------|------|-------------|
672	| `GET` | `/` | List all content items (with filtering: status, type, channel) |
673	| `POST` | `/` | Create a new content item |
674	| `GET` | `/{id}` | Get a single content item |
675	| `PUT` | `/{id}` | Update a content item (body, title, status, channel) |
676	| `DELETE` | `/{id}` | Delete a content item |
677	| `GET` | `/{id}/versions` | Get version history for a content item |
678	| `GET` | `/{id}/versions/{version}` | Get a specific version snapshot |
679	| `POST` | `/{id}/status` | Transition content status (with validation) |
680	
681	### Generation API (`/api/generation`)
682	| Method | Path | Description |
683	|--------|------|-------------|
684	| `POST` | `/generate` | Generate content (full response) |
685	| `POST` | `/stream` | Generate content with SSE streaming |
686	| `GET` | `/models` | List available AI models and their status |
687	
688	### Validation API (`/api/validation`)
689	| Method | Path | Description |
690	|--------|------|-------------|
691	| `POST` | `/validate/{content_id}` | Trigger validation for a content item |
692	| `GET` | `/history/{content_id}` | Get validation history for a content item |
693	| `GET` | `/status` | Check validation service availability |
694	
695	### Schedule API (`/api/schedule`)
696	| Method | Path | Description |
697	|--------|------|-------------|
698	| `GET` | `/calendar` | Get scheduled items for a date range |
699	| `POST` | `/{content_id}/schedule` | Schedule a content item |
700	| `PUT` | `/{content_id}/reschedule` | Reschedule a content item |
701	| `GET` | `/export/ical` | Export calendar as .ics file |
702	
703	### Dashboard API (`/api/dashboard`)
704	| Method | Path | Description |
705	|--------|------|-------------|
706	| `GET` | `/summary` | Pipeline summary (counts per status, recent items) |
707	| `GET` | `/upcoming` | Next 7 days of scheduled content |
708	
709	### SEO API (`/api/seo`)
710	| Method | Path | Description |
711	|--------|------|-------------|
712	| `GET` | `/{content_id}` | Get SEO metadata for a content item |
713	| `PUT` | `/{content_id}` | Update SEO metadata |
714	| `POST` | `/{content_id}/analyze` | Run readability analysis |
715	| `POST` | `/{content_id}/keywords` | Generate keyword suggestions via AI |
716	
717	## Implementation Phases
718	
719	### Phase 1: Foundation (Backend + Frontend Skeleton)
720	1. Set up project structure (backend and frontend directories)
721	2. Create FastAPI app with CORS, lifespan, and router structure
722	3. Define SQLModel data models and database initialization
723	4. Create React app with routing, Tailwind, and UI component library
724	5. Implement content CRUD (backend API + frontend list/create pages)
725	6. **Milestone**: Can create, list, edit, and delete content items
726	
727	### Phase 2: AI Generation Integration
728	1. Implement Claude provider with streaming
729	2. Implement OpenAI provider with streaming
730	3. Create AI service abstraction layer
731	4. Build generation API endpoints (full + streaming)
732	5. Build frontend GenerationPanel with model selector and streaming display
733	6. Implement content type templates (system prompts per type)
734	7. **Milestone**: Can generate content via Claude or ChatGPT with real-time streaming
735	
736	### Phase 3: Theological Validation
737	1. Implement NotebookLM provider with error handling and retry
738	2. Implement AI-based fallback validation provider
739	3. Create validation service with automatic failover
740	4. Build validation API endpoints
741	5. Build frontend ValidationPanel with score, flags, citations
742	6. Implement validation history tracking
743	7. **Milestone**: Can validate content with citation display and fallback
744	
745	### Phase 4: Calendar, SEO, and Polish
746	1. Build editorial calendar UI (week/month views)
747	2. Implement scheduling API and status transitions
748	3. Add iCal export
749	4. Build SEO metadata panel for blog posts
750	5. Implement content versioning
751	6. Build dashboard with pipeline summary
752	7. Portuguese localization of interface elements
753	8. **Milestone**: Full content lifecycle operational
754	
755	## Success Criteria
756	
757	The task is complete when:
758	
759	1. [ ] Project structure is set up with backend (FastAPI/Python) and frontend (React/TypeScript) directories
760	2. [ ] SQLite database is created with all required tables (content_items, content_versions, validation_records, seo_metadata)
761	3. [ ] Content CRUD operations work end-to-end (create, read, update, delete, list with filtering)
762	4. [ ] AI generation works with both Claude and ChatGPT, including real-time streaming to the editor
763	5. [ ] Content type templates (short video, long video, blog post) produce appropriately structured output
764	6. [ ] Theological validation via NotebookLM returns score, flags, and traceable citations
765	7. [ ] Validation fallback to AI-based provider works when NotebookLM is unavailable
766	8. [ ] Content status lifecycle (draft → validated → scheduled → published) is enforced and tracked
767	9. [ ] Editorial calendar displays scheduled items in week and month views
768	10. [ ] Portuguese SEO metadata tools work for blog posts (meta fields, readability, keyword suggestions)
769	11. [ ] Content versioning tracks edit history with version snapshots
770	12. [ ] iCal export produces valid .ics files
771	13. [ ] Dashboard shows pipeline summary with status counts and upcoming items
772	14. [ ] All API keys are managed via environment variables — none exposed in frontend
773	15. [ ] Frontend is Portuguese-first with consistent, responsive design
774	16. [ ] No console errors in normal operation
775	17. [ ] All existing tests pass, new tests cover core functionality
776	
777	## QA Acceptance Criteria
778	
779	**CRITICAL**: These criteria must be verified by the QA Agent before sign-off.
780

783	| Test | File | What to Verify |

49	    """Reschedule a content item."""
50	    service = ScheduleService(session)
51	    try:
52	        entry = await service.reschedule(content_id, new_date, channel)
53	    except ValueError as e:
54	        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail=str(e))
55	    return _entry_to_dict(entry)
56	
57	
58	@router.get("/export/ical")
59	async def export_ical(
60	    session: SessionDep,
61	    start: Optional[datetime] = Query(None),
62	    end: Optional[datetime] = Query(None),
63	):
64	    """Export editorial calendar as iCal (.ics) file."""
65	    service = ScheduleService(session)
66	    ical_data = await service.export_ical(start, end)
67	    return Response(
68	        content=ical_data,
69	        media_type="text/calendar",
70	        headers={"Content-Disposition": "attachment; filename=verbum-calendar.ics"},
71	    )
72	
73	
74	def _entry_to_dict(entry) -> dict:
75	    return {
76	        "id": entry.id,
77	        "content_id": entry.content_id,
78	        "scheduled_date": entry.scheduled_date.isoformat(),
79	        "channel": entry.channel,
80	        "status": entry.status,
81	        "reminder_sent": entry.reminder_sent,
82	        "created_at": entry.created_at.isoformat(),
83	        "updated_at": entry.updated_at.isoformat(),
84	    }
85

1	import json
2	import logging
3	from uuid import uuid4
4	
5	from fastapi import APIRouter, HTTPException
6	from sqlmodel import select
7	
8	from app.core.deps import SessionDep, ValidationServiceDep
9	from app.models.content import ContentItem, ContentStatus, ContentVersion
10	from app.models.validation import ValidationRecord
11	from app.schemas.validation import (
12	    CitationResponse,
13	    ValidationHistoryResponse,
14	    ValidationResponse,
15	    ValidationStatusResponse,
16	)
17	
18	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
19	
20	router = APIRouter()
21	
22	
23	def _record_to_response(record: ValidationRecord) -> ValidationResponse:
24	    """Convert a ValidationRecord to a ValidationResponse."""
25	    return ValidationResponse(
26	        id=record.id,
27	        content_id=record.content_id,
28	        score=record.score,
29	        flags=json.loads(record.flags),
30	        citations=[
31	            CitationResponse(**c) for c in json.loads(record.citations)
32	        ],
33	        provider=record.provider,
34	        raw_answer=record.raw_answer,
35	        content_version=record.content_version,
36	        created_at=record.created_at,
37	    )
38	
39	
40	@router.post("/validate/{content_id}", response_model=ValidationResponse)
41	async def validate_content(
42	    content_id: str,
43	    session: SessionDep,
44	    validation_service: ValidationServiceDep,
45	):
46	    """Trigger theological validation for content. Creates a version snapshot before validating."""
47	    item = await session.get(ContentItem, content_id)
48	    if not item:
49	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Content not found")
50	
51	    # Create version snapshot before validation
52	    snapshot = ContentVersion(
53	        id=str(uuid4()),
54	        content_id=item.id,
55	        version_number=item.version,
56	        title=item.title,
57	        body=item.body,
58	        ai_model_used=item.ai_model_used or "",
59	    )
60	    session.add(snapshot)
61	
62	    # Run validation
63	    result = await validation_service.validate(item.body, item.content_type.value)
64	
65	    # Persist validation record
66	    record = ValidationRecord(
67	        content_id=content_id,
68	        score=result.score,
69	        flags=json.dumps(result.flags),
70	        citations=json.dumps(
71	            [
72	                {
73	                    "citation_number": c.citation_number,
74	                    "source_id": c.source_id,
75	                    "cited_text": c.cited_text,
76	                }
77	                for c in result.citations
78	            ]
79	        ),
80	        provider=result.provider,
81	        raw_answer=result.raw_answer,
82	        content_version=item.version,
83	    )
84	    session.add(record)
85	
86	    # Update content status to validated
87	    item.status = ContentStatus.VALIDATED
88	    session.add(item)
89	
90	    await session.commit()
91	    await session.refresh(record)
92	
93	    return _record_to_response(record)
94	
95	
96	@router.get("/history/{content_id}", response_model=ValidationHistoryResponse)
97	async def get_validation_history(content_id: str, session: SessionDep):
98	    """Get validation history for content."""
99	    item = await session.get(ContentItem, content_id)
100	    if not item:
101	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Content not found")
102	
103	    stmt = (
104	        select(ValidationRecord)
105	        .where(ValidationRecord.content_id == content_id)
106	        .order_by(ValidationRecord.created_at.desc())
107	    )
108	    results = await session.exec(stmt)
109	    records = results.all()
110	
111	    return ValidationHistoryResponse(
112	        items=[_record_to_response(r) for r in records],
113	        total=len(records),
114	    )
115	
116	
117	@router.get("/status", response_model=ValidationStatusResponse)
118	async def get_validation_status(validation_service: ValidationServiceDep):
119	    """Check validation service availability."""
120	    primary_available = await validation_service._primary.is_available()
121	    return ValidationStatusResponse(
122	        primary_available=primary_available,
123	        primary_provider=type(validation_service._primary).__name__,
124	        fallback_provider=type(validation_service._fallback).__name__,
125	    )

12	    ModelsResponse,
13	)

15	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
16	
17	router = APIRouter()
18	
19	DEFAULT_SYSTEM_PROMPT = (
20	    "Você é um assistente católico especializado em criar conteúdo "
21	    "fiel à doutrina da Igreja Católica, em português brasileiro."
22	)
23	
24	
25	@router.post("/generate", response_model=GenerationResponse)
26	async def generate_content(request: GenerationRequest, ai_service: AIServiceDep):
27	    """Generate content using AI (full response)."""
28	    system_prompt = request.system_prompt or DEFAULT_SYSTEM_PROMPT
29	    try:
30	        text = await ai_service.generate(
31	            model_key=request.model,
32	            prompt=request.prompt,
33	            system_prompt=system_prompt,
34	            max_tokens=request.max_tokens,
35	        )
36	        return GenerationResponse(text=text, model=request.model)
37	    except ValueError as exc:
38	        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail=str(exc))
39	    except Exception as exc:
40	        logger.error("Generation failed: %s", exc)
41	        raise HTTPException(status_code=502, detail="AI generation failed")
42	
43	
44	@router.post("/stream")
45	async def generate_content_stream(
46	    request: GenerationRequest, ai_service: AIServiceDep
47	):
48	    """Generate content using AI with SSE streaming."""
49	    system_prompt = request.system_prompt or DEFAULT_SYSTEM_PROMPT
50	
51	    try:
52	        ai_service.get_provider(request.model)
53	    except ValueError as exc:
54	        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail=str(exc))
55	
56	    async def event_generator():
57	        try:
58	            async for chunk in ai_service.generate_stream(
59	                model_key=request.model,
60	                prompt=request.prompt,
61	                system_prompt=system_prompt,
62	                max_tokens=request.max_tokens,
63	            ):
64	                yield f"data: {json.dumps({'text': chunk})}\n\n"
65	            yield f"data: {json.dumps({'done': True})}\n\n"
66	        except Exception as exc:
67	            logger.error("Stream generation failed: %s", exc)
68	            yield f"data: {json.dumps({'error': str(exc), 'done': True})}\n\n"
69	
70	    return StreamingResponse(event_generator(), media_type="text/event-stream")
71	
72	
73	@router.get("/models", response_model=ModelsResponse)
74	async def list_models(ai_service: AIServiceDep):
75	    """List available AI models and their status."""
76	    model_names = {"claude": "Anthropic Claude", "openai": "OpenAI ChatGPT"}
77	    available = ai_service.available_models
78	    models = [
79	        ModelInfo(
80	            key=key,
81	            name=model_names.get(key, key),
82	            available=key in available,
83	        )
84	        for key in ["claude", "openai"]
85	    ]
86	    return ModelsResponse(models=models)

49	@lru_cache
50	def get_validation_service() -> ValidationService:
51	    """Create and cache the ValidationService with primary and fallback providers."""
52	    settings = get_settings()
53	
54	    # Primary provider: NotebookLM
55	    try:
56	        from app.services.notebooklm_provider import NotebookLMProvider
57	
58	        primary = NotebookLMProvider(api_key=getattr(settings, "notebooklm_api_key", "") or "")
59	        logger.info("NotebookLM validation provider registered")
60	    except Exception as exc:
61	        logger.warning("Failed to initialize NotebookLM provider: %s", exc)
62	        primary = None
63	
64	    # Fallback provider: AI-based validation
65	    try:
66	        from app.services.fallback_validation_provider import FallbackValidationProvider
67	
68	        fallback = FallbackValidationProvider()
69	        logger.info("Fallback validation provider registered")
70	    except Exception as exc:
71	        logger.warning("Failed to initialize fallback validation provider: %s", exc)
72	        fallback = None
73	
74	    if primary is None and fallback is None:
75	        raise RuntimeError("No validation providers available")
76	
77	    # If primary is missing, use fallback as both
78	    if primary is None:
79	        primary = fallback
80	    if fallback is None:
81	        fallback = primary
82	
83	    return ValidationService(primary=primary, fallback=fallback)

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	from datetime import datetime, timezone
4	from typing import Optional
5	
6	from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession
7	from sqlmodel import select
8	
9	from app.models.content import ContentItem, ContentStatus
10	from app.models.schedule import ScheduleEntry, ScheduleStatus
11	
12	
13	class ScheduleService:
14	    def __init__(self, session: AsyncSession):
15	        self.session = session
16	
17	    async def get_calendar(
18	        self, start: datetime, end: datetime, channel: Optional[str] = None
19	    ) -> list[ScheduleEntry]:
20	        """Get scheduled items within a date range, optionally filtered by channel."""
21	        statement = select(ScheduleEntry).where(
22	            ScheduleEntry.scheduled_date >= start,
23	            ScheduleEntry.scheduled_date <= end,
24	            ScheduleEntry.status != ScheduleStatus.CANCELLED,
25	        )
26	        if channel:
27	            statement = statement.where(ScheduleEntry.channel == channel)
28	        statement = statement.order_by(ScheduleEntry.scheduled_date)
29	        result = await self.session.exec(statement)
30	        return list(result.all())
31	
32	    async def schedule(
33	        self, content_id: str, scheduled_date: datetime, channel: str
34	    ) -> ScheduleEntry:
35	        """Schedule a content item for publishing on a given date and channel."""
36	        item = await self.session.get(ContentItem, content_id)
37	        if item is None:
38	            raise ValueError(f"Content item {content_id} not found")
39	
40	        if item.status not in (ContentStatus.VALIDATED, ContentStatus.SCHEDULED):
41	            raise ValueError(
42	                f"Content must be validated before scheduling (current: {item.status})"
43	            )
44	
45	        entry = ScheduleEntry(
46	            content_id=content_id,
47	            scheduled_date=scheduled_date,
48	            channel=channel,
49	        )
50	        self.session.add(entry)
51	
52	        item.status = ContentStatus.SCHEDULED
53	        item.scheduled_date = scheduled_date
54	        item.updated_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
55	        self.session.add(item)
56	
57	        await self.session.commit()
58	        await self.session.refresh(entry)
59	        return entry
60	
61	    async def reschedule(
62	        self, content_id: str, new_date: datetime, channel: Optional[str] = None
63	    ) -> ScheduleEntry:
64	        """Reschedule a content item to a new date (and optionally new channel)."""
65	        statement = select(ScheduleEntry).where(
66	            ScheduleEntry.content_id == content_id,
67	            ScheduleEntry.status == ScheduleStatus.PENDING,
68	        )
69	        result = await self.session.exec(statement)
70	        entry = result.first()
71	        if entry is None:
72	            raise ValueError(
73	                f"No pending schedule entry found for content {content_id}"
74	            )
75	
76	        entry.scheduled_date = new_date
77	        if channel:
78	            entry.channel = channel
79	        entry.updated_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
80	        self.session.add(entry)
81	
82	        item = await self.session.get(ContentItem, content_id)
83	        if item:
84	            item.scheduled_date = new_date
85	            item.updated_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
86	            self.session.add(item)
87	
88	        await self.session.commit()
89	        await self.session.refresh(entry)
90	        return entry
91	
92	    async def export_ical(
93	        self, start: Optional[datetime] = None, end: Optional[datetime] = None
94	    ) -> str:
95	        """Export scheduled items as an iCal (.ics) string."""
96	        from icalendar import Calendar, Event
97	
98	        statement = select(ScheduleEntry).where(
99	            ScheduleEntry.status != ScheduleStatus.CANCELLED
100	        )
101	        if start:
102	            statement = statement.where(ScheduleEntry.scheduled_date >= start)
103	        if end:
104	            statement = statement.where(ScheduleEntry.scheduled_date <= end)
105	        statement = statement.order_by(ScheduleEntry.scheduled_date)
106	
107	        result = await self.session.exec(statement)
108	        entries = list(result.all())
109	
110	        cal = Calendar()
111	        cal.add("prodid", "-//Verbum Content Calendar//EN")
112	        cal.add("version", "2.0")
113	        cal.add("calscale", "GREGORIAN")
114	
115	        for entry in entries:
116	            item = await self.session.get(ContentItem, entry.content_id)
117	            title = item.title if item else f"Content {entry.content_id}"
118	
119	            event = Event()
120	            event.add("uid", f"{entry.id}@verbum")
121	            event.add("dtstart", entry.scheduled_date)
122	            event.add("summary", f"[{entry.channel}] {title}")
123	            event.add("description", f"Channel: {entry.channel}\nStatus: {entry.status}")
124	            event.add("created", entry.created_at)
125	            cal.add_component(event)
126	
127	        return cal.to_ical().decode("utf-8")
128

23	    reminder_sent: bool = False
24	    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))

1	# Specification: Verbum — Catholic Content Management Platform
2	
3	## Overview
4	
5	Verbum is a greenfield, single-user Catholic content management platform for Ramir, a Portuguese-speaking Catholic content creator. The platform centralizes the full content lifecycle — from AI-assisted generation (via Anthropic Claude and OpenAI ChatGPT) through theological validation (via NotebookLM), editorial scheduling, and multi-channel delivery. It supports three content types: short-form video scripts (1 min), long-form video scripts (10 min), and SEO-optimized blog posts in Portuguese. The goal is to reduce time-to-publish by 50%, ensure 100% doctrinal accuracy through automated validation, and maintain a consistent publishing cadence of at least 3 pieces per week. The entire system is designed for a single user — no multi-tenancy, no scaling concerns, no monetization.
6	
7	## Workflow Type
8	
9	**Type**: feature
10	
11	**Rationale**: This is a brand-new full-stack application being built from scratch within an empty repository. There is no existing application logic to refactor or migrate — only a Vite/React/Tailwind prototype shell (`prototypes-magic/`) with reusable UI components. Every layer (database schema, backend API, service integrations, frontend pages) must be designed and implemented.
12	
13	## Task Scope
14	
15	### Services Involved
16	- **verbum-backend** (primary) — Python FastAPI backend handling API routing, AI integration orchestration, NotebookLM validation, database persistence, and scheduling logic
17	- **verbum-frontend** (primary) — React TypeScript SPA providing the dashboard, content editor, validation panel, editorial calendar, and SEO tools
18	- **SQLite database** (infrastructure) — Embedded file-based database for content items, validation records, scheduling metadata, and version history
19	
20	### This Task Will:
21	- [ ] Set up the full project structure (backend + frontend monorepo)
22	- [ ] Implement the Python FastAPI backend with async architecture
23	- [ ] Design and create the SQLite database schema via SQLModel
24	- [ ] Integrate Anthropic Claude API for AI content generation with streaming
25	- [ ] Integrate OpenAI ChatGPT API as an alternate drafting engine with streaming
26	- [ ] Integrate NotebookLM (via `notebooklm-py`) for theological validation with citation retrieval
27	- [ ] Build a validation abstraction layer to enable graceful degradation and provider swapping
28	- [ ] Create the React frontend with dashboard, content editor, validation panel, and calendar views
29	- [ ] Implement the full content lifecycle: draft → validated → scheduled → published
30	- [ ] Add Portuguese SEO metadata tools for blog post optimization
31	- [ ] Implement content versioning and validation history tracking
32	- [ ] Add iCal export for editorial calendar reminders
33	- [ ] Implement environment-based configuration with secure API key management
34	
35	### Out of Scope:
36	- Custom Catholic knowledge base or RAG pipeline (NotebookLM handles this)
37	- Social media analytics or community management
38	- Monetization or revenue features
39	- Multi-user authentication or multi-tenancy
40	- Direct Google Calendar API integration (using .ics export instead)
41	- Bulk social media publishing automation
42	- Mobile native app (responsive web only)
43	- Docker containerization (single-user local/hosted deployment)
44	
45	## Service Context
46	
47	### verbum-backend
48	
49	**Tech Stack:**
50	- Language: Python 3.11+
51	- Framework: FastAPI >= 0.135.0 (with `[standard]` extras for uvicorn)
52	- ORM: SQLModel >= 0.0.37 (Pydantic + SQLAlchemy combined)
53	- Database: SQLite (via aiosqlite for async operations)
54	- AI SDKs: `anthropic >= 0.88.0`, `openai >= 2.30.0`
55	- Validation: `notebooklm-py[browser] >= 0.3.4` (UNOFFICIAL — HIGH RISK)
56	- Calendar: `icalendar` for .ics export
57	- Config: `python-dotenv` for environment variable management
58	- Key directories: `backend/app/`, `backend/app/api/`, `backend/app/models/`, `backend/app/services/`, `backend/app/core/`
59
