0	# Specification: Verbum — Catholic Content Management Platform
1	
2	## Overview
3	
4	Verbum is a greenfield, single-user Catholic content management platform for Ramir, a Portuguese-speaking Catholic content creator. The platform centralizes the full content lifecycle — from AI-assisted generation (via Anthropic Claude and OpenAI ChatGPT) through theological validation (via NotebookLM), editorial scheduling, and multi-channel delivery. It supports three content types: short-form video scripts (1 min), long-form video scripts (10 min), and SEO-optimized blog posts in Portuguese. The goal is to reduce time-to-publish by 50%, ensure 100% doctrinal accuracy through automated validation, and maintain a consistent publishing cadence of at least 3 pieces per week. The entire system is designed for a single user — no multi-tenancy, no scaling concerns, no monetization.
5	
6	## Workflow Type
7	
8	**Type**: feature
9	
10	**Rationale**: This is a brand-new full-stack application being built from scratch within an empty repository. There is no existing application logic to refactor or migrate — only a Vite/React/Tailwind prototype shell (`prototypes-magic/`) with reusable UI components. Every layer (database schema, backend API, service integrations, frontend pages) must be designed and implemented.
11	
12	## Task Scope
13	
14	### Services Involved
15	- **verbum-backend** (primary) — Python FastAPI backend handling API routing, AI integration orchestration, NotebookLM validation, database persistence, and scheduling logic
16	- **verbum-frontend** (primary) — React TypeScript SPA providing the dashboard, content editor, validation panel, editorial calendar, and SEO tools
17	- **SQLite database** (infrastructure) — Embedded file-based database for content items, validation records, scheduling metadata, and version history
18	
19	### This Task Will:
20	- [ ] Set up the full project structure (backend + frontend monorepo)
21	- [ ] Implement the Python FastAPI backend with async architecture
22	- [ ] Design and create the SQLite database schema via SQLModel
23	- [ ] Integrate Anthropic Claude API for AI content generation with streaming
24	- [ ] Integrate OpenAI ChatGPT API as an alternate drafting engine with streaming
25	- [ ] Integrate NotebookLM (via `notebooklm-py`) for theological validation with citation retrieval
26	- [ ] Build a validation abstraction layer to enable graceful degradation and provider swapping
27	- [ ] Create the React frontend with dashboard, content editor, validation panel, and calendar views
28	- [ ] Implement the full content lifecycle: draft → validated → scheduled → published
29	- [ ] Add Portuguese SEO metadata tools for blog post optimization
30	- [ ] Implement content versioning and validation history tracking
31	- [ ] Add iCal export for editorial calendar reminders
32	- [ ] Implement environment-based configuration with secure API key management
33	
34	### Out of Scope:
35	- Custom Catholic knowledge base or RAG pipeline (NotebookLM handles this)
36	- Social media analytics or community management
37	- Monetization or revenue features
38	- Multi-user authentication or multi-tenancy
39	- Direct Google Calendar API integration (using .ics export instead)
40	- Bulk social media publishing automation
41	- Mobile native app (responsive web only)
42	- Docker containerization (single-user local/hosted deployment)
43	
44	## Service Context
45	
46	### verbum-backend
47	
48	**Tech Stack:**
49	- Language: Python 3.11+
50	- Framework: FastAPI >= 0.135.0 (with `[standard]` extras for uvicorn)
51	- ORM: SQLModel >= 0.0.37 (Pydantic + SQLAlchemy combined)
52	- Database: SQLite (via aiosqlite for async operations)
53	- AI SDKs: `anthropic >= 0.88.0`, `openai >= 2.30.0`
54	- Validation: `notebooklm-py[browser] >= 0.3.4` (UNOFFICIAL — HIGH RISK)
55	- Calendar: `icalendar` for .ics export
56	- Config: `python-dotenv` for environment variable management
57	- Key directories: `backend/app/`, `backend/app/api/`, `backend/app/models/`, `backend/app/services/`, `backend/app/core/`
58	
59	**Entry Point:** `backend/app/main.py`
60	
61	**How to Run:**
62	```bash
63	cd backend
64	pip install -r requirements.txt
65	uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8000
66	```
67	
68	**Port:** 8000
69	
70	---
71	
72	### verbum-frontend
73	
74	**Tech Stack:**
75	- Language: TypeScript 5.5+
76	- Framework: React 18.3
77	- Bundler: Vite 5.2
78	- Styling: Tailwind CSS 3.4
79	- Icons: lucide-react 0.522
80	- UI Components: Reuse from `prototypes-magic/src/components/ui/` (Button, Input, Select, Card, Tabs, Badge, Table, Textarea, Progress, etc.)
81	- Charts: recharts (already in prototype dependencies)
82	- Key directories: `frontend/src/`, `frontend/src/components/`, `frontend/src/pages/`, `frontend/src/services/`, `frontend/src/hooks/`
83	
84	**Entry Point:** `frontend/src/main.tsx`
85	
86	**How to Run:**
87	```bash
88	cd frontend
89	npm install
90	npm run dev
91	```
92	
93	**Port:** 5173 (Vite default)
94	
95	## Files to Create
96	
97	### Backend Structure
98	
99	| File | Service | Purpose |

from datetime import datetime, timezone
from enum import Enum
from typing import Optional
from uuid import uuid4

from sqlmodel import Field, SQLModel


class ContentType(str, Enum):
    SHORT_VIDEO = "short_video"
    LONG_VIDEO = "long_video"
    BLOG_POST = "blog_post"


class ContentStatus(str, Enum):
    DRAFT = "draft"
    VALIDATED = "validated"
    SCHEDULED = "scheduled"
    PUBLISHED = "published"


class ContentItem(SQLModel, table=True):
    __tablename__ = "content_items"

    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid4()), primary_key=True)
    title: str
    body: str = ""
    content_type: ContentType
    status: ContentStatus = ContentStatus.DRAFT
    language: str = "pt"
    channels: str = "[]"  # JSON list of channel strings, e.g. '["youtube", "blog"]'
    ai_model_used: Optional[str] = None
    scheduled_date: Optional[datetime] = None
    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
    updated_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
    version: int = 1


class ContentVersion(SQLModel, table=True):
    __tablename__ = "content_versions"

    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid4()), primary_key=True)
    content_id: str = Field(foreign_key="content_items.id", index=True)
    version_number: int
    title: str
    body: str = ""
    ai_model_used: Optional[str] = None
    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))


class SEOMetadata(SQLModel, table=True):
    __tablename__ = "seo_metadata"

    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid4()), primary_key=True)
    content_id: str = Field(foreign_key="content_items.id", unique=True, index=True)
    meta_title: str = ""
    meta_description: str = ""
    keywords: str = "[]"  # JSON list of keyword strings
    slug: str = ""
    readability_score: Optional[float] = None
    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
    updated_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))

import json
from typing import Optional

from fastapi import APIRouter, HTTPException, Query

from app.core.deps import SessionDep
from app.models.content import ContentStatus, ContentType
from app.schemas.content import (
    ContentCreate,
    ContentListParams,
    ContentListResponse,
    ContentResponse,
    ContentUpdate,
    ContentVersionResponse,
    StatusTransition,
)
from app.services.content_service import ContentService

router = APIRouter()


def _to_response(item) -> ContentResponse:
    return ContentResponse(
        id=item.id,
        title=item.title,
        body=item.body,
        content_type=item.content_type,
        status=item.status,
        language=item.language,
        channels=json.loads(item.channels),
        ai_model_used=item.ai_model_used,
        scheduled_date=item.scheduled_date,
        created_at=item.created_at,
        updated_at=item.updated_at,
        version=item.version,
    )


@router.get("", response_model=ContentListResponse)
async def list_content(
    session: SessionDep,
    status: Optional[ContentStatus] = Query(None),
    content_type: Optional[ContentType] = Query(None),
    channel: Optional[str] = Query(None),
):
    service = ContentService(session)
    params = ContentListParams(status=status, content_type=content_type, channel=channel)
    items, total = await service.list(params)
    return ContentListResponse(
        items=[_to_response(item) for item in items],
        total=total,
    )


@router.post("", response_model=ContentResponse, status_code=201)
async def create_content(data: ContentCreate, session: SessionDep):
    service = ContentService(session)
    item = await service.create(data)
    return _to_response(item)


@router.get("/{content_id}", response_model=ContentResponse)
async def get_content(content_id: str, session: SessionDep):
    service = ContentService(session)
    item = await service.get(content_id)
    if item is None:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Content item not found")
    return _to_response(item)


@router.put("/{content_id}", response_model=ContentResponse)
async def update_content(content_id: str, data: ContentUpdate, session: SessionDep):
    service = ContentService(session)
    item = await service.update(content_id, data)
    if item is None:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Content item not found")
    return _to_response(item)


@router.delete("/{content_id}", status_code=204)
async def delete_content(content_id: str, session: SessionDep):
    service = ContentService(session)
    deleted = await service.delete(content_id)
    if not deleted:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Content item not found")


@router.get("/{content_id}/versions", response_model=list[ContentVersionResponse])
async def get_versions(content_id: str, session: SessionDep):
    service = ContentService(session)
    item = await service.get(content_id)
    if item is None:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Content item not found")
    versions = await service.get_versions(content_id)
    return versions


@router.post("/{content_id}/status", response_model=ContentResponse)
async def transition_status(
    content_id: str, data: StatusTransition, session: SessionDep
):
    service = ContentService(session)
    try:
        item = await service.transition_status(content_id, data.new_status)
    except ValueError as e:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail=str(e))
    return _to_response(item)

from contextlib import asynccontextmanager

from fastapi import FastAPI
from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware

from app.api import content, dashboard, generation, schedule, seo, validation
from app.core.database import init_db


@asynccontextmanager
async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):
    await init_db()
    yield


app = FastAPI(title="Verbum API", lifespan=lifespan)

app.add_middleware(
    CORSMiddleware,
    allow_origins=["http://localhost:5173"],
    allow_credentials=True,
    allow_methods=["*"],
    allow_headers=["*"],
)

app.include_router(content.router, prefix="/api/content", tags=["content"])
app.include_router(generation.router, prefix="/api/generation", tags=["generation"])
app.include_router(validation.router, prefix="/api/validation", tags=["validation"])
app.include_router(schedule.router, prefix="/api/schedule", tags=["schedule"])

from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession, create_async_engine
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker
from sqlmodel import SQLModel

from app.core.config import get_settings

settings = get_settings()

engine = create_async_engine(
    settings.database_url,
    echo=settings.debug,
    future=True,
)

async_session_factory = sessionmaker(
    engine,
    class_=AsyncSession,
    expire_on_commit=False,
)


async def init_db() -> None:
    async with engine.begin() as conn:
        await conn.run_sync(SQLModel.metadata.create_all)


async def get_session() -> AsyncSession:
    async with async_session_factory() as session:
        yield session

from datetime import datetime
from typing import Optional

from pydantic import BaseModel, Field

from app.models.content import ContentStatus, ContentType


class ContentCreate(BaseModel):

    body: Optional[str] = None
    channels: Optional[list[str]] = None
    ai_model_used: Optional[str] = None


class StatusTransition(BaseModel):
    new_status: ContentStatus

    id: str
    title: str
    body: str

    model_config = {"from_attributes": True}


class ContentListResponse(BaseModel):

    total: int

    content_id: str
    version_number: int
    title: str
    body: str
    ai_model_used: Optional[str]
    created_at: datetime

    model_config = {"from_attributes": True}


class ContentListParams(BaseModel):
    status: Optional[ContentStatus] = None
    content_type: Optional[ContentType] = None
    channel: Optional[str] = None

from __future__ import annotations

import json
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from typing import Optional

from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession
from sqlmodel import select

from app.models.content import ContentItem, ContentStatus, ContentType, ContentVersion
from app.schemas.content import ContentCreate, ContentListParams, ContentUpdate


# Valid status transitions: draft→validated→scheduled→published
VALID_TRANSITIONS: dict[ContentStatus, list[ContentStatus]] = {
    ContentStatus.DRAFT: [ContentStatus.VALIDATED],
    ContentStatus.VALIDATED: [ContentStatus.SCHEDULED, ContentStatus.DRAFT],
    ContentStatus.SCHEDULED: [ContentStatus.PUBLISHED, ContentStatus.VALIDATED],
    ContentStatus.PUBLISHED: [],
}


class ContentService:
    def __init__(self, session: AsyncSession):
        self.session = session

    async def create(self, data: ContentCreate) -> ContentItem:
        item = ContentItem(
            title=data.title,
            body=data.body,
            content_type=data.content_type,
            language=data.language,
            channels=json.dumps(data.channels),
            ai_model_used=data.ai_model_used,
        )
        self.session.add(item)
        await self.session.commit()
        await self.session.refresh(item)
        return item

    async def get(self, content_id: str) -> Optional[ContentItem]:
        return await self.session.get(ContentItem, content_id)

    async def list(self, params: ContentListParams) -> tuple[list[ContentItem], int]:
        statement = select(ContentItem)

        if params.status is not None:
            statement = statement.where(ContentItem.status == params.status)
        if params.content_type is not None:
            statement = statement.where(ContentItem.content_type == params.content_type)
        if params.channel is not None:
            statement = statement.where(ContentItem.channels.contains(params.channel))

        statement = statement.order_by(ContentItem.updated_at.desc())

        result = await self.session.exec(statement)
        items = list(result.all())
        return items, len(items)

    async def update(self, content_id: str, data: ContentUpdate) -> Optional[ContentItem]:
        item = await self.get(content_id)
        if item is None:
            return None

        # Create version snapshot before updating
        await self._create_version_snapshot(item)

        if data.title is not None:
            item.title = data.title
        if data.body is not None:
            item.body = data.body
        if data.channels is not None:
            item.channels = json.dumps(data.channels)
        if data.ai_model_used is not None:
            item.ai_model_used = data.ai_model_used

        item.version += 1
        item.updated_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

        self.session.add(item)
        await self.session.commit()
        await self.session.refresh(item)
        return item

    async def delete(self, content_id: str) -> bool:
        item = await self.get(content_id)
        if item is None:
            return False
        await self.session.delete(item)
        await self.session.commit()
        return True

    async def transition_status(
        self, content_id: str, new_status: ContentStatus
    ) -> ContentItem:
        item = await self.get(content_id)
        if item is None:
            raise ValueError(f"Content item '{content_id}' not found")

        allowed = VALID_TRANSITIONS.get(item.status, [])
        if new_status not in allowed:
            raise ValueError(
                f"Invalid status transition from '{item.status.value}' to '{new_status.value}'. "
                f"Allowed transitions: {[s.value for s in allowed]}"
            )

        item.status = new_status
        item.updated_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

        self.session.add(item)
        await self.session.commit()
        await self.session.refresh(item)
        return item

    async def get_versions(self, content_id: str) -> list[ContentVersion]:
        statement = (
            select(ContentVersion)
            .where(ContentVersion.content_id == content_id)
            .order_by(ContentVersion.version_number.desc())
        )
        result = await self.session.exec(statement)
        return list(result.all())

    async def _create_version_snapshot(self, item: ContentItem) -> ContentVersion:
        version = ContentVersion(
            content_id=item.id,
            version_number=item.version,
            title=item.title,
            body=item.body,
            ai_model_used=item.ai_model_used,
        )
        self.session.add(version)
        return version

import logging
from functools import lru_cache
from typing import Annotated

from fastapi import Depends
from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession

from app.core.config import Settings, get_settings
from app.core.database import get_session
from app.services.ai_service import AIService
from app.services.validation_service import ValidationService

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

SessionDep = Annotated[AsyncSession, Depends(get_session)]
SettingsDep = Annotated[Settings, Depends(get_settings)]


@lru_cache
def get_ai_service() -> AIService:
    """Create and cache the AIService with available providers."""
    settings = get_settings()
    providers: dict = {}

    if settings.anthropic_api_key:
        try:
            from app.services.claude_provider import ClaudeProvider

            providers["claude"] = ClaudeProvider(api_key=settings.anthropic_api_key)
            logger.info("Claude provider registered")
        except Exception as exc:
            logger.warning("Failed to initialize Claude provider: %s", exc)

    if settings.openai_api_key:
        try:
            from app.services.openai_provider import OpenAIProvider

            providers["openai"] = OpenAIProvider(api_key=settings.openai_api_key)
            logger.info("OpenAI provider registered")
        except Exception as exc:
            logger.warning("Failed to initialize OpenAI provider: %s", exc)

    return AIService(providers=providers)


AIServiceDep = Annotated[AIService, Depends(get_ai_service)]


@lru_cache
def get_validation_service() -> ValidationService:
    """Create and cache the ValidationService with primary and fallback providers."""
    settings = get_settings()

    # Primary provider: NotebookLM
    try:
        from app.services.notebooklm_provider import NotebookLMProvider

        primary = NotebookLMProvider(api_key=getattr(settings, "notebooklm_api_key", "") or "")
        logger.info("NotebookLM validation provider registered")
    except Exception as exc:
        logger.warning("Failed to initialize NotebookLM provider: %s", exc)
        primary = None

    # Fallback provider: AI-based validation
    try:
        from app.services.fallback_validation_provider import FallbackValidationProvider

        fallback = FallbackValidationProvider()
        logger.info("Fallback validation provider registered")
    except Exception as exc:
        logger.warning("Failed to initialize fallback validation provider: %s", exc)
        fallback = None

    if primary is None and fallback is None:
        raise RuntimeError("No validation providers available")

    # If primary is missing, use fallback as both
    if primary is None:
        primary = fallback
    if fallback is None:
        fallback = primary

    return ValidationService(primary=primary, fallback=fallback)


ValidationServiceDep = Annotated[ValidationService, Depends(get_validation_service)]

from functools import lru_cache
from typing import Optional

from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings


class Settings(BaseSettings):
    # Application
    app_env: str = "development"
    debug: bool = True

    # Database
    database_url: str = "sqlite+aiosqlite:///./verbum.db"

    # Anthropic Claude API
    anthropic_api_key: Optional[str] = None

    # OpenAI ChatGPT API
    openai_api_key: Optional[str] = None

    model_config = {
        "env_file": ".env",
        "env_file_encoding": "utf-8",
    }


@lru_cache
def get_settings() -> Settings:
    return Settings()

- **TestContentCRUD** (10 tests): create, read, list, filter, update, delete
- **TestStatusTransitions** (6 tests): valid transitions through the lifecycle, invalid transitions rejected
- **TestVersioning** (2 tests): version snapshots created on update
- **TestFieldValidation** (5 tests): required fields, invalid types, empty title, default language

1	# Specification: Verbum — Catholic Content Management Platform
2	
3	## Overview
4	
5	Verbum is a greenfield, single-user Catholic content management platform for Ramir, a Portuguese-speaking Catholic content creator. The platform centralizes the full content lifecycle — from AI-assisted generation (via Anthropic Claude and OpenAI ChatGPT) through theological validation (via NotebookLM), editorial scheduling, and multi-channel delivery. It supports three content types: short-form video scripts (1 min), long-form video scripts (10 min), and SEO-optimized blog posts in Portuguese. The goal is to reduce time-to-publish by 50%, ensure 100% doctrinal accuracy through automated validation, and maintain a consistent publishing cadence of at least 3 pieces per week. The entire system is designed for a single user — no multi-tenancy, no scaling concerns, no monetization.
6	
7	## Workflow Type
8	
9	**Type**: feature
10	
11	**Rationale**: This is a brand-new full-stack application being built from scratch within an empty repository. There is no existing application logic to refactor or migrate — only a Vite/React/Tailwind prototype shell (`prototypes-magic/`) with reusable UI components. Every layer (database schema, backend API, service integrations, frontend pages) must be designed and implemented.
12	
13	## Task Scope
14	
15	### Services Involved
16	- **verbum-backend** (primary) — Python FastAPI backend handling API routing, AI integration orchestration, NotebookLM validation, database persistence, and scheduling logic
17	- **verbum-frontend** (primary) — React TypeScript SPA providing the dashboard, content editor, validation panel, editorial calendar, and SEO tools
18	- **SQLite database** (infrastructure) — Embedded file-based database for content items, validation records, scheduling metadata, and version history
19	
20	### This Task Will:
21	- [ ] Set up the full project structure (backend + frontend monorepo)
22	- [ ] Implement the Python FastAPI backend with async architecture
23	- [ ] Design and create the SQLite database schema via SQLModel
24	- [ ] Integrate Anthropic Claude API for AI content generation with streaming
25	- [ ] Integrate OpenAI ChatGPT API as an alternate drafting engine with streaming
26	- [ ] Integrate NotebookLM (via `notebooklm-py`) for theological validation with citation retrieval
27	- [ ] Build a validation abstraction layer to enable graceful degradation and provider swapping
28	- [ ] Create the React frontend with dashboard, content editor, validation panel, and calendar views
29	- [ ] Implement the full content lifecycle: draft → validated → scheduled → published
30	- [ ] Add Portuguese SEO metadata tools for blog post optimization
31	- [ ] Implement content versioning and validation history tracking
32	- [ ] Add iCal export for editorial calendar reminders
33	- [ ] Implement environment-based configuration with secure API key management
34	
35	### Out of Scope:
36	- Custom Catholic knowledge base or RAG pipeline (NotebookLM handles this)
37	- Social media analytics or community management
38	- Monetization or revenue features
39	- Multi-user authentication or multi-tenancy
40	- Direct Google Calendar API integration (using .ics export instead)
41	- Bulk social media publishing automation
42	- Mobile native app (responsive web only)
43	- Docker containerization (single-user local/hosted deployment)
44	
45	## Service Context
46	
47	### verbum-backend
48	
49	**Tech Stack:**
50	- Language: Python 3.11+
51	- Framework: FastAPI >= 0.135.0 (with `[standard]` extras for uvicorn)
52	- ORM: SQLModel >= 0.0.37 (Pydantic + SQLAlchemy combined)
53	- Database: SQLite (via aiosqlite for async operations)
54	- AI SDKs: `anthropic >= 0.88.0`, `openai >= 2.30.0`
55	- Validation: `notebooklm-py[browser] >= 0.3.4` (UNOFFICIAL — HIGH RISK)
56	- Calendar: `icalendar` for .ics export
57	- Config: `python-dotenv` for environment variable management
58	- Key directories: `backend/app/`, `backend/app/api/`, `backend/app/models/`, `backend/app/services/`, `backend/app/core/`
59	
60	**Entry Point:** `backend/app/main.py`
61	
62	**How to Run:**
63	```bash
64	cd backend
65	pip install -r requirements.txt
66	uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8000
67	```
68	
69	**Port:** 8000
70	
71	---
72	
73	### verbum-frontend
74	
75	**Tech Stack:**
76	- Language: TypeScript 5.5+
77	- Framework: React 18.3
78	- Bundler: Vite 5.2
79	- Styling: Tailwind CSS 3.4
80	- Icons: lucide-react 0.522
81	- UI Components: Reuse from `prototypes-magic/src/components/ui/` (Button, Input, Select, Card, Tabs, Badge, Table, Textarea, Progress, etc.)
82	- Charts: recharts (already in prototype dependencies)
83	- Key directories: `frontend/src/`, `frontend/src/components/`, `frontend/src/pages/`, `frontend/src/services/`, `frontend/src/hooks/`
84	
85	**Entry Point:** `frontend/src/main.tsx`
86	
87	**How to Run:**
88	```bash
89	cd frontend
90	npm install
91	npm run dev
92	```
93	
94	**Port:** 5173 (Vite default)
95	
96	## Files to Create
97	
98	### Backend Structure
99	
100	| File | Service | Purpose |

1	from datetime import datetime, timezone
2	from enum import Enum
3	from typing import Optional
4	from uuid import uuid4
5	
6	from sqlmodel import Field, SQLModel
7	
8	
9	class ContentType(str, Enum):
10	    SHORT_VIDEO = "short_video"
11	    LONG_VIDEO = "long_video"
12	    BLOG_POST = "blog_post"
13	
14	
15	class ContentStatus(str, Enum):
16	    DRAFT = "draft"
17	    VALIDATED = "validated"
18	    SCHEDULED = "scheduled"
19	    PUBLISHED = "published"
20	
21	
22	class ContentItem(SQLModel, table=True):
23	    __tablename__ = "content_items"
24	
25	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid4()), primary_key=True)
26	    title: str
27	    body: str = ""
28	    content_type: ContentType
29	    status: ContentStatus = ContentStatus.DRAFT
30	    language: str = "pt"
31	    channels: str = "[]"  # JSON list of channel strings, e.g. '["youtube", "blog"]'
32	    ai_model_used: Optional[str] = None
33	    scheduled_date: Optional[datetime] = None
34	    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
35	    updated_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
36	    version: int = 1
37	
38	
39	class ContentVersion(SQLModel, table=True):
40	    __tablename__ = "content_versions"
41	
42	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid4()), primary_key=True)
43	    content_id: str = Field(foreign_key="content_items.id", index=True)
44	    version_number: int
45	    title: str
46	    body: str = ""
47	    ai_model_used: Optional[str] = None
48	    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
49	
50	
51	class SEOMetadata(SQLModel, table=True):
52	    __tablename__ = "seo_metadata"
53	
54	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid4()), primary_key=True)
55	    content_id: str = Field(foreign_key="content_items.id", unique=True, index=True)
56	    meta_title: str = ""
57	    meta_description: str = ""
58	    keywords: str = "[]"  # JSON list of keyword strings
59	    slug: str = ""
60	    readability_score: Optional[float] = None
61	    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
62	    updated_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
63

1	import json
2	from typing import Optional
3	
4	from fastapi import APIRouter, HTTPException, Query
5	
6	from app.core.deps import SessionDep
7	from app.models.content import ContentStatus, ContentType
8	from app.schemas.content import (
9	    ContentCreate,
10	    ContentListParams,
11	    ContentListResponse,
12	    ContentResponse,
13	    ContentUpdate,
14	    ContentVersionResponse,
15	    StatusTransition,
16	)
17	from app.services.content_service import ContentService
18	
19	router = APIRouter()
20	
21	
22	def _to_response(item) -> ContentResponse:
23	    return ContentResponse(
24	        id=item.id,
25	        title=item.title,
26	        body=item.body,
27	        content_type=item.content_type,
28	        status=item.status,
29	        language=item.language,
30	        channels=json.loads(item.channels),
31	        ai_model_used=item.ai_model_used,
32	        scheduled_date=item.scheduled_date,
33	        created_at=item.created_at,
34	        updated_at=item.updated_at,
35	        version=item.version,
36	    )
37	
38	
39	@router.get("", response_model=ContentListResponse)
40	async def list_content(
41	    session: SessionDep,
42	    status: Optional[ContentStatus] = Query(None),
43	    content_type: Optional[ContentType] = Query(None),
44	    channel: Optional[str] = Query(None),
45	):
46	    service = ContentService(session)
47	    params = ContentListParams(status=status, content_type=content_type, channel=channel)
48	    items, total = await service.list(params)
49	    return ContentListResponse(
50	        items=[_to_response(item) for item in items],
51	        total=total,
52	    )
53	
54	
55	@router.post("", response_model=ContentResponse, status_code=201)
56	async def create_content(data: ContentCreate, session: SessionDep):
57	    service = ContentService(session)
58	    item = await service.create(data)
59	    return _to_response(item)
60	
61	
62	@router.get("/{content_id}", response_model=ContentResponse)
63	async def get_content(content_id: str, session: SessionDep):
64	    service = ContentService(session)
65	    item = await service.get(content_id)
66	    if item is None:
67	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Content item not found")
68	    return _to_response(item)
69	
70	
71	@router.put("/{content_id}", response_model=ContentResponse)
72	async def update_content(content_id: str, data: ContentUpdate, session: SessionDep):
73	    service = ContentService(session)
74	    item = await service.update(content_id, data)
75	    if item is None:
76	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Content item not found")
77	    return _to_response(item)
78	
79	
80	@router.delete("/{content_id}", status_code=204)
81	async def delete_content(content_id: str, session: SessionDep):
82	    service = ContentService(session)
83	    deleted = await service.delete(content_id)
84	    if not deleted:
85	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Content item not found")
86	
87	
88	@router.get("/{content_id}/versions", response_model=list[ContentVersionResponse])
89	async def get_versions(content_id: str, session: SessionDep):
90	    service = ContentService(session)
91	    item = await service.get(content_id)
92	    if item is None:
93	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Content item not found")
94	    versions = await service.get_versions(content_id)
95	    return versions
96	
97	
98	@router.post("/{content_id}/status", response_model=ContentResponse)
99	async def transition_status(
100	    content_id: str, data: StatusTransition, session: SessionDep
101	):
102	    service = ContentService(session)
103	    try:
104	        item = await service.transition_status(content_id, data.new_status)
105	    except ValueError as e:
106	        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail=str(e))
107	    return _to_response(item)

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	import json
4	from datetime import datetime, timezone
5	from typing import Optional
6	
7	from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession
8	from sqlmodel import select
9	
10	from app.models.content import ContentItem, ContentStatus, ContentType, ContentVersion
11	from app.schemas.content import ContentCreate, ContentListParams, ContentUpdate
12	
13	
14	# Valid status transitions: draft→validated→scheduled→published
15	VALID_TRANSITIONS: dict[ContentStatus, list[ContentStatus]] = {
16	    ContentStatus.DRAFT: [ContentStatus.VALIDATED],
17	    ContentStatus.VALIDATED: [ContentStatus.SCHEDULED, ContentStatus.DRAFT],
18	    ContentStatus.SCHEDULED: [ContentStatus.PUBLISHED, ContentStatus.VALIDATED],
19	    ContentStatus.PUBLISHED: [],
20	}
21	
22	
23	class ContentService:
24	    def __init__(self, session: AsyncSession):
25	        self.session = session
26	
27	    async def create(self, data: ContentCreate) -> ContentItem:
28	        item = ContentItem(
29	            title=data.title,
30	            body=data.body,
31	            content_type=data.content_type,
32	            language=data.language,
33	            channels=json.dumps(data.channels),
34	            ai_model_used=data.ai_model_used,
35	        )
36	        self.session.add(item)
37	        await self.session.commit()
38	        await self.session.refresh(item)
39	        return item
40	
41	    async def get(self, content_id: str) -> Optional[ContentItem]:
42	        return await self.session.get(ContentItem, content_id)
43	
44	    async def list(self, params: ContentListParams) -> tuple[list[ContentItem], int]:
45	        statement = select(ContentItem)
46	
47	        if params.status is not None:
48	            statement = statement.where(ContentItem.status == params.status)
49	        if params.content_type is not None:
50	            statement = statement.where(ContentItem.content_type == params.content_type)
51	        if params.channel is not None:
52	            statement = statement.where(ContentItem.channels.contains(params.channel))
53	
54	        statement = statement.order_by(ContentItem.updated_at.desc())
55	
56	        result = await self.session.exec(statement)
57	        items = list(result.all())
58	        return items, len(items)
59	
60	    async def update(self, content_id: str, data: ContentUpdate) -> Optional[ContentItem]:
61	        item = await self.get(content_id)
62	        if item is None:
63	            return None
64	
65	        # Create version snapshot before updating
66	        await self._create_version_snapshot(item)
67	
68	        if data.title is not None:
69	            item.title = data.title
70	        if data.body is not None:
71	            item.body = data.body
72	        if data.channels is not None:
73	            item.channels = json.dumps(data.channels)
74	        if data.ai_model_used is not None:
75	            item.ai_model_used = data.ai_model_used
76	
77	        item.version += 1
78	        item.updated_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
79	
80	        self.session.add(item)
81	        await self.session.commit()
82	        await self.session.refresh(item)
83	        return item
84	
85	    async def delete(self, content_id: str) -> bool:
86	        item = await self.get(content_id)
87	        if item is None:
88	            return False
89	        await self.session.delete(item)
90	        await self.session.commit()
91	        return True
92	
93	    async def transition_status(
94	        self, content_id: str, new_status: ContentStatus
95	    ) -> ContentItem:
96	        item = await self.get(content_id)
97	        if item is None:
98	            raise ValueError(f"Content item '{content_id}' not found")
99	
100	        allowed = VALID_TRANSITIONS.get(item.status, [])
101	        if new_status not in allowed:
102	            raise ValueError(
103	                f"Invalid status transition from '{item.status.value}' to '{new_status.value}'. "
104	                f"Allowed transitions: {[s.value for s in allowed]}"
105	            )
106	
107	        item.status = new_status
108	        item.updated_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
109	
110	        self.session.add(item)
111	        await self.session.commit()
112	        await self.session.refresh(item)
113	        return item
114	
115	    async def get_versions(self, content_id: str) -> list[ContentVersion]:
116	        statement = (
117	            select(ContentVersion)
118	            .where(ContentVersion.content_id == content_id)
119	            .order_by(ContentVersion.version_number.desc())
120	        )
121	        result = await self.session.exec(statement)
122	        return list(result.all())
123	
124	    async def _create_version_snapshot(self, item: ContentItem) -> ContentVersion:
125	        version = ContentVersion(
126	            content_id=item.id,
127	            version_number=item.version,
128	            title=item.title,
129	            body=item.body,
130	            ai_model_used=item.ai_model_used,
131	        )
132	        self.session.add(version)
133	        return version
134

1	from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession, create_async_engine
2	from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker
3	from sqlmodel import SQLModel
4	
5	from app.core.config import get_settings
6	
7	settings = get_settings()
8	
9	engine = create_async_engine(
10	    settings.database_url,
11	    echo=settings.debug,
12	    future=True,
13	)
14	
15	async_session_factory = sessionmaker(
16	    engine,
17	    class_=AsyncSession,
18	    expire_on_commit=False,
19	)
20	
21	
22	async def init_db() -> None:
23	    async with engine.begin() as conn:
24	        await conn.run_sync(SQLModel.metadata.create_all)
25	
26	
27	async def get_session() -> AsyncSession:
28	    async with async_session_factory() as session:
29	        yield session
30

1	import logging
2	from functools import lru_cache
3	from typing import Annotated
4	
5	from fastapi import Depends
6	from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession
7	
8	from app.core.config import Settings, get_settings
9	from app.core.database import get_session
10	from app.services.ai_service import AIService
11	from app.services.validation_service import ValidationService
12	
13	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
14	
15	SessionDep = Annotated[AsyncSession, Depends(get_session)]
16	SettingsDep = Annotated[Settings, Depends(get_settings)]
17	
18	
19	@lru_cache
20	def get_ai_service() -> AIService:
21	    """Create and cache the AIService with available providers."""
22	    settings = get_settings()
23	    providers: dict = {}
24	
25	    if settings.anthropic_api_key:
26	        try:
27	            from app.services.claude_provider import ClaudeProvider
28	
29	            providers["claude"] = ClaudeProvider(api_key=settings.anthropic_api_key)
30	            logger.info("Claude provider registered")
31	        except Exception as exc:
32	            logger.warning("Failed to initialize Claude provider: %s", exc)
33	
34	    if settings.openai_api_key:
35	        try:
36	            from app.services.openai_provider import OpenAIProvider
37	
38	            providers["openai"] = OpenAIProvider(api_key=settings.openai_api_key)
39	            logger.info("OpenAI provider registered")
40	        except Exception as exc:
41	            logger.warning("Failed to initialize OpenAI provider: %s", exc)
42	
43	    return AIService(providers=providers)
44	
45	
46	AIServiceDep = Annotated[AIService, Depends(get_ai_service)]
47	
48	
49	@lru_cache
50	def get_validation_service() -> ValidationService:
51	    """Create and cache the ValidationService with primary and fallback providers."""
52	    settings = get_settings()
53	
54	    # Primary provider: NotebookLM
55	    try:
56	        from app.services.notebooklm_provider import NotebookLMProvider
57	
58	        primary = NotebookLMProvider(api_key=getattr(settings, "notebooklm_api_key", "") or "")
59	        logger.info("NotebookLM validation provider registered")
60	    except Exception as exc:
61	        logger.warning("Failed to initialize NotebookLM provider: %s", exc)
62	        primary = None
63	
64	    # Fallback provider: AI-based validation
65	    try:
66	        from app.services.fallback_validation_provider import FallbackValidationProvider
67	
68	        fallback = FallbackValidationProvider()
69	        logger.info("Fallback validation provider registered")
70	    except Exception as exc:
71	        logger.warning("Failed to initialize fallback validation provider: %s", exc)
72	        fallback = None
73	
74	    if primary is None and fallback is None:
75	        raise RuntimeError("No validation providers available")
76	
77	    # If primary is missing, use fallback as both
78	    if primary is None:
79	        primary = fallback
80	    if fallback is None:
81	        fallback = primary
82	
83	    return ValidationService(primary=primary, fallback=fallback)
84	
85	
86	ValidationServiceDep = Annotated[ValidationService, Depends(get_validation_service)]
87

1	from contextlib import asynccontextmanager
2	
3	from fastapi import FastAPI
4	from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware
5	
6	from app.api import content, dashboard, generation, schedule, seo, validation
7	from app.core.database import init_db
8	
9	
10	@asynccontextmanager
11	async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):
12	    await init_db()
13	    yield
14	
15	
16	app = FastAPI(title="Verbum API", lifespan=lifespan)
17	
18	app.add_middleware(
19	    CORSMiddleware,
20	    allow_origins=["http://localhost:5173"],
21	    allow_credentials=True,
22	    allow_methods=["*"],
23	    allow_headers=["*"],
24	)
25

entries.detail:
0	# Specification: Verbum — Catholic Content Management Platform
1	
2	## Overview
3	
4	Verbum is a greenfield, single-user Catholic content management platform for Ramir, a Portuguese-speaking Catholic content creator. The platform centralizes the full content lifecycle — from AI-assisted generation (via Anthropic Claude and OpenAI ChatGPT) through theological validation (via NotebookLM), editorial scheduling, and multi-channel delivery. It supports three content types: short-form video scripts (1 min), long-form video scripts (10 min), and SEO-optimized blog posts in Portuguese. The goal is to reduce time-to-publish by 50%, ensure 100% doctrinal accuracy through automated validation, and maintain a consistent publishing cadence of at least 3 pieces per week. The entire system is designed for a single user — no multi-tenancy, no scaling concerns, no monetization.
5	
6	## Workflow Type
7	
8	**Type**: feature
9	
10	**Rationale**: This is a brand-new full-stack application being built from scratch within an empty repository. There is no existing application logic to refactor or migrate — only a Vite/React/Tailwind prototype shell (`prototypes-magic/`) with reusable UI components. Every layer (database schema, backend API, service integrations, frontend pages) must be designed and implemented.
11	
12	## Task Scope
13	
14	### Services Involved
15	- **verbum-backend** (primary) — Python FastAPI backend handling API routing, AI integration orchestration, NotebookLM validation, database persistence, and scheduling logic
16	- **verbum-frontend** (primary) — React TypeScript SPA providing the dashboard, content editor, validation panel, editorial calendar, and SEO tools
17	- **SQLite database** (infrastructure) — Embedded file-based database for content items, validation records, scheduling metadata, and version history
18	
19	### This Task Will:
20	- [ ] Set up the full project structure (backend + frontend monorepo)
21	- [ ] Implement the Python FastAPI backend with async architecture
22	- [ ] Design and create the SQLite database schema via SQLModel
23	- [ ] Integrate Anthropic Claude API for AI content generation with streaming
24	- [ ] Integrate OpenAI ChatGPT API as an alternate drafting engine with streaming
25	- [ ] Integrate NotebookLM (via `notebooklm-py`) for theological validation with citation retrieval
26	- [ ] Build a validation abstraction layer to enable graceful degradation and provider swapping
27	- [ ] Create the React frontend with dashboard, content editor, validation panel, and calendar views
28	- [ ] Implement the full content lifecycle: draft → validated → scheduled → published
29	- [ ] Add Portuguese SEO metadata tools for blog post optimization
30	- [ ] Implement content versioning and validation history tracking
31	- [ ] Add iCal export for editorial calendar reminders
32	- [ ] Implement environment-based configuration with secure API key management
33	
34	### Out of Scope:
35	- Custom Catholic knowledge base or RAG pipeline (NotebookLM handles this)
36	- Social media analytics or community management
37	- Monetization or revenue features
38	- Multi-user authentication or multi-tenancy
39	- Direct Google Calendar API integration (using .ics export instead)
40	- Bulk social media publishing automation
41	- Mobile native app (responsive web only)
42	- Docker containerization (single-user local/hosted deployment)
43	
44	## Service Context
45	
46	### verbum-backend
47	
48	**Tech Stack:**
49	- Language: Python 3.11+
50	- Framework: FastAPI >= 0.135.0 (with `[standard]` extras for uvicorn)
51	- ORM: SQLModel >= 0.0.37 (Pydantic + SQLAlchemy combined)
52	- Database: SQLite (via aiosqlite for async operations)
53	- AI SDKs: `anthropic >= 0.88.0`, `openai >= 2.30.0`
54	- Validation: `notebooklm-py[browser] >= 0.3.4` (UNOFFICIAL — HIGH RISK)
55	- Calendar: `icalendar` for .ics export
56	- Config: `python-dotenv` for environment variable management
57	- Key directories: `backend/app/`, `backend/app/api/`, `backend/app/models/`, `backend/app/services/`, `backend/app/core/`
58	
59	**Entry Point:** `backend/app/main.py`
60	
61	**How to Run:**
62	```bash
63	cd backend
64	pip install -r requirements.txt
65	uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8000
66	```
67	
68	**Port:** 8000
69	
70	---
71	
72	### verbum-frontend
73	
74	**Tech Stack:**
75	- Language: TypeScript 5.5+
76	- Framework: React 18.3
77	- Bundler: Vite 5.2
78	- Styling: Tailwind CSS 3.4
79	- Icons: lucide-react 0.522
80	- UI Components: Reuse from `prototypes-magic/src/components/ui/` (Button, Input, Select, Card, Tabs, Badge, Table, Textarea, Progress, etc.)
81	- Charts: recharts (already in prototype dependencies)
82	- Key directories: `frontend/src/`, `frontend/src/components/`, `frontend/src/pages/`, `frontend/src/services/`, `frontend/src/hooks/`
83	
84	**Entry Point:** `frontend/src/main.tsx`
85	
86	**How to Run:**
87	```bash
88	cd frontend
89	npm install
90	npm run dev
91	```
92	
93	**Port:** 5173 (Vite default)
94	
95	## Files to Create
96	
97	### Backend Structure
98	
99	| File | Service | Purpose |

./backend/app/schemas/generation.py
./backend/app/schemas/validation.py

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	from typing import AsyncIterator, Protocol, runtime_checkable
4	
5	
6	@runtime_checkable
7	class AIProvider(Protocol):
8	    """Protocol for AI content generation providers."""
9	
10	    async def generate(
11	        self, prompt: str, system_prompt: str, max_tokens: int
12	    ) -> str: ...
13	
14	    async def generate_stream(
15	        self, prompt: str, system_prompt: str, max_tokens: int
16	    ) -> AsyncIterator[str]: ...
17	
18	
19	class AIService:
20	    """Abstraction layer that routes generation requests to registered providers."""
21	
22	    def __init__(self, providers: dict[str, AIProvider]) -> None:
23	        self._providers = providers
24	
25	    @property
26	    def available_models(self) -> list[str]:
27	        return list(self._providers.keys())
28	
29	    def get_provider(self, model_key: str) -> AIProvider:
30	        provider = self._providers.get(model_key)
31	        if provider is None:
32	            raise ValueError(
33	                f"Unknown model '{model_key}'. "
34	                f"Available models: {self.available_models}"
35	            )
36	        return provider
37	
38	    async def generate(
39	        self,
40	        model_key: str,
41	        prompt: str,
42	        system_prompt: str,
43	        max_tokens: int = 2048,
44	    ) -> str:
45	        provider = self.get_provider(model_key)
46	        return await provider.generate(prompt, system_prompt, max_tokens)
47	
48	    async def generate_stream(
49	        self,
50	        model_key: str,
51	        prompt: str,
52	        system_prompt: str,
53	        max_tokens: int = 2048,
54	    ) -> AsyncIterator[str]:
55	        provider = self.get_provider(model_key)
56	        async for chunk in provider.generate_stream(prompt, system_prompt, max_tokens):
57	            yield chunk
58

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	import logging
4	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
5	from typing import Protocol, runtime_checkable
6	
7	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
8	
9	
10	@dataclass
11	class Citation:
12	    """A single citation from a validation source."""
13	
14	    citation_number: int
15	    source_id: str
16	    cited_text: str
17	
18	
19	@dataclass
20	class ValidationResult:
21	    """Structured result from a validation provider."""
22	
23	    score: float  # 0.0 to 1.0
24	    flags: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
25	    citations: list[Citation] = field(default_factory=list)
26	    provider: str = ""  # "notebooklm" or "ai_fallback"
27	    raw_answer: str = ""
28	
29	
30	@runtime_checkable
31	class ValidationProvider(Protocol):
32	    """Protocol for theological validation providers."""
33	
34	    async def validate(self, content: str, content_type: str) -> ValidationResult: ...
35	
36	    async def is_available(self) -> bool: ...
37	
38	
39	class ValidationService:
40	    """Validation abstraction layer with primary→fallback failover logic."""
41	
42	    def __init__(
43	        self, primary: ValidationProvider, fallback: ValidationProvider
44	    ) -> None:
45	        self._primary = primary
46	        self._fallback = fallback
47	
48	    async def validate(
49	        self, content: str, content_type: str
50	    ) -> ValidationResult:
51	        if await self._primary.is_available():
52	            try:
53	                return await self._primary.validate(content, content_type)
54	            except Exception:
55	                logger.warning(
56	                    "Primary validation provider failed, falling back",
57	                    exc_info=True,
58	                )
59	        return await self._fallback.validate(content, content_type)
60

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	from datetime import datetime, timezone
4	from typing import Optional
5	
6	from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession
7	from sqlmodel import select
8	
9	from app.models.content import ContentItem, ContentStatus
10	from app.models.schedule import ScheduleEntry, ScheduleStatus
11	
12	
13	class ScheduleService:
14	    def __init__(self, session: AsyncSession):
15	        self.session = session
16	
17	    async def get_calendar(
18	        self, start: datetime, end: datetime, channel: Optional[str] = None
19	    ) -> list[ScheduleEntry]:
20	        """Get scheduled items within a date range, optionally filtered by channel."""
21	        statement = select(ScheduleEntry).where(
22	            ScheduleEntry.scheduled_date >= start,
23	            ScheduleEntry.scheduled_date <= end,
24	            ScheduleEntry.status != ScheduleStatus.CANCELLED,
25	        )
26	        if channel:
27	            statement = statement.where(ScheduleEntry.channel == channel)
28	        statement = statement.order_by(ScheduleEntry.scheduled_date)
29	        result = await self.session.exec(statement)
30	        return list(result.all())
31	
32	    async def schedule(
33	        self, content_id: str, scheduled_date: datetime, channel: str
34	    ) -> ScheduleEntry:
35	        """Schedule a content item for publishing on a given date and channel."""
36	        item = await self.session.get(ContentItem, content_id)
37	        if item is None:
38	            raise ValueError(f"Content item {content_id} not found")
39	
40	        if item.status not in (ContentStatus.VALIDATED, ContentStatus.SCHEDULED):
41	            raise ValueError(
42	                f"Content must be validated before scheduling (current: {item.status})"
43	            )
44	
45	        entry = ScheduleEntry(
46	            content_id=content_id,
47	            scheduled_date=scheduled_date,
48	            channel=channel,
49	        )
50	        self.session.add(entry)
51	
52	        item.status = ContentStatus.SCHEDULED
53	        item.scheduled_date = scheduled_date
54	        item.updated_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
55	        self.session.add(item)
56	
57	        await self.session.commit()
58	        await self.session.refresh(entry)
59	        return entry
60	
61	    async def reschedule(
62	        self, content_id: str, new_date: datetime, channel: Optional[str] = None
63	    ) -> ScheduleEntry:
64	        """Reschedule a content item to a new date (and optionally new channel)."""
65	        statement = select(ScheduleEntry).where(
66	            ScheduleEntry.content_id == content_id,
67	            ScheduleEntry.status == ScheduleStatus.PENDING,
68	        )
69	        result = await self.session.exec(statement)
70	        entry = result.first()
71	        if entry is None:
72	            raise ValueError(
73	                f"No pending schedule entry found for content {content_id}"
74	            )
75	
76	        entry.scheduled_date = new_date
77	        if channel:
78	            entry.channel = channel
79	        entry.updated_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
80	        self.session.add(entry)
81	
82	        item = await self.session.get(ContentItem, content_id)
83	        if item:
84	            item.scheduled_date = new_date
85	            item.updated_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
86	            self.session.add(item)
87	
88	        await self.session.commit()
89	        await self.session.refresh(entry)
90	        return entry
91	
92	    async def export_ical(
93	        self, start: Optional[datetime] = None, end: Optional[datetime] = None
94	    ) -> str:
95	        """Export scheduled items as an iCal (.ics) string."""
96	        from icalendar import Calendar, Event
97	
98	        statement = select(ScheduleEntry).where(
99	            ScheduleEntry.status != ScheduleStatus.CANCELLED
100	        )
101	        if start:
102	            statement = statement.where(ScheduleEntry.scheduled_date >= start)
103	        if end:
104	            statement = statement.where(ScheduleEntry.scheduled_date <= end)
105	        statement = statement.order_by(ScheduleEntry.scheduled_date)
106	
107	        result = await self.session.exec(statement)
108	        entries = list(result.all())
109	
110	        cal = Calendar()
111	        cal.add("prodid", "-//Verbum Content Calendar//EN")
112	        cal.add("version", "2.0")
113	        cal.add("calscale", "GREGORIAN")
114	
115	        for entry in entries:
116	            item = await self.session.get(ContentItem, entry.content_id)
117	            title = item.title if item else f"Content {entry.content_id}"
118	
119	            event = Event()
120	            event.add("uid", f"{entry.id}@verbum")
121	            event.add("dtstart", entry.scheduled_date)
122	            event.add("summary", f"[{entry.channel}] {title}")
123	            event.add("description", f"Channel: {entry.channel}\nStatus: {entry.status}")
124	            event.add("created", entry.created_at)
125	            cal.add_component(event)
126	
127	        return cal.to_ical().decode("utf-8")
128

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	import json
4	import re
5	from datetime import datetime, timezone
6	from typing import Optional
7	
8	from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession
9	from sqlmodel import select
10	
11	from app.models.content import ContentItem, SEOMetadata
12	from app.services.ai_service import AIService
13	
14	
15	# Portuguese common/simple words (stop words + high-frequency words)
16	_PORTUGUESE_SIMPLE_WORDS = {
17	    "a", "o", "e", "de", "do", "da", "em", "um", "uma", "que", "é", "para",
18	    "com", "não", "se", "os", "as", "no", "na", "por", "mais", "dos", "das",
19	    "foi", "como", "mas", "ao", "ele", "ela", "seu", "sua", "ou", "ser",
20	    "quando", "muito", "há", "nos", "já", "está", "eu", "também", "só",
21	    "pelo", "pela", "até", "isso", "entre", "era", "depois", "sem", "mesmo",
22	    "aos", "ter", "seus", "quem", "nas", "me", "esse", "eles", "estão",
23	    "você", "tinha", "foram", "essa", "num", "nem", "suas", "meu", "às",
24	    "minha", "têm", "numa", "sobre", "tem", "nos", "às", "este", "esta",
25	}
26	
27	# Average syllables threshold for "complex" Portuguese words
28	_COMPLEX_WORD_SYLLABLE_THRESHOLD = 4
29	
30	
31	def _count_syllables_pt(word: str) -> int:
32	    """Estimate syllable count for a Portuguese word using vowel groups."""
33	    word = word.lower().strip()
34	    # Count vowel groups (including accented vowels)
35	    vowel_groups = re.findall(r"[aeiouáéíóúâêôãõàü]+", word)
36	    return max(len(vowel_groups), 1)
37	
38	
39	def _split_sentences(text: str) -> list[str]:
40	    """Split text into sentences."""
41	    sentences = re.split(r"[.!?]+", text)
42	    return [s.strip() for s in sentences if s.strip()]
43	
44	
45	def _extract_words(text: str) -> list[str]:
46	    """Extract words from text."""
47	    return re.findall(r"\b[a-záéíóúâêôãõàüç]+\b", text.lower())
48	
49	
50	def calculate_readability_score(text: str) -> float:
51	    """
52	    Calculate a Portuguese readability score (0-100).
53	
54	    Based on sentence length and word complexity:
55	    - Shorter sentences score higher
56	    - Fewer complex words score higher
57	    - 100 = very easy to read, 0 = very difficult
58	    """
59	    if not text or not text.strip():
60	        return 0.0
61	
62	    sentences = _split_sentences(text)
63	    if not sentences:
64	        return 0.0
65	
66	    words = _extract_words(text)
67	    if not words:
68	        return 0.0
69	
70	    # Average sentence length (words per sentence)
71	    avg_sentence_length = len(words) / len(sentences)
72	
73	    # Percentage of complex words (>= 4 syllables, not in simple word list)
74	    complex_count = sum(
75	        1
76	        for w in words
77	        if w not in _PORTUGUESE_SIMPLE_WORDS
78	        and _count_syllables_pt(w) >= _COMPLEX_WORD_SYLLABLE_THRESHOLD
79	    )
80	    complex_pct = (complex_count / len(words)) * 100 if words else 0
81	
82	    # Score formula (inspired by Flesch for Portuguese)
83	    # Ideal: avg_sentence_length ~15, complex_pct ~5%
84	    score = 100 - (avg_sentence_length * 1.5) - (complex_pct * 2.0)
85	    return round(max(0.0, min(100.0, score)), 1)
86	
87	
88	class SEOService:
89	    def __init__(self, session: AsyncSession, ai_service: Optional[AIService] = None):
90	        self.session = session
91	        self.ai_service = ai_service
92	
93	    async def get_metadata(self, content_id: str) -> Optional[SEOMetadata]:
94	        """Get SEO metadata for a content item."""
95	        statement = select(SEOMetadata).where(SEOMetadata.content_id == content_id)
96	        result = await self.session.exec(statement)
97	        return result.first()
98	
99	    async def upsert_metadata(
100	        self,
101	        content_id: str,
102	        meta_title: Optional[str] = None,
103	        meta_description: Optional[str] = None,
104	        keywords: Optional[list[str]] = None,
105	        slug: Optional[str] = None,
106	    ) -> SEOMetadata:
107	        """Create or update SEO metadata for a content item."""
108	        metadata = await self.get_metadata(content_id)
109	
110	        if metadata is None:
111	            metadata = SEOMetadata(content_id=content_id)
112	            self.session.add(metadata)
113	
114	        if meta_title is not None:
115	            metadata.meta_title = meta_title
116	        if meta_description is not None:
117	            metadata.meta_description = meta_description
118	        if keywords is not None:
119	            metadata.keywords = json.dumps(keywords)
120	        if slug is not None:
121	            metadata.slug = slug
122	
123	        metadata.updated_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
124	
125	        await self.session.commit()
126	        await self.session.refresh(metadata)
127	        return metadata
128	
129	    async def analyze(self, content_id: str) -> dict:
130	        """Analyze content for SEO readability and update the score."""
131	        content = await self.session.get(ContentItem, content_id)
132	        if content is None:
133	            raise ValueError(f"Content item '{content_id}' not found")
134	
135	        score = calculate_readability_score(content.body)
136	
137	        # Ensure metadata record exists and update score
138	        metadata = await self.get_metadata(content_id)
139	        if metadata is None:
140	            metadata = SEOMetadata(content_id=content_id, readability_score=score)
141	            self.session.add(metadata)
142	        else:
143	            metadata.readability_score = score
144	            metadata.updated_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
145	
146	        await self.session.commit()
147	        await self.session.refresh(metadata)
148	
149	        sentences = _split_sentences(content.body)
150	        words = _extract_words(content.body)
151	
152	        return {
153	            "content_id": content_id,
154	            "readability_score": score,
155	            "word_count": len(words),
156	            "sentence_count": len(sentences),
157	            "avg_sentence_length": round(len(words) / max(len(sentences), 1), 1),
158	        }
159	
160	    async def suggest_keywords(self, content_id: str) -> list[str]:
161	        """Use AI to suggest SEO keywords for the content."""
162	        content = await self.session.get(ContentItem, content_id)
163	        if content is None:
164	            raise ValueError(f"Content item '{content_id}' not found")
165	
166	        if self.ai_service is None or not self.ai_service.available_models:
167	            raise ValueError("No AI provider available for keyword suggestions")
168	
169	        model_key = self.ai_service.available_models[0]
170	
171	        prompt = (
172	            f"Analise o seguinte conteúdo em português e sugira de 5 a 10 palavras-chave "
173	            f"para SEO. Retorne APENAS as palavras-chave, uma por linha, sem numeração.\n\n"
174	            f"Título: {content.title}\n\n"
175	            f"Conteúdo: {content.body[:2000]}"
176	        )
177	        system_prompt = (
178	            "Você é um especialista em SEO para conteúdo católico em português brasileiro. "
179	            "Responda apenas com as palavras-chave, uma por linha."
180	        )
181	
182	        response = await self.ai_service.generate(
183	            model_key=model_key,
184	            prompt=prompt,
185	            system_prompt=system_prompt,
186	            max_tokens=256,
187	        )
188	
189	        keywords = [
190	            line.strip()
191	            for line in response.strip().splitlines()
192	            if line.strip()
193	        ]
194	        return keywords
195

85	                model=self._model,

87	                messages=[
88	                    {"role": "system", "content": system_prompt},
89	                    {"role": "user", "content": prompt},
90	                ],
91	            )
92	            return response.choices[0].message.content or ""
93	        except RateLimitError:

95	            raise

2	
3	import json
4	import logging
5	import re
6	
7	from anthropic import AsyncAnthropic
8	
9	from app.core.config import get_settings
10	from app.services.validation_service import Citation, ValidationResult
11	
12	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
13	
14	_SYSTEM_PROMPT = """\
15	Você é um teólogo católico especialista em doutrina e magistério da Igreja Católica. \
16	Sua tarefa é analisar conteúdo católico quanto à precisão doutrinária, identificando \
17	quaisquer erros, imprecisões ou afirmações que contradizem o ensinamento oficial da \
18	Igreja conforme expresso no Catecismo da Igreja Católica, documentos do Concílio \
19	Vaticano II, encíclicas papais e outros documentos do Magistério.
20	
21	Responda SEMPRE em JSON válido com a seguinte estrutura:
22	{
23	  "score": <número de 0 a 100 indicando precisão doutrinária>,
24	  "flags": [<lista de strings descrevendo problemas encontrados, vazia se nenhum>],
25	  "citations": [
26	    {
27	      "source": "<documento de referência, ex: CIC 1234, Lumen Gentium 12>",
28	      "text": "<trecho relevante da fonte>"
29	    }
30	  ],
31	  "analysis": "<breve análise geral do conteúdo>"
32	}
33	
34	Seja rigoroso mas justo. Conteúdo opinativo ou devocional que não contradiz a doutrina \
35	deve receber pontuação alta. Apenas sinalize erros claros ou imprecisões significativas.\
36	"""
37	
38	_USER_PROMPT_TEMPLATE = (
39	    "Analise o seguinte conteúdo católico (tipo: {content_type}) quanto à "
40	    "precisão doutrinária. Responda em JSON conforme instruído.\n\n"
41	    "---\n{content}\n---"
42	)
43	
44	_DEFAULT_MODEL = "claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
45	
46	
47	class FallbackValidationProvider:
48	    """AI-based fallback validation provider using Claude.
49	
50	    Used when the primary NotebookLM provider is unavailable.
51	    Always returns ``provider='ai_fallback'`` to indicate the result
52	    is from a non-authoritative source.
53	    """
54	
55	    def __init__(
56	        self,
57	        api_key: str | None = None,
58	        model: str | None = None,
59	    ) -> None:
60	        settings = get_settings()
61	        self._api_key = api_key or settings.anthropic_api_key
62	        self._model = model or _DEFAULT_MODEL
63	        self._client: AsyncAnthropic | None = None
64	
65	    def _get_client(self) -> AsyncAnthropic:
66	        if self._client is None:
67	            if not self._api_key:
68	                raise ValueError(
69	                    "Anthropic API key is required for fallback validation. "
70	                    "Set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in your environment."
71	                )
72	            self._client = AsyncAnthropic(api_key=self._api_key)
73	        return self._client
74	
75	    async def is_available(self) -> bool:
76	        """The fallback provider is available whenever an API key is configured."""
77	        return bool(self._api_key)
78	
79	    async def validate(
80	        self, content: str, content_type: str
81	    ) -> ValidationResult:
82	        """Validate content using Claude as a theological expert."""
83	        client = self._get_client()
84	
85	        user_prompt = _USER_PROMPT_TEMPLATE.format(
86	            content_type=content_type,
87	            content=content,
88	        )
89	
90	        try:
91	            message = await client.messages.create(
92	                model=self._model,
93	                max_tokens=2048,
94	                system=_SYSTEM_PROMPT,
95	                messages=[{"role": "user", "content": user_prompt}],
96	            )
97	            raw_answer = message.content[0].text
98	        except Exception as exc:
99	            logger.error("Fallback validation AI call failed: %s", exc)
100	            raise
101	
102	        return self._parse_response(raw_answer)
103	
104	    def _parse_response(self, raw_answer: str) -> ValidationResult:
105	        """Parse the JSON response from Claude into a ValidationResult."""
106	        try:
107	            # Extract JSON from response (may be wrapped in markdown code block)
108	            json_match = re.search(r"\{[\s\S]*\}", raw_answer)
109	            if not json_match:
110	                raise ValueError("No JSON object found in response")
111	            data = json.loads(json_match.group())
112	        except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValueError):
113	            logger.warning("Failed to parse fallback validation JSON, using defaults")
114	            return ValidationResult(
115	                score=0.5,
116	                flags=["Não foi possível analisar a resposta da validação"],
117	                citations=[],
118	                provider="ai_fallback",
119	                raw_answer=raw_answer,
120	            )
121	
122	        score_raw = data.get("score", 50)
123	        score = max(0.0, min(1.0, float(score_raw) / 100.0))
124	
125	        flags = [str(f) for f in data.get("flags", [])]
126	
127	        citations: list[Citation] = []
128	        for idx, cit in enumerate(data.get("citations", []), start=1):
129	            citations.append(
130	                Citation(
131	                    citation_number=idx,
132	                    source_id=str(cit.get("source", "")),
133	                    cited_text=str(cit.get("text", "")),
134	                )
135	            )
136	
137	        return ValidationResult(
138	            score=score,
139	            flags=flags,
140	            citations=citations,
141	            provider="ai_fallback",
142	            raw_answer=raw_answer,
143	        )
144

15	    session: SessionDep,
16	    start: datetime = Query(..., description="Start date for calendar range"),

28	    content_id: str,
29	    session: SessionDep,
30	    scheduled_date: datetime = Query(..., description="Date to schedule"),
31	    channel: str = Query(..., description="Publishing channel"),

46	    new_date: datetime = Query(..., description="New scheduled date"),
47	    channel: Optional[str] = Query(None, description="New channel (optional)"),
48	):
49	    """Reschedule a content item."""
50	    service = ScheduleService(session)
51	    try:
52	        entry = await service.reschedule(content_id, new_date, channel)
53	    except ValueError as e:
54	        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail=str(e))
55	    return _entry_to_dict(entry)
56	
57	
58	@router.get("/export/ical")
59	async def export_ical(
60	    session: SessionDep,
61	    start: Optional[datetime] = Query(None),
62	    end: Optional[datetime] = Query(None),
63	):
64	    """Export editorial calendar as iCal (.ics) file."""
65	    service = ScheduleService(session)
66	    ical_data = await service.export_ical(start, end)
67	    return Response(
68	        content=ical_data,
69	        media_type="text/calendar",
70	        headers={"Content-Disposition": "attachment; filename=verbum-calendar.ics"},
71	    )
72	
73	
74	def _entry_to_dict(entry) -> dict:
75	    return {
76	        "id": entry.id,
77	        "content_id": entry.content_id,
78	        "scheduled_date": entry.scheduled_date.isoformat(),
79	        "channel": entry.channel,
80	        "status": entry.status,
81	        "reminder_sent": entry.reminder_sent,
82	        "created_at": entry.created_at.isoformat(),
83	        "updated_at": entry.updated_at.isoformat(),
84	    }

5	from pydantic import BaseModel

7	from app.core.deps import AIServiceDep, SessionDep
8	from app.services.seo_service import SEOService
9	
10	router = APIRouter()
11	
12	
13	class SEOMetadataResponse(BaseModel):
14	    id: str
15	    content_id: str
16	    meta_title: str
17	    meta_description: str
18	    keywords: list[str]
19	    slug: str
20	    readability_score: Optional[float]
21	
22	
23	class SEOMetadataUpdate(BaseModel):
24	    meta_title: Optional[str] = None
25	    meta_description: Optional[str] = None
26	    keywords: Optional[list[str]] = None
27	    slug: Optional[str] = None
28	
29	
30	def _to_response(metadata) -> SEOMetadataResponse:
31	    return SEOMetadataResponse(
32	        id=metadata.id,
33	        content_id=metadata.content_id,
34	        meta_title=metadata.meta_title,
35	        meta_description=metadata.meta_description,
36	        keywords=json.loads(metadata.keywords),
37	        slug=metadata.slug,
38	        readability_score=metadata.readability_score,
39	    )
40	
41	
42	@router.get("/{content_id}", response_model=SEOMetadataResponse)
43	async def get_seo_metadata(content_id: str, session: SessionDep):
44	    """Get SEO metadata for content."""
45	    service = SEOService(session)
46	    metadata = await service.get_metadata(content_id)
47	    if metadata is None:
48	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="SEO metadata not found")
49	    return _to_response(metadata)
50	
51	
52	@router.put("/{content_id}", response_model=SEOMetadataResponse)
53	async def update_seo_metadata(
54	    content_id: str, data: SEOMetadataUpdate, session: SessionDep
55	):
56	    """Update SEO metadata for content."""
57	    service = SEOService(session)
58	    metadata = await service.upsert_metadata(
59	        content_id=content_id,
60	        meta_title=data.meta_title,
61	        meta_description=data.meta_description,
62	        keywords=data.keywords,
63	        slug=data.slug,
64	    )
65	    return _to_response(metadata)
66	
67

69	async def analyze_seo(content_id: str, session: SessionDep):
70	    """Analyze SEO readability for content."""

72	    try:
73	        result = await service.analyze(content_id)
74	    except ValueError as e:
75	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=str(e))
76	    return result

entries.detail:
1	# Specification: Verbum — Catholic Content Management Platform
2	
3	## Overview
4	
5	Verbum is a greenfield, single-user Catholic content management platform for Ramir, a Portuguese-speaking Catholic content creator. The platform centralizes the full content lifecycle — from AI-assisted generation (via Anthropic Claude and OpenAI ChatGPT) through theological validation (via NotebookLM), editorial scheduling, and multi-channel delivery. It supports three content types: short-form video scripts (1 min), long-form video scripts (10 min), and SEO-optimized blog posts in Portuguese. The goal is to reduce time-to-publish by 50%, ensure 100% doctrinal accuracy through automated validation, and maintain a consistent publishing cadence of at least 3 pieces per week. The entire system is designed for a single user — no multi-tenancy, no scaling concerns, no monetization.
6	
7	## Workflow Type
8	
9	**Type**: feature
10	
11	**Rationale**: This is a brand-new full-stack application being built from scratch within an empty repository. There is no existing application logic to refactor or migrate — only a Vite/React/Tailwind prototype shell (`prototypes-magic/`) with reusable UI components. Every layer (database schema, backend API, service integrations, frontend pages) must be designed and implemented.
12	
13	## Task Scope
14	
15	### Services Involved
16	- **verbum-backend** (primary) — Python FastAPI backend handling API routing, AI integration orchestration, NotebookLM validation, database persistence, and scheduling logic
17	- **verbum-frontend** (primary) — React TypeScript SPA providing the dashboard, content editor, validation panel, editorial calendar, and SEO tools
18	- **SQLite database** (infrastructure) — Embedded file-based database for content items, validation records, scheduling metadata, and version history
19	
20	### This Task Will:
21	- [ ] Set up the full project structure (backend + frontend monorepo)
22	- [ ] Implement the Python FastAPI backend with async architecture
23	- [ ] Design and create the SQLite database schema via SQLModel
24	- [ ] Integrate Anthropic Claude API for AI content generation with streaming
25	- [ ] Integrate OpenAI ChatGPT API as an alternate drafting engine with streaming
26	- [ ] Integrate NotebookLM (via `notebooklm-py`) for theological validation with citation retrieval
27	- [ ] Build a validation abstraction layer to enable graceful degradation and provider swapping
28	- [ ] Create the React frontend with dashboard, content editor, validation panel, and calendar views
29	- [ ] Implement the full content lifecycle: draft → validated → scheduled → published
30	- [ ] Add Portuguese SEO metadata tools for blog post optimization
31	- [ ] Implement content versioning and validation history tracking
32	- [ ] Add iCal export for editorial calendar reminders
33	- [ ] Implement environment-based configuration with secure API key management
34	
35	### Out of Scope:
36	- Custom Catholic knowledge base or RAG pipeline (NotebookLM handles this)
37	- Social media analytics or community management
38	- Monetization or revenue features
39	- Multi-user authentication or multi-tenancy
40	- Direct Google Calendar API integration (using .ics export instead)
41	- Bulk social media publishing automation
42	- Mobile native app (responsive web only)
43	- Docker containerization (single-user local/hosted deployment)
44	
45	## Service Context
46	
47	### verbum-backend
48	
49	**Tech Stack:**
50	- Language: Python 3.11+
51	- Framework: FastAPI >= 0.135.0 (with `[standard]` extras for uvicorn)
52	- ORM: SQLModel >= 0.0.37 (Pydantic + SQLAlchemy combined)
53	- Database: SQLite (via aiosqlite for async operations)
54	- AI SDKs: `anthropic >= 0.88.0`, `openai >= 2.30.0`
55	- Validation: `notebooklm-py[browser] >= 0.3.4` (UNOFFICIAL — HIGH RISK)
56	- Calendar: `icalendar` for .ics export
57	- Config: `python-dotenv` for environment variable management
58	- Key directories: `backend/app/`, `backend/app/api/`, `backend/app/models/`, `backend/app/services/`, `backend/app/core/`
59	
60	**Entry Point:** `backend/app/main.py`
61	
62	**How to Run:**
63	```bash
64	cd backend
65	pip install -r requirements.txt
66	uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8000
67	```
68	
69	**Port:** 8000
70	
71	---
72	
73	### verbum-frontend
74	
75	**Tech Stack:**
76	- Language: TypeScript 5.5+
77	- Framework: React 18.3
78	- Bundler: Vite 5.2
79	- Styling: Tailwind CSS 3.4
80	- Icons: lucide-react 0.522
81	- UI Components: Reuse from `prototypes-magic/src/components/ui/` (Button, Input, Select, Card, Tabs, Badge, Table, Textarea, Progress, etc.)
82	- Charts: recharts (already in prototype dependencies)
83	- Key directories: `frontend/src/`, `frontend/src/components/`, `frontend/src/pages/`, `frontend/src/services/`, `frontend/src/hooks/`
84	
85	**Entry Point:** `frontend/src/main.tsx`
86	
87	**How to Run:**
88	```bash
89	cd frontend
90	npm install
91	npm run dev
92	```
93	
94	**Port:** 5173 (Vite default)
95	
96	## Files to Create
97	
98	### Backend Structure
99	
100	| File | Service | Purpose |

1	{
