1	import json
2	import logging
3	
4	from fastapi import APIRouter, HTTPException
5	from fastapi.responses import StreamingResponse
6	
7	from app.core.deps import AIServiceDep
8	from app.schemas.generation import (
9	    GenerationRequest,
10	    GenerationResponse,
11	    ModelInfo,
12	    ModelsResponse,
13	)
14	
15	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
16	
17	router = APIRouter()
18	
19	DEFAULT_SYSTEM_PROMPT = (
20	    "Você é um assistente católico especializado em criar conteúdo "
21	    "fiel à doutrina da Igreja Católica, em português brasileiro."
22	)
23	
24	
25	@router.post("/generate", response_model=GenerationResponse)
26	async def generate_content(request: GenerationRequest, ai_service: AIServiceDep):
27	    """Generate content using AI (full response)."""
28	    system_prompt = request.system_prompt or DEFAULT_SYSTEM_PROMPT
29	    try:
30	        text = await ai_service.generate(
31	            model_key=request.model,
32	            prompt=request.prompt,
33	            system_prompt=system_prompt,
34	            max_tokens=request.max_tokens,
35	        )
36	        return GenerationResponse(text=text, model=request.model)
37	    except ValueError as exc:
38	        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail=str(exc))
39	    except Exception as exc:
40	        logger.error("Generation failed: %s", exc)
41	        raise HTTPException(status_code=502, detail="AI generation failed")
42	
43	
44	@router.post("/stream")
45	async def generate_content_stream(
46	    request: GenerationRequest, ai_service: AIServiceDep
47	):
48	    """Generate content using AI with SSE streaming."""
49	    system_prompt = request.system_prompt or DEFAULT_SYSTEM_PROMPT
50	
51	    try:
52	        ai_service.get_provider(request.model)
53	    except ValueError as exc:
54	        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail=str(exc))
55	
56	    async def event_generator():
57	        try:
58	            async for chunk in ai_service.generate_stream(
59	                model_key=request.model,
60	                prompt=request.prompt,
61	                system_prompt=system_prompt,
62	                max_tokens=request.max_tokens,
63	            ):
64	                yield f"data: {json.dumps({'text': chunk})}\n\n"
65	            yield f"data: {json.dumps({'done': True})}\n\n"
66	        except Exception as exc:
67	            logger.error("Stream generation failed: %s", exc)
68	            yield f"data: {json.dumps({'error': str(exc), 'done': True})}\n\n"
69	
70	    return StreamingResponse(event_generator(), media_type="text/event-stream")
71	
72	
73	@router.get("/models", response_model=ModelsResponse)
74	async def list_models(ai_service: AIServiceDep):
75	    """List available AI models and their status."""
76	    model_names = {"claude": "Anthropic Claude", "openai": "OpenAI ChatGPT"}
77	    available = ai_service.available_models
78	    models = [
79	        ModelInfo(
80	            key=key,
81	            name=model_names.get(key, key),
82	            available=key in available,
83	        )
84	        for key in ["claude", "openai"]
85	    ]
86	    return ModelsResponse(models=models)

1	import json
2	import logging
3	from uuid import uuid4
4	
5	from fastapi import APIRouter, HTTPException
6	from sqlmodel import select
7	
8	from app.core.deps import SessionDep, ValidationServiceDep
9	from app.models.content import ContentItem, ContentStatus, ContentVersion
10	from app.models.validation import ValidationRecord
11	from app.schemas.validation import (
12	    CitationResponse,
13	    ValidationHistoryResponse,
14	    ValidationResponse,
15	    ValidationStatusResponse,
16	)
17	
18	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
19	
20	router = APIRouter()
21	
22	
23	def _record_to_response(record: ValidationRecord) -> ValidationResponse:
24	    """Convert a ValidationRecord to a ValidationResponse."""
25	    return ValidationResponse(
26	        id=record.id,
27	        content_id=record.content_id,
28	        score=record.score,
29	        flags=json.loads(record.flags),
30	        citations=[
31	            CitationResponse(**c) for c in json.loads(record.citations)
32	        ],
33	        provider=record.provider,
34	        raw_answer=record.raw_answer,
35	        content_version=record.content_version,
36	        created_at=record.created_at,
37	    )
38	
39	
40	@router.post("/validate/{content_id}", response_model=ValidationResponse)
41	async def validate_content(
42	    content_id: str,
43	    session: SessionDep,
44	    validation_service: ValidationServiceDep,
45	):
46	    """Trigger theological validation for content. Creates a version snapshot before validating."""
47	    item = await session.get(ContentItem, content_id)
48	    if not item:
49	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Content not found")
50	
51	    # Create version snapshot before validation
52	    snapshot = ContentVersion(
53	        id=str(uuid4()),
54	        content_id=item.id,
55	        version_number=item.version,
56	        title=item.title,
57	        body=item.body,
58	        ai_model_used=item.ai_model_used or "",
59	    )
60	    session.add(snapshot)
61	
62	    # Run validation
63	    result = await validation_service.validate(item.body, item.content_type.value)
64	
65	    # Persist validation record
66	    record = ValidationRecord(
67	        content_id=content_id,
68	        score=result.score,
69	        flags=json.dumps(result.flags),
70	        citations=json.dumps(
71	            [
72	                {
73	                    "citation_number": c.citation_number,
74	                    "source_id": c.source_id,
75	                    "cited_text": c.cited_text,
76	                }
77	                for c in result.citations
78	            ]
79	        ),
80	        provider=result.provider,
81	        raw_answer=result.raw_answer,
82	        content_version=item.version,
83	    )
84	    session.add(record)
85	
86	    # Update content status to validated
87	    item.status = ContentStatus.VALIDATED
88	    session.add(item)
89	
90	    await session.commit()
91	    await session.refresh(record)
92	
93	    return _record_to_response(record)
94	
95	
96	@router.get("/history/{content_id}", response_model=ValidationHistoryResponse)
97	async def get_validation_history(content_id: str, session: SessionDep):
98	    """Get validation history for content."""
99	    item = await session.get(ContentItem, content_id)
100	    if not item:
101	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Content not found")
102	
103	    stmt = (
104	        select(ValidationRecord)
105	        .where(ValidationRecord.content_id == content_id)
106	        .order_by(ValidationRecord.created_at.desc())
107	    )
108	    results = await session.exec(stmt)
109	    records = results.all()
110	
111	    return ValidationHistoryResponse(
112	        items=[_record_to_response(r) for r in records],
113	        total=len(records),
114	    )
115	
116	
117	@router.get("/status", response_model=ValidationStatusResponse)
118	async def get_validation_status(validation_service: ValidationServiceDep):
119	    """Check validation service availability."""
120	    primary_available = await validation_service._primary.is_available()
121	    return ValidationStatusResponse(
122	        primary_available=primary_available,
123	        primary_provider=type(validation_service._primary).__name__,
124	        fallback_provider=type(validation_service._fallback).__name__,
125	    )

1	from datetime import datetime, timedelta
2	
3	from fastapi import APIRouter
4	from sqlmodel import func, select
5	
6	from app.core.deps import SessionDep
7	from app.models.content import ContentItem, ContentStatus, ContentType
8	from app.models.schedule import ScheduleEntry, ScheduleStatus
9	
10	router = APIRouter()
11	
12	
13	@router.get("/summary")
14	async def get_dashboard_summary(session: SessionDep):
15	    """Get dashboard summary with pipeline counts per status, recent items, and total counts by type."""
16	    # Counts per status
17	    status_counts: dict[str, int] = {}
18	    for status in ContentStatus:
19	        stmt = select(func.count()).where(ContentItem.status == status)
20	        result = await session.exec(stmt)
21	        status_counts[status.value] = result.one()
22	
23	    # Total counts by content type
24	    type_counts: dict[str, int] = {}
25	    for content_type in ContentType:
26	        stmt = select(func.count()).where(ContentItem.content_type == content_type)
27	        result = await session.exec(stmt)
28	        type_counts[content_type.value] = result.one()
29	
30	    # Recent items (last 10)
31	    stmt = select(ContentItem).order_by(ContentItem.updated_at.desc()).limit(10)
32	    result = await session.exec(stmt)
33	    recent_items = [
34	        {
35	            "id": item.id,
36	            "title": item.title,
37	            "content_type": item.content_type.value,
38	            "status": item.status.value,
39	            "updated_at": item.updated_at.isoformat(),
40	        }
41	        for item in result.all()
42	    ]
43	
44	    return {
45	        "status_counts": status_counts,
46	        "type_counts": type_counts,
47	        "recent_items": recent_items,
48	        "total": sum(status_counts.values()),
49	    }
50	
51	
52	@router.get("/upcoming")
53	async def get_upcoming_content(session: SessionDep):
54	    """Get next 7 days of scheduled content."""
55	    now = datetime.utcnow()
56	    week_later = now + timedelta(days=7)
57	
58	    stmt = (
59	        select(ScheduleEntry)
60	        .where(
61	            ScheduleEntry.scheduled_date >= now,
62	            ScheduleEntry.scheduled_date <= week_later,
63	            ScheduleEntry.status == ScheduleStatus.PENDING,
64	        )
65	        .order_by(ScheduleEntry.scheduled_date.asc())
66	    )
67	    result = await session.exec(stmt)
68	    entries = result.all()
69	
70	    # Fetch associated content items
71	    items = []
72	    for entry in entries:
73	        content = await session.get(ContentItem, entry.content_id)
74	        items.append(
75	            {
76	                "schedule_id": entry.id,
77	                "content_id": entry.content_id,
78	                "title": content.title if content else "Unknown",
79	                "content_type": content.content_type.value if content else None,
80	                "channel": entry.channel,
81	                "scheduled_date": entry.scheduled_date.isoformat(),
82	            }
83	        )
84	
85	    return {"upcoming": items, "count": len(items)}

0	# Specification: Verbum — Catholic Content Management Platform
1	
2	## Overview
3	
4	Verbum is a greenfield, single-user Catholic content management platform for Ramir, a Portuguese-speaking Catholic content creator. The platform centralizes the full content lifecycle — from AI-assisted generation (via Anthropic Claude and OpenAI ChatGPT) through theological validation (via NotebookLM), editorial scheduling, and multi-channel delivery. It supports three content types: short-form video scripts (1 min), long-form video scripts (10 min), and SEO-optimized blog posts in Portuguese. The goal is to reduce time-to-publish by 50%, ensure 100% doctrinal accuracy through automated validation, and maintain a consistent publishing cadence of at least 3 pieces per week. The entire system is designed for a single user — no multi-tenancy, no scaling concerns, no monetization.
5	
6	## Workflow Type
7	
8	**Type**: feature
9	
10	**Rationale**: This is a brand-new full-stack application being built from scratch within an empty repository. There is no existing application logic to refactor or migrate — only a Vite/React/Tailwind prototype shell (`prototypes-magic/`) with reusable UI components. Every layer (database schema, backend API, service integrations, frontend pages) must be designed and implemented.
11	
12	## Task Scope
13	
14	### Services Involved
15	- **verbum-backend** (primary) — Python FastAPI backend handling API routing, AI integration orchestration, NotebookLM validation, database persistence, and scheduling logic
16	- **verbum-frontend** (primary) — React TypeScript SPA providing the dashboard, content editor, validation panel, editorial calendar, and SEO tools
17	- **SQLite database** (infrastructure) — Embedded file-based database for content items, validation records, scheduling metadata, and version history
18	
19	### This Task Will:
20	- [ ] Set up the full project structure (backend + frontend monorepo)
21	- [ ] Implement the Python FastAPI backend with async architecture
22	- [ ] Design and create the SQLite database schema via SQLModel
23	- [ ] Integrate Anthropic Claude API for AI content generation with streaming
24	- [ ] Integrate OpenAI ChatGPT API as an alternate drafting engine with streaming
25	- [ ] Integrate NotebookLM (via `notebooklm-py`) for theological validation with citation retrieval
26	- [ ] Build a validation abstraction layer to enable graceful degradation and provider swapping
27	- [ ] Create the React frontend with dashboard, content editor, validation panel, and calendar views
28	- [ ] Implement the full content lifecycle: draft → validated → scheduled → published
29	- [ ] Add Portuguese SEO metadata tools for blog post optimization
30	- [ ] Implement content versioning and validation history tracking
31	- [ ] Add iCal export for editorial calendar reminders
32	- [ ] Implement environment-based configuration with secure API key management
33	
34	### Out of Scope:
35	- Custom Catholic knowledge base or RAG pipeline (NotebookLM handles this)
36	- Social media analytics or community management
37	- Monetization or revenue features
38	- Multi-user authentication or multi-tenancy
39	- Direct Google Calendar API integration (using .ics export instead)
40	- Bulk social media publishing automation
41	- Mobile native app (responsive web only)
42	- Docker containerization (single-user local/hosted deployment)
43	
44	## Service Context
45	
46	### verbum-backend
47	
48	**Tech Stack:**
49	- Language: Python 3.11+
50	- Framework: FastAPI >= 0.135.0 (with `[standard]` extras for uvicorn)
51	- ORM: SQLModel >= 0.0.37 (Pydantic + SQLAlchemy combined)
52	- Database: SQLite (via aiosqlite for async operations)
53	- AI SDKs: `anthropic >= 0.88.0`, `openai >= 2.30.0`
54	- Validation: `notebooklm-py[browser] >= 0.3.4` (UNOFFICIAL — HIGH RISK)
55	- Calendar: `icalendar` for .ics export
56	- Config: `python-dotenv` for environment variable management
57	- Key directories: `backend/app/`, `backend/app/api/`, `backend/app/models/`, `backend/app/services/`, `backend/app/core/`
58	
59	**Entry Point:** `backend/app/main.py`
60	
61	**How to Run:**
62	```bash
63	cd backend
64	pip install -r requirements.txt
65	uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8000
66	```
67	
68	**Port:** 8000
69	
70	---
71	
72	### verbum-frontend
73	
74	**Tech Stack:**
75	- Language: TypeScript 5.5+
76	- Framework: React 18.3
77	- Bundler: Vite 5.2
78	- Styling: Tailwind CSS 3.4
79	- Icons: lucide-react 0.522
777:## QA Acceptance Criteria

entries.detail:
777	## QA Acceptance Criteria
778	
779	**CRITICAL**: These criteria must be verified by the QA Agent before sign-off.
780	
781	### Unit Tests
782	
783	| Test | File | What to Verify |
784	|------|------|----------------|
785	| Content CRUD | `backend/tests/test_content.py` | Create, read, update, delete content items; validate field defaults; enforce required fields |
786	| Status Transitions | `backend/tests/test_content.py` | Valid transitions (draft→validated→scheduled→published); reject invalid transitions |
787	| Claude Provider | `backend/tests/test_generation.py` | Mock Anthropic client; verify correct model/params; handle APIError, RateLimitError |
788	| OpenAI Provider | `backend/tests/test_generation.py` | Mock OpenAI client; verify Chat Completions format; handle errors |
789	| Validation Service | `backend/tests/test_validation.py` | Primary→fallback failover; score/flag/citation parsing; unavailability detection |
790	| NotebookLM Provider | `backend/tests/test_validation.py` | Mock notebooklm client; parse AskResult with citations; handle RPCError |
791	| Schedule Service | `backend/tests/test_schedule.py` | Schedule/reschedule items; iCal export format; date range queries |
792	| SEO Service | `backend/tests/test_seo.py` | Readability score calculation; keyword suggestion format; metadata validation |
793	| Content Versioning | `backend/tests/test_content.py` | Version increment on save; snapshot creation; version retrieval |
794	
795	### Integration Tests
796	
797	| Test | Services | What to Verify |
798	|------|----------|----------------|
799	| Content → Generation | backend API + AI service | POST to /api/generation/generate returns valid content; POST to /api/generation/stream returns SSE events |
800	| Content → Validation | backend API + validation service | POST to /api/validation/validate/{id} returns ValidationResult; GET /api/validation/history/{id} returns history |
801	| Content → Schedule | backend API + schedule service | POST schedule updates content status; GET /api/schedule/calendar returns items in date range |
802	| Validation Failover | validation service + providers | When primary provider raises, fallback is used; response includes `provider` field indicating fallback |
803	| Dashboard Aggregation | backend API + database | GET /api/dashboard/summary returns correct counts per status |
804	
805	### End-to-End Tests
806	
807	| Flow | Steps | Expected Outcome |
808	|------|-------|------------------|
809	| Content Creation | 1. Navigate to content list 2. Click "New Content" 3. Select "Short Video" type 4. Enter title | Content item created with status "draft", appears in list |
810	| AI Generation | 1. Open content in editor 2. Select "Claude" model 3. Enter prompt 4. Click "Generate" | Text streams into editor in real-time; content body is updated |
811	| Theological Validation | 1. Open content with body text 2. Click "Validate" | Validation panel shows score (0-100%), any flags, and expandable citations |
812	| Schedule Content | 1. Validate content first 2. Click "Schedule" 3. Select date and channel | Content appears on editorial calendar at selected date; status changes to "scheduled" |
813	| Full Pipeline | 1. Create → 2. Generate → 3. Validate → 4. Schedule → 5. Mark Published | Content item transitions through all statuses correctly; appears in dashboard counts |
814	| Validation Fallback | 1. Disable NotebookLM (env var) 2. Trigger validation | Validation completes via AI fallback; panel shows "fallback" indicator |
815	
816	### Browser Verification
817	
818	| Page/Component | URL | Checks |
819	|----------------|-----|--------|
820	| Dashboard | `http://localhost:5173/` | Pipeline status counts display; upcoming items list populates; mini-calendar renders |
821	| Content List | `http://localhost:5173/content` | Content items load; filtering by status/type works; create button navigates to editor |
822	| Content Editor | `http://localhost:5173/content/{id}` | Editor loads content body; right panel shows validation results; generation panel has model selector |
823	| Generation Panel | `http://localhost:5173/content/{id}` | Model dropdown lists Claude + ChatGPT; streaming text appears character-by-character |
824	| Validation Panel | `http://localhost:5173/content/{id}` | Score displays as percentage with color coding; flags listed; citations expandable |
825	| Editorial Calendar | `http://localhost:5173/calendar` | Week/month toggle works; scheduled items appear on correct dates; click opens content |
826	| SEO Panel | `http://localhost:5173/content/{id}` (blog type) | SEO fields visible for blog posts only; readability score updates; keyword suggestions generate |
827	
828	### Database Verification
829	
830	| Check | Query/Command | Expected |
831	|-------|---------------|----------|
832	| Tables exist | `sqlite3 verbum.db ".tables"` | `content_items content_versions validation_records seo_metadata` |
833	| Content creation | `sqlite3 verbum.db "SELECT count(*) FROM content_items"` | Count increments after creating content |
834	| Status tracking | `sqlite3 verbum.db "SELECT status, count(*) FROM content_items GROUP BY status"` | Correct distribution across statuses |
835	| Validation records | `sqlite3 verbum.db "SELECT count(*) FROM validation_records WHERE content_id='{id}'"` | Records created after each validation |
836	| Version history | `sqlite3 verbum.db "SELECT count(*) FROM content_versions WHERE content_id='{id}'"` | Versions increment on saves |
837	| UTF-8 Portuguese | `sqlite3 verbum.db "SELECT title FROM content_items WHERE title LIKE '%ção%'"` | Portuguese characters stored and retrieved correctly |
838	
839	### Security Verification
840	
841	| Check | How to Verify | Expected |

2. **Missing `@radix-ui/*` type declarations** — dependencies are listed but likely not installed (or need `npm install`)
3. **`import.meta.env`** needs Vite types (`vite/client`)
4. **Implicit `any` types** and unused imports

OK, I have a clear picture now. The critical issues are:

1. **Missing npm dependencies** — radix-ui packages listed in package.json but not installed in node_modules

4. **Minor TS issues** — unused imports, implicit `any` params

entries.detail:
# QA Fix Request

**Status**: REJECTED
**Date**: 2026-04-02
**QA Session**: 1

## Critical Issues to Fix

### 1. Missing barrel export index files for all UI components
**Problem**: UI components in `frontend/src/components/ui/*/` are imported as barrel imports (e.g., `./ui/accordion`) but no `index.ts` files exist. Both `vite build` and `tsc --noEmit` fail.
**Location**: All 17 folders under `frontend/src/components/ui/`: accordion, badge, button, card, input, label, progress, scroll-area, select, separator, slider, sonner, table, tabs, textarea, toggle-group, toggle, tooltip
**Required Fix**: Add `index.ts` to each folder that re-exports from the component file. Example for `accordion/index.ts`:
```ts
export * from './Accordion';
```
Also check `utils.ts` — it's at `ui/utils.ts` (not in a subfolder), verify it's imported correctly.
**Verification**: `npx vite build` succeeds

### 2. Missing Vite client type declarations
**Problem**: `import.meta.env` causes TS errors in `api.ts`, `streaming.ts`, `useSchedule.ts`
**Location**: `frontend/src/`
**Required Fix**: Create `frontend/src/vite-env.d.ts` containing:
```ts
/// <reference types="vite/client" />
```
**Verification**: `npx tsc --noEmit` reports no `import.meta.env` errors

### 3. TypeScript strict mode violations
**Problem**: Implicit `any` types and unused imports
**Location**: 
- `frontend/src/pages/ContentListPage.tsx:89,108` — `value` param needs `string` type
- `frontend/src/hooks/useGeneration.ts:3` — unused `ContentType` import
**Required Fix**: Add types, remove unused import
**Verification**: `npx tsc --noEmit` passes with 0 errors

## After Fixes

Once fixes are complete:
1. Verify `npx vite build` succeeds
2. Verify `npx tsc --noEmit` passes
3. Commit with message: "fix: add UI component barrel exports and fix TypeScript errors (qa-requested)"
4. QA will automatically re-run

# QA Validation Report

**Spec**: 002-verbum-implementation
**Date**: 2026-04-02
**QA Agent Session**: 1

## Summary

| Category | Status | Details |
|----------|--------|---------|
| Subtasks Complete | ✓ | 30/30 completed |
| Unit Tests | ✓ | 94/94 passing |
| Integration Tests | ✓ | Included in unit test suite |
| E2E Tests | ✗ | Frontend does not build — cannot verify |
| Visual Verification | ✗ | Frontend does not build — cannot start app |
| Database Verification | N/A | DB created at runtime |
| Security Review | ✓ | No hardcoded keys, CORS properly configured |
| Pattern Compliance | ✓ | Protocol interfaces, async, SQLModel patterns used |
| Regression Check | ✓ | Backend 94/94 tests pass |

## Issues Found

### Critical (Blocks Sign-off)

1. **Frontend build fails — missing barrel export index files for UI components**
   - **Location**: `frontend/src/components/ui/*/` (all UI component folders)
   - **Problem**: Components are in folders like `ui/accordion/Accordion.tsx` but imports reference `./ui/accordion` (barrel import). No `index.ts` files exist in any UI component folder, so both Vite build and TypeScript compilation fail.
   - **Error**: `Could not resolve "./ui/badge" from "src/components/ValidationPanel.tsx"`
   - **Fix**: Add `index.ts` barrel export file to each UI component folder. For example, `frontend/src/components/ui/accordion/index.ts` should contain `export * from './Accordion';` (or `export { ... } from './Accordion'`). This is needed for all 17 UI component folders.

2. **Missing Vite client types — `import.meta.env` errors**
   - **Location**: `frontend/src/services/api.ts:3`, `frontend/src/services/streaming.ts:3`, `frontend/src/hooks/useSchedule.ts:67`
   - **Problem**: `import.meta.env` is Vite-specific and requires type declarations. TypeScript reports `Property 'env' does not exist on type 'ImportMeta'`.
   - **Fix**: Add `/// <reference types="vite/client" />` in a `src/vite-env.d.ts` file, or add `"types": ["vite/client"]` to tsconfig.json compilerOptions.

### Major (Should Fix)

3. **TypeScript strict mode violations**
   - **Location**: `frontend/src/pages/ContentListPage.tsx:89,108` — implicit `any` type on `value` parameter
   - **Location**: `frontend/src/hooks/useGeneration.ts:3` — unused import `ContentType`
   - **Fix**: Add explicit types to the `value` parameters; remove unused `ContentType` import.

## Recommended Fixes

### Issue 1: Missing barrel exports (CRITICAL)
Create `index.ts` in each of these 17 directories under `frontend/src/components/ui/`:
- accordion, badge, button, card, input, label, progress, scroll-area, select, separator, slider, sonner, table, tabs, textarea, toggle, toggle-group, tooltip

Each `index.ts` should re-export from the component file, e.g.:
```ts

```

### Issue 2: Vite client types (CRITICAL)
Create `frontend/src/vite-env.d.ts`:
```ts
/// <reference types="vite/client" />
```

### Issue 3: TypeScript strict violations (MAJOR)
- `ContentListPage.tsx:89,108`: Add `string` type to `value` parameter
- `useGeneration.ts:3`: Remove `ContentType` from import

**Verification**: After fixes, `npx tsc --noEmit` should pass with 0 errors and `npx vite build` should succeed.

## Verdict

**SIGN-OFF**: REJECTED

**Reason**: Frontend cannot compile or build. The Vite production build fails due to missing barrel export index files in all UI component folders. This is a critical blocking issue — the application cannot be started, tested end-to-end, or deployed.

# Specification: Verbum — Catholic Content Management Platform

## Overview

Verbum is a greenfield, single-user Catholic content management platform for Ramir, a Portuguese-speaking Catholic content creator. The platform centralizes the full content lifecycle — from AI-assisted generation (via Anthropic Claude and OpenAI ChatGPT) through theological validation (via NotebookLM), editorial scheduling, and multi-channel delivery. It supports three content types: short-form video scripts (1 min), long-form video scripts (10 min), and SEO-optimized blog posts in Portuguese. The goal is to reduce time-to-publish by 50%, ensure 100% doctrinal accuracy through automated validation, and maintain a consistent publishing cadence of at least 3 pieces per week. The entire system is designed for a single user — no multi-tenancy, no scaling concerns, no monetization.

## Workflow Type

**Type**: feature

**Rationale**: This is a brand-new full-stack application being built from scratch within an empty repository. There is no existing application logic to refactor or migrate — only a Vite/React/Tailwind prototype shell (`prototypes-magic/`) with reusable UI components. Every layer (database schema, backend API, service integrations, frontend pages) must be designed and implemented.

## Task Scope

### Services Involved
- **verbum-backend** (primary) — Python FastAPI backend handling API routing, AI integration orchestration, NotebookLM validation, database persistence, and scheduling logic
- **verbum-frontend** (primary) — React TypeScript SPA providing the dashboard, content editor, validation panel, editorial calendar, and SEO tools
- **SQLite database** (infrastructure) — Embedded file-based database for content items, validation records, scheduling metadata, and version history

### This Task Will:
- [ ] Set up the full project structure (backend + frontend monorepo)
- [ ] Implement the Python FastAPI backend with async architecture
- [ ] Design and create the SQLite database schema via SQLModel
- [ ] Integrate Anthropic Claude API for AI content generation with streaming
- [ ] Integrate OpenAI ChatGPT API as an alternate drafting engine with streaming
- [ ] Integrate NotebookLM (via `notebooklm-py`) for theological validation with citation retrieval
- [ ] Build a validation abstraction layer to enable graceful degradation and provider swapping
- [ ] Create the React frontend with dashboard, content editor, validation panel, and calendar views
- [ ] Implement the full content lifecycle: draft → validated → scheduled → published
- [ ] Add Portuguese SEO metadata tools for blog post optimization
- [ ] Implement content versioning and validation history tracking
- [ ] Add iCal export for editorial calendar reminders
- [ ] Implement environment-based configuration with secure API key management

### Out of Scope:
- Custom Catholic knowledge base or RAG pipeline (NotebookLM handles this)
- Social media analytics or community management
- Monetization or revenue features
- Multi-user authentication or multi-tenancy
- Direct Google Calendar API integration (using .ics export instead)
- Bulk social media publishing automation
- Mobile native app (responsive web only)
- Docker containerization (single-user local/hosted deployment)

## Service Context

### verbum-backend

**Tech Stack:**
- Language: Python 3.11+
- Framework: FastAPI >= 0.135.0 (with `[standard]` extras for uvicorn)
- ORM: SQLModel >= 0.0.37 (Pydantic + SQLAlchemy combined)
- Database: SQLite (via aiosqlite for async operations)
- AI SDKs: `anthropic >= 0.88.0`, `openai >= 2.30.0`
- Validation: `notebooklm-py[browser] >= 0.3.4` (UNOFFICIAL — HIGH RISK)
- Calendar: `icalendar` for .ics export
- Config: `python-dotenv` for environment variable management
- Key directories: `backend/app/`, `backend/app/api/`, `backend/app/models/`, `backend/app/services/`, `backend/app/core/`

**Entry Point:** `backend/app/main.py`

**How to Run:**
```bash
cd backend
pip install -r requirements.txt
uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8000
```

**Port:** 8000

---

### verbum-frontend

**Tech Stack:**
- Language: TypeScript 5.5+
- Framework: React 18.3
- Bundler: Vite 5.2
- Styling: Tailwind CSS 3.4
- Icons: lucide-react 0.522

23	- Phase 6 (Calendar/SEO/Dashboard Backend): 3 subtasks, depends on [phase-1] — schedule/SEO/dashboard services and APIs
24	- Phase 7 (Calendar/SEO/Dashboard Frontend): 3 subtasks, depends on [phase-2, phase-5, phase-6] — calendar UI, SEO panel, dashboard page
25	- Phase 8 (Testing & Polish): 4 subtasks, depends on [phase-5, phase-6, phase-7] — test suite, versioning UI, Portuguese i18n, E2E verification
26	
27	Services Involved:
28	- verbum-backend: Python FastAPI + SQLModel + SQLite + AI SDKs (port 8000)
29	- verbum-frontend: React + TypeScript + Vite + Tailwind CSS (port 5173)
30	
31	Parallelism Analysis:
32	- Max parallel phases: 3 (Phases 3, 4, 6 can all run concurrently after Phase 1)
33	- Recommended workers: 2
34	- Parallel groups:
35	  * Group A: Phase 1 (backend) || Phase 2 (frontend) — independent services
36	  * Group B: Phase 3 (AI backend) || Phase 4 (validation backend) || Phase 6 (calendar/seo/dashboard backend) — different files, same service
37	
38	Key Technical Decisions:
39	- Backend uses FastAPI lifespan for DB init (not deprecated on_event)
40	- Python Protocol classes for AI and validation interfaces
41	- SQLModel for combined Pydantic+SQLAlchemy models
42	- UUID string primary keys throughout
43	- SSE streaming via FastAPI StreamingResponse
44	- UI components copied from prototypes-magic/ to frontend/
45	- NotebookLM HIGH RISK — fallback AI validation always available
46	- No auth (single-user) — no Docker
47

0	# Specification: Verbum — Catholic Content Management Platform
1	
2	## Overview
3	
4	Verbum is a greenfield, single-user Catholic content management platform for Ramir, a Portuguese-speaking Catholic content creator. The platform centralizes the full content lifecycle — from AI-assisted generation (via Anthropic Claude and OpenAI ChatGPT) through theological validation (via NotebookLM), editorial scheduling, and multi-channel delivery. It supports three content types: short-form video scripts (1 min), long-form video scripts (10 min), and SEO-optimized blog posts in Portuguese. The goal is to reduce time-to-publish by 50%, ensure 100% doctrinal accuracy through automated validation, and maintain a consistent publishing cadence of at least 3 pieces per week. The entire system is designed for a single user — no multi-tenancy, no scaling concerns, no monetization.
5	
6	## Workflow Type
7	
8	**Type**: feature
9	
10	**Rationale**: This is a brand-new full-stack application being built from scratch within an empty repository. There is no existing application logic to refactor or migrate — only a Vite/React/Tailwind prototype shell (`prototypes-magic/`) with reusable UI components. Every layer (database schema, backend API, service integrations, frontend pages) must be designed and implemented.
11	
12	## Task Scope
13	
14	### Services Involved
15	- **verbum-backend** (primary) — Python FastAPI backend handling API routing, AI integration orchestration, NotebookLM validation, database persistence, and scheduling logic
16	- **verbum-frontend** (primary) — React TypeScript SPA providing the dashboard, content editor, validation panel, editorial calendar, and SEO tools
17	- **SQLite database** (infrastructure) — Embedded file-based database for content items, validation records, scheduling metadata, and version history
18	
19	### This Task Will:
20	- [ ] Set up the full project structure (backend + frontend monorepo)
21	- [ ] Implement the Python FastAPI backend with async architecture
22	- [ ] Design and create the SQLite database schema via SQLModel
23	- [ ] Integrate Anthropic Claude API for AI content generation with streaming
24	- [ ] Integrate OpenAI ChatGPT API as an alternate drafting engine with streaming
25	- [ ] Integrate NotebookLM (via `notebooklm-py`) for theological validation with citation retrieval
26	- [ ] Build a validation abstraction layer to enable graceful degradation and provider swapping
27	- [ ] Create the React frontend with dashboard, content editor, validation panel, and calendar views
28	- [ ] Implement the full content lifecycle: draft → validated → scheduled → published
29	- [ ] Add Portuguese SEO metadata tools for blog post optimization
30	- [ ] Implement content versioning and validation history tracking
31	- [ ] Add iCal export for editorial calendar reminders
32	- [ ] Implement environment-based configuration with secure API key management
33	
34	### Out of Scope:
35	- Custom Catholic knowledge base or RAG pipeline (NotebookLM handles this)
36	- Social media analytics or community management
37	- Monetization or revenue features
38	- Multi-user authentication or multi-tenancy
39	- Direct Google Calendar API integration (using .ics export instead)
40	- Bulk social media publishing automation
41	- Mobile native app (responsive web only)
42	- Docker containerization (single-user local/hosted deployment)
43	
44	## Service Context
45	
46	### verbum-backend
47	
48	**Tech Stack:**
49	- Language: Python 3.11+
50	- Framework: FastAPI >= 0.135.0 (with `[standard]` extras for uvicorn)
51	- ORM: SQLModel >= 0.0.37 (Pydantic + SQLAlchemy combined)
52	- Database: SQLite (via aiosqlite for async operations)
53	- AI SDKs: `anthropic >= 0.88.0`, `openai >= 2.30.0`
54	- Validation: `notebooklm-py[browser] >= 0.3.4` (UNOFFICIAL — HIGH RISK)
55	- Calendar: `icalendar` for .ics export
56	- Config: `python-dotenv` for environment variable management
57	- Key directories: `backend/app/`, `backend/app/api/`, `backend/app/models/`, `backend/app/services/`, `backend/app/core/`
58	
59	**Entry Point:** `backend/app/main.py`
60	
61	**How to Run:**
62	```bash
63	cd backend
64	pip install -r requirements.txt
65	uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8000
66	```
67	
68	**Port:** 8000
69	
70	---
71	
72	### verbum-frontend
73	
74	**Tech Stack:**
75	- Language: TypeScript 5.5+
76	- Framework: React 18.3
77	- Bundler: Vite 5.2
78	- Styling: Tailwind CSS 3.4
79	- Icons: lucide-react 0.522
80	- UI Components: Reuse from `prototypes-magic/src/components/ui/` (Button, Input, Select, Card, Tabs, Badge, Table, Textarea, Progress, etc.)
81	- Charts: recharts (already in prototype dependencies)
82	- Key directories: `frontend/src/`, `frontend/src/components/`, `frontend/src/pages/`, `frontend/src/services/`, `frontend/src/hooks/`
83	
84	**Entry Point:** `frontend/src/main.tsx`
85	
86	**How to Run:**
87	```bash
88	cd frontend
89	npm install
90	npm run dev
91	```
92	
93	**Port:** 5173 (Vite default)
94	
95	## Files to Create
96	
97	### Backend Structure
98	
99	| File | Service | Purpose |

777:## QA Acceptance Criteria
778-
779-**CRITICAL**: These criteria must be verified by the QA Agent before sign-off.
780-
781-### Unit Tests
782-
783-| Test | File | What to Verify |
784-|------|------|----------------|
785-| Content CRUD | `backend/tests/test_content.py` | Create, read, update, delete content items; validate field defaults; enforce required fields |
786-| Status Transitions | `backend/tests/test_content.py` | Valid transitions (draft→validated→scheduled→published); reject invalid transitions |
787-| Claude Provider | `backend/tests/test_generation.py` | Mock Anthropic client; verify correct model/params; handle APIError, RateLimitError |
788-| OpenAI Provider | `backend/tests/test_generation.py` | Mock OpenAI client; verify Chat Completions format; handle errors |
789-| Validation Service | `backend/tests/test_validation.py` | Primary→fallback failover; score/flag/citation parsing; unavailability detection |
790-| NotebookLM Provider | `backend/tests/test_validation.py` | Mock notebooklm client; parse AskResult with citations; handle RPCError |
791-| Schedule Service | `backend/tests/test_schedule.py` | Schedule/reschedule items; iCal export format; date range queries |
792-| SEO Service | `backend/tests/test_seo.py` | Readability score calculation; keyword suggestion format; metadata validation |
793-| Content Versioning | `backend/tests/test_content.py` | Version increment on save; snapshot creation; version retrieval |
794-
795-### Integration Tests
796-
797-| Test | Services | What to Verify |
798-|------|----------|----------------|
799-| Content → Generation | backend API + AI service | POST to /api/generation/generate returns valid content; POST to /api/generation/stream returns SSE events |
800-| Content → Validation | backend API + validation service | POST to /api/validation/validate/{id} returns ValidationResult; GET /api/validation/history/{id} returns history |
801-| Content → Schedule | backend API + schedule service | POST schedule updates content status; GET /api/schedule/calendar returns items in date range |
802-| Validation Failover | validation service + providers | When primary provider raises, fallback is used; response includes `provider` field indicating fallback |
803-| Dashboard Aggregation | backend API + database | GET /api/dashboard/summary returns correct counts per status |
804-
805-### End-to-End Tests
806-
807-| Flow | Steps | Expected Outcome |
808-|------|-------|------------------|
809-| Content Creation | 1. Navigate to content list 2. Click "New Content" 3. Select "Short Video" type 4. Enter title | Content item created with status "draft", appears in list |
810-| AI Generation | 1. Open content in editor 2. Select "Claude" model 3. Enter prompt 4. Click "Generate" | Text streams into editor in real-time; content body is updated |
811-| Theological Validation | 1. Open content with body text 2. Click "Validate" | Validation panel shows score (0-100%), any flags, and expandable citations |
812-| Schedule Content | 1. Validate content first 2. Click "Schedule" 3. Select date and channel | Content appears on editorial calendar at selected date; status changes to "scheduled" |
813-| Full Pipeline | 1. Create → 2. Generate → 3. Validate → 4. Schedule → 5. Mark Published | Content item transitions through all statuses correctly; appears in dashboard counts |
814-| Validation Fallback | 1. Disable NotebookLM (env var) 2. Trigger validation | Validation completes via AI fallback; panel shows "fallback" indicator |
815-
816-### Browser Verification
817-
818-| Page/Component | URL | Checks |
819-|----------------|-----|--------|
820-| Dashboard | `http://localhost:5173/` | Pipeline status counts display; upcoming items list populates; mini-calendar renders |
821-| Content List | `http://localhost:5173/content` | Content items load; filtering by status/type works; create button navigates to editor |
822-| Content Editor | `http://localhost:5173/content/{id}` | Editor loads content body; right panel shows validation results; generation panel has model selector |
823-| Generation Panel | `http://localhost:5173/content/{id}` | Model dropdown lists Claude + ChatGPT; streaming text appears character-by-character |
824-| Validation Panel | `http://localhost:5173/content/{id}` | Score displays as percentage with color coding; flags listed; citations expandable |
825-| Editorial Calendar | `http://localhost:5173/calendar` | Week/month toggle works; scheduled items appear on correct dates; click opens content |
826-| SEO Panel | `http://localhost:5173/content/{id}` (blog type) | SEO fields visible for blog posts only; readability score updates; keyword suggestions generate |
827-
828-### Database Verification
829-
830-| Check | Query/Command | Expected |
831-|-------|---------------|----------|
832-| Tables exist | `sqlite3 verbum.db ".tables"` | `content_items content_versions validation_records seo_metadata` |
833-| Content creation | `sqlite3 verbum.db "SELECT count(*) FROM content_items"` | Count increments after creating content |
834-| Status tracking | `sqlite3 verbum.db "SELECT status, count(*) FROM content_items GROUP BY status"` | Correct distribution across statuses |
835-| Validation records | `sqlite3 verbum.db "SELECT count(*) FROM validation_records WHERE content_id='{id}'"` | Records created after each validation |
836-| Version history | `sqlite3 verbum.db "SELECT count(*) FROM content_versions WHERE content_id='{id}'"` | Versions increment on saves |
837-| UTF-8 Portuguese | `sqlite3 verbum.db "SELECT title FROM content_items WHERE title LIKE '%ção%'"` | Portuguese characters stored and retrieved correctly |
838-
839-### Security Verification
840-
841-| Check | How to Verify | Expected |
842-|-------|---------------|----------|
843-| No API keys in frontend | Inspect network requests in browser DevTools | No `sk-ant-*` or `sk-*` keys in any request or response |
844-| API keys in env only | `grep -r "sk-ant\|sk-" frontend/` | No matches (keys only in backend/.env) |
845-| CORS configured | `curl -H "Origin: http://evil.com" http://localhost:8000/api/content` | Request blocked (only localhost:5173 allowed) |
846-| Environment validation | Remove ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from .env and restart backend | Clear error message at startup, not a runtime crash |
847-
848-### Performance Verification
849-
850-| Check | How to Verify | Target |
851-|-------|---------------|--------|
852-| AI generation latency | Time from "Generate" click to first streamed token | < 3 seconds |
853-| Validation latency | Time from "Validate" click to score display | < 5 seconds (NotebookLM), < 3 seconds (fallback) |
854-| Content list load | Navigate to content list with 50+ items | < 1 second page load |
855-| Calendar render | Navigate to calendar with 20+ scheduled items | < 1 second render |
856-
857-### QA Sign-off Requirements
858-- [ ] All unit tests pass (`cd backend && pytest`)
859-- [ ] All integration tests pass
860-- [ ] All E2E tests pass
861-- [ ] Browser verification complete (all pages render correctly)
862-- [ ] Database state verified (tables, relationships, UTF-8)
863-- [ ] No regressions in existing functionality
864-- [ ] Code follows established patterns (async everywhere, Protocol interfaces, SQLModel)
865-- [ ] No security vulnerabilities introduced (API keys secure, CORS configured)
866-- [ ] Portuguese content renders correctly throughout
867-- [ ] Streaming AI generation works smoothly in the browser
868-- [ ] Validation fallback triggers correctly when NotebookLM is unavailable
869-- [ ] Editorial calendar displays items on correct dates
870-- [ ] Content status transitions are enforced (cannot skip validation)

1	# QA Validation Report
2	
3	**Spec**: 002-verbum-implementation
4	**Date**: 2026-04-02
5	**QA Agent Session**: 1
6	
7	## Summary
8	
9	| Category | Status | Details |
10	|----------|--------|---------|
11	| Subtasks Complete | ✓ | 30/30 completed |
12	| Unit Tests | ✓ | 94/94 passing |
13	| Integration Tests | ✓ | Included in unit test suite |
14	| E2E Tests | ✗ | Frontend does not build — cannot verify |
15	| Visual Verification | ✗ | Frontend does not build — cannot start app |
16	| Database Verification | N/A | DB created at runtime |
17	| Security Review | ✓ | No hardcoded keys, CORS properly configured |
18	| Pattern Compliance | ✓ | Protocol interfaces, async, SQLModel patterns used |
19	| Regression Check | ✓ | Backend 94/94 tests pass |
20	
21	## Issues Found
22	
23	### Critical (Blocks Sign-off)
24	
25	1. **Frontend build fails — missing barrel export index files for UI components**
26	   - **Location**: `frontend/src/components/ui/*/` (all UI component folders)
27	   - **Problem**: Components are in folders like `ui/accordion/Accordion.tsx` but imports reference `./ui/accordion` (barrel import). No `index.ts` files exist in any UI component folder, so both Vite build and TypeScript compilation fail.
28	   - **Error**: `Could not resolve "./ui/badge" from "src/components/ValidationPanel.tsx"`
29	   - **Fix**: Add `index.ts` barrel export file to each UI component folder. For example, `frontend/src/components/ui/accordion/index.ts` should contain `export * from './Accordion';` (or `export { ... } from './Accordion'`). This is needed for all 17 UI component folders.
30	
31	2. **Missing Vite client types — `import.meta.env` errors**
32	   - **Location**: `frontend/src/services/api.ts:3`, `frontend/src/services/streaming.ts:3`, `frontend/src/hooks/useSchedule.ts:67`
33	   - **Problem**: `import.meta.env` is Vite-specific and requires type declarations. TypeScript reports `Property 'env' does not exist on type 'ImportMeta'`.
34	   - **Fix**: Add `/// <reference types="vite/client" />` in a `src/vite-env.d.ts` file, or add `"types": ["vite/client"]` to tsconfig.json compilerOptions.
35	
36	### Major (Should Fix)
37	
38	3. **TypeScript strict mode violations**
39	   - **Location**: `frontend/src/pages/ContentListPage.tsx:89,108` — implicit `any` type on `value` parameter
40	   - **Location**: `frontend/src/hooks/useGeneration.ts:3` — unused import `ContentType`
41	   - **Fix**: Add explicit types to the `value` parameters; remove unused `ContentType` import.
42	
43	## Recommended Fixes
44	
45	### Issue 1: Missing barrel exports (CRITICAL)
46	Create `index.ts` in each of these 17 directories under `frontend/src/components/ui/`:
47	- accordion, badge, button, card, input, label, progress, scroll-area, select, separator, slider, sonner, table, tabs, textarea, toggle, toggle-group, tooltip
48	
49	Each `index.ts` should re-export from the component file, e.g.:
50	```ts
51	export * from './Accordion';
52	```
53	
54	### Issue 2: Vite client types (CRITICAL)
55	Create `frontend/src/vite-env.d.ts`:
56	```ts
57	/// <reference types="vite/client" />
58	```
59	
60	### Issue 3: TypeScript strict violations (MAJOR)
61	- `ContentListPage.tsx:89,108`: Add `string` type to `value` parameter
62	- `useGeneration.ts:3`: Remove `ContentType` from import
63	
64	**Verification**: After fixes, `npx tsc --noEmit` should pass with 0 errors and `npx vite build` should succeed.
65	
66	## Verdict
67

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	from typing import AsyncIterator, Protocol, runtime_checkable
4	
5	
6	@runtime_checkable
7	class AIProvider(Protocol):
8	    """Protocol for AI content generation providers."""
9	
10	    async def generate(
11	        self, prompt: str, system_prompt: str, max_tokens: int
12	    ) -> str: ...
13	
14	    async def generate_stream(
15	        self, prompt: str, system_prompt: str, max_tokens: int
16	    ) -> AsyncIterator[str]: ...
17	
18	
19	class AIService:
20	    """Abstraction layer that routes generation requests to registered providers."""
21	
22	    def __init__(self, providers: dict[str, AIProvider]) -> None:
23	        self._providers = providers
24	
25	    @property
26	    def available_models(self) -> list[str]:
27	        return list(self._providers.keys())
28	
29	    def get_provider(self, model_key: str) -> AIProvider:
30	        provider = self._providers.get(model_key)
31	        if provider is None:
32	            raise ValueError(
33	                f"Unknown model '{model_key}'. "
34	                f"Available models: {self.available_models}"
35	            )
36	        return provider
37	
38	    async def generate(
39	        self,
40	        model_key: str,
41	        prompt: str,
42	        system_prompt: str,
43	        max_tokens: int = 2048,
44	    ) -> str:
45	        provider = self.get_provider(model_key)
46	        return await provider.generate(prompt, system_prompt, max_tokens)
47	
48	    async def generate_stream(
49	        self,
50	        model_key: str,
51	        prompt: str,
52	        system_prompt: str,
53	        max_tokens: int = 2048,
54	    ) -> AsyncIterator[str]:
55	        provider = self.get_provider(model_key)
56	        async for chunk in provider.generate_stream(prompt, system_prompt, max_tokens):
57	            yield chunk

30	@runtime_checkable
31	class ValidationProvider(Protocol):
32	    """Protocol for theological validation providers."""
33	
34	    async def validate(self, content: str, content_type: str) -> ValidationResult: ...
35	
36	    async def is_available(self) -> bool: ...
37	
38	
39	class ValidationService:
40	    """Validation abstraction layer with primary→fallback failover logic."""
41	
42	    def __init__(
43	        self, primary: ValidationProvider, fallback: ValidationProvider
44	    ) -> None:
45	        self._primary = primary
46	        self._fallback = fallback
47	
48	    async def validate(
49	        self, content: str, content_type: str
50	    ) -> ValidationResult:
51	        if await self._primary.is_available():
52	            try:
53	                return await self._primary.validate(content, content_type)
54	            except Exception:
55	                logger.warning(
56	                    "Primary validation provider failed, falling back",
57	                    exc_info=True,
58	                )
59	        return await self._fallback.validate(content, content_type)

14	# Valid status transitions: draft→validated→scheduled→published
15	VALID_TRANSITIONS: dict[ContentStatus, list[ContentStatus]] = {
16	    ContentStatus.DRAFT: [ContentStatus.VALIDATED],
17	    ContentStatus.VALIDATED: [ContentStatus.SCHEDULED, ContentStatus.DRAFT],
18	    ContentStatus.SCHEDULED: [ContentStatus.PUBLISHED, ContentStatus.VALIDATED],
19	    ContentStatus.PUBLISHED: [],
20	}

23	class ContentService:
24	    def __init__(self, session: AsyncSession):
25	        self.session = session
26	
27	    async def create(self, data: ContentCreate) -> ContentItem:
28	        item = ContentItem(
29	            title=data.title,
30	            body=data.body,
31	            content_type=data.content_type,
32	            language=data.language,
33	            channels=json.dumps(data.channels),
34	            ai_model_used=data.ai_model_used,
35	        )
36	        self.session.add(item)
37	        await self.session.commit()
38	        await self.session.refresh(item)
39	        return item
40	
41	    async def get(self, content_id: str) -> Optional[ContentItem]:
42	        return await self.session.get(ContentItem, content_id)
43	
44	    async def list(self, params: ContentListParams) -> tuple[list[ContentItem], int]:
45	        statement = select(ContentItem)
46	
47	        if params.status is not None:
48	            statement = statement.where(ContentItem.status == params.status)
49	        if params.content_type is not None:
50	            statement = statement.where(ContentItem.content_type == params.content_type)
51	        if params.channel is not None:
52	            statement = statement.where(ContentItem.channels.contains(params.channel))
53	
54	        statement = statement.order_by(ContentItem.updated_at.desc())
55	
56	        result = await self.session.exec(statement)
57	        items = list(result.all())
58	        return items, len(items)
59	
60	    async def update(self, content_id: str, data: ContentUpdate) -> Optional[ContentItem]:
61	        item = await self.get(content_id)
62	        if item is None:
63	            return None
64	
65	        # Create version snapshot before updating
66	        await self._create_version_snapshot(item)
67	
68	        if data.title is not None:
69	            item.title = data.title
70	        if data.body is not None:
71	            item.body = data.body
72	        if data.channels is not None:
73	            item.channels = json.dumps(data.channels)
74	        if data.ai_model_used is not None:
75	            item.ai_model_used = data.ai_model_used
76	
77	        item.version += 1
78	        item.updated_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
79	
80	        self.session.add(item)
81	        await self.session.commit()
82	        await self.session.refresh(item)
83	        return item
84	
85	    async def delete(self, content_id: str) -> bool:
86	        item = await self.get(content_id)
87	        if item is None:
88	            return False
89	        await self.session.delete(item)
90	        await self.session.commit()
91	        return True
92	
93	    async def transition_status(
94	        self, content_id: str, new_status: ContentStatus
95	    ) -> ContentItem:
96	        item = await self.get(content_id)
97	        if item is None:
98	            raise ValueError(f"Content item '{content_id}' not found")
99	
100	        allowed = VALID_TRANSITIONS.get(item.status, [])
101	        if new_status not in allowed:
102	            raise ValueError(
103	                f"Invalid status transition from '{item.status.value}' to '{new_status.value}'. "
104	                f"Allowed transitions: {[s.value for s in allowed]}"
105	            )
106	
107	        item.status = new_status
108	        item.updated_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
109	
110	        self.session.add(item)
111	        await self.session.commit()
112	        await self.session.refresh(item)
113	        return item
114	
115	    async def get_versions(self, content_id: str) -> list[ContentVersion]:
116	        statement = (
117	            select(ContentVersion)
118	            .where(ContentVersion.content_id == content_id)
119	            .order_by(ContentVersion.version_number.desc())
120	        )
121	        result = await self.session.exec(statement)
122	        return list(result.all())
123	
124	    async def _create_version_snapshot(self, item: ContentItem) -> ContentVersion:
125	        version = ContentVersion(
126	            content_id=item.id,
127	            version_number=item.version,
128	            title=item.title,
129	            body=item.body,
130	            ai_model_used=item.ai_model_used,
131	        )
132	        self.session.add(version)
133	        return version

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	from datetime import datetime, timezone
4	from typing import Optional
5	
6	from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession
7	from sqlmodel import select
8	
9	from app.models.content import ContentItem, ContentStatus
10	from app.models.schedule import ScheduleEntry, ScheduleStatus
11	
12	
13	class ScheduleService:
14	    def __init__(self, session: AsyncSession):
15	        self.session = session
16	
17	    async def get_calendar(
18	        self, start: datetime, end: datetime, channel: Optional[str] = None
19	    ) -> list[ScheduleEntry]:
20	        """Get scheduled items within a date range, optionally filtered by channel."""
21	        statement = select(ScheduleEntry).where(
22	            ScheduleEntry.scheduled_date >= start,
23	            ScheduleEntry.scheduled_date <= end,
24	            ScheduleEntry.status != ScheduleStatus.CANCELLED,
25	        )
26	        if channel:
27	            statement = statement.where(ScheduleEntry.channel == channel)
28	        statement = statement.order_by(ScheduleEntry.scheduled_date)
29	        result = await self.session.exec(statement)
30	        return list(result.all())
31	
32	    async def schedule(
33	        self, content_id: str, scheduled_date: datetime, channel: str
34	    ) -> ScheduleEntry:
35	        """Schedule a content item for publishing on a given date and channel."""
36	        item = await self.session.get(ContentItem, content_id)
37	        if item is None:
38	            raise ValueError(f"Content item {content_id} not found")
39	
40	        if item.status not in (ContentStatus.VALIDATED, ContentStatus.SCHEDULED):
41	            raise ValueError(
42	                f"Content must be validated before scheduling (current: {item.status})"
43	            )
44	
45	        entry = ScheduleEntry(
46	            content_id=content_id,
47	            scheduled_date=scheduled_date,
48	            channel=channel,
49	        )
50	        self.session.add(entry)
51	
52	        item.status = ContentStatus.SCHEDULED
53	        item.scheduled_date = scheduled_date
54	        item.updated_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
55	        self.session.add(item)
56	
57	        await self.session.commit()
58	        await self.session.refresh(entry)
59	        return entry
60	
61	    async def reschedule(
62	        self, content_id: str, new_date: datetime, channel: Optional[str] = None
63	    ) -> ScheduleEntry:
64	        """Reschedule a content item to a new date (and optionally new channel)."""
65	        statement = select(ScheduleEntry).where(
66	            ScheduleEntry.content_id == content_id,
67	            ScheduleEntry.status == ScheduleStatus.PENDING,
68	        )
69	        result = await self.session.exec(statement)
70	        entry = result.first()
71	        if entry is None:
72	            raise ValueError(
73	                f"No pending schedule entry found for content {content_id}"
74	            )
75	
76	        entry.scheduled_date = new_date
77	        if channel:
78	            entry.channel = channel
79	        entry.updated_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
80	        self.session.add(entry)
81	
82	        item = await self.session.get(ContentItem, content_id)
83	        if item:
84	            item.scheduled_date = new_date
85	            item.updated_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
86	            self.session.add(item)
87	
88	        await self.session.commit()
89	        await self.session.refresh(entry)
90	        return entry
91	
92	    async def export_ical(
93	        self, start: Optional[datetime] = None, end: Optional[datetime] = None
94	    ) -> str:
95	        """Export scheduled items as an iCal (.ics) string."""
96	        from icalendar import Calendar, Event
97	
98	        statement = select(ScheduleEntry).where(
99	            ScheduleEntry.status != ScheduleStatus.CANCELLED
100	        )
101	        if start:
102	            statement = statement.where(ScheduleEntry.scheduled_date >= start)
103	        if end:
104	            statement = statement.where(ScheduleEntry.scheduled_date <= end)
105	        statement = statement.order_by(ScheduleEntry.scheduled_date)
106	
107	        result = await self.session.exec(statement)
108	        entries = list(result.all())
109	
110	        cal = Calendar()
111	        cal.add("prodid", "-//Verbum Content Calendar//EN")
112	        cal.add("version", "2.0")
113	        cal.add("calscale", "GREGORIAN")
114	
115	        for entry in entries:
116	            item = await self.session.get(ContentItem, entry.content_id)
117	            title = item.title if item else f"Content {entry.content_id}"
118	
119	            event = Event()
120	            event.add("uid", f"{entry.id}@verbum")
121	            event.add("dtstart", entry.scheduled_date)
122	            event.add("summary", f"[{entry.channel}] {title}")
123	            event.add("description", f"Channel: {entry.channel}\nStatus: {entry.status}")
124	            event.add("created", entry.created_at)
125	            cal.add_component(event)
126	
127	        return cal.to_ical().decode("utf-8")
128

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	import logging
4	from typing import AsyncIterator
5	
6	from anthropic import APIError, AsyncAnthropic, RateLimitError
7	
8	from app.core.config import get_settings
9	
10	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
11	
12	# Default model — can be overridden via ANTHROPIC_MODEL env var or constructor
13	DEFAULT_MODEL = "claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
14	
15	
16	class ClaudeProvider:
17	    """Anthropic Claude AI provider with streaming support."""
18	
19	    def __init__(
20	        self,
21	        api_key: str | None = None,
22	        model: str | None = None,
23	    ) -> None:
24	        settings = get_settings()
25	        resolved_key = api_key or settings.anthropic_api_key
26	        if not resolved_key:
27	            raise ValueError(
28	                "Anthropic API key is required. Set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in your environment."
29	            )
30	        self._client = AsyncAnthropic(api_key=resolved_key)
31	        self._model = model or DEFAULT_MODEL
32	
33	    async def generate(
34	        self, prompt: str, system_prompt: str, max_tokens: int
35	    ) -> str:
36	        try:
37	            message = await self._client.messages.create(
38	                model=self._model,
39	                max_tokens=max_tokens,
40	                system=system_prompt,
41	                messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}],
42	            )
43	            return message.content[0].text
44	        except RateLimitError:
45	            logger.warning("Claude rate limit hit, raising to caller")
46	            raise
47	        except APIError as exc:
48	            logger.error("Claude API error: %s", exc)
49	            raise
50	
51	    async def generate_stream(
52	        self, prompt: str, system_prompt: str, max_tokens: int
53	    ) -> AsyncIterator[str]:
54	        try:
55	            async with self._client.messages.stream(
56	                model=self._model,
57	                max_tokens=max_tokens,
58	                system=system_prompt,
59	                messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}],
60	            ) as stream:
61	                async for text in stream.text_stream:
62	                    yield text
63	        except RateLimitError:
64	            logger.warning("Claude rate limit hit during stream, raising to caller")
65	            raise
66	        except APIError as exc:
67	            logger.error("Claude API error during stream: %s", exc)
68	            raise

63	class OpenAIProvider:
64	    """OpenAI ChatGPT AI provider with streaming support."""
65	
66	    def __init__(
67	        self,
68	        api_key: str | None = None,
69	        model: str | None = None,
70	    ) -> None:
71	        settings = get_settings()
72	        resolved_key = api_key or settings.openai_api_key
73	        if not resolved_key:
74	            raise ValueError(
75	                "OpenAI API key is required. Set OPENAI_API_KEY in your environment."
76	            )
77	        self._client = AsyncOpenAI(api_key=resolved_key)
78	        self._model = model or DEFAULT_MODEL
79	
80	    async def generate(
81	        self, prompt: str, system_prompt: str, max_tokens: int
82	    ) -> str:
83	        try:
84	            response = await self._client.chat.completions.create(
85	                model=self._model,
86	                max_tokens=max_tokens,
87	                messages=[
88	                    {"role": "system", "content": system_prompt},
89	                    {"role": "user", "content": prompt},
90	                ],
91	            )
92	            return response.choices[0].message.content or ""
93	        except RateLimitError:
94	            logger.warning("OpenAI rate limit hit, raising to caller")
95	            raise
96	        except APIError as exc:
97	            logger.error("OpenAI API error: %s", exc)
98	            raise
99	
100	    async def generate_stream(
101	        self, prompt: str, system_prompt: str, max_tokens: int
102	    ) -> AsyncIterator[str]:
103	        try:
104	            stream = await self._client.chat.completions.create(
105	                model=self._model,
106	                max_tokens=max_tokens,
107	                messages=[
108	                    {"role": "system", "content": system_prompt},
109	                    {"role": "user", "content": prompt},
110	                ],
111	                stream=True,
112	            )
113	            async for chunk in stream:
114	                if chunk.choices and chunk.choices[0].delta.content:
115	                    yield chunk.choices[0].delta.content
116	        except RateLimitError:
117	            logger.warning("OpenAI rate limit hit during stream, raising to caller")
118	            raise
119	        except APIError as exc:
120	            logger.error("OpenAI API error during stream: %s", exc)
121	            raise

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	import asyncio
4	import logging
5	from typing import Any
6	
7	from app.services.validation_service import Citation, ValidationResult
8	
9	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
10	
11	_MAX_RETRIES = 3
12	_BACKOFF_BASE = 1.0  # seconds
13	
14	
15	class NotebookLMProvider:
16	    """NotebookLM validation provider with retry logic and citation parsing."""
17	
18	    def __init__(self, notebook_id: str | None = None) -> None:
19	        self._notebook_id = notebook_id
20	        self._client: Any = None
21	
22	    def _get_client(self) -> Any:
23	        """Lazily initialise the NotebookLM client."""
24	        if self._client is None:
25	            try:
26	                from notebooklm import NotebookLM  # type: ignore[import-untyped]
27	
28	                self._client = NotebookLM()
29	            except Exception:
30	                logger.error("Failed to initialise NotebookLM client", exc_info=True)
31	                raise
32	        return self._client
33	
34	    async def is_available(self) -> bool:
35	        """Check whether the NotebookLM service is reachable."""
36	        try:
37	            self._get_client()
38	            return True
39	        except Exception:
40	            return False
41	
42	    async def validate(
43	        self, content: str, content_type: str
44	    ) -> ValidationResult:
45	        """Validate content via NotebookLM with retry + exponential backoff."""
46	        prompt = (
47	            f"Analise o seguinte conteúdo católico ({content_type}) quanto à "
48	            "precisão doutrinária. Identifique quaisquer problemas teológicos "
49	            "e forneça citações das fontes. Responda com uma pontuação de 0 a 100 "
50	            "indicando a precisão doutrinária.\n\n"
51	            f"{content}"
52	        )
53	
54	        last_error: Exception | None = None
55	        for attempt in range(_MAX_RETRIES):
56	            try:
57	                result = await self._ask(prompt)
58	                return self._parse_result(result)
59	            except Exception as exc:
60	                last_error = exc
61	                if attempt < _MAX_RETRIES - 1:
62	                    delay = _BACKOFF_BASE * (2 ** attempt)
63	                    logger.warning(
64	                        "NotebookLM attempt %d failed, retrying in %.1fs: %s",
65	                        attempt + 1,
66	                        delay,
67	                        exc,
68	                    )
69	                    await asyncio.sleep(delay)
70	
71	        logger.error("NotebookLM validation failed after %d retries", _MAX_RETRIES)
72	        raise RuntimeError(
73	            f"NotebookLM validation failed after {_MAX_RETRIES} retries"
74	        ) from last_error
75	
76	    async def _ask(self, prompt: str) -> Any:
77	        """Send a question to NotebookLM and return the raw AskResult."""
78	        client = self._get_client()
79	        try:
80	            return await asyncio.to_thread(
81	                client.ask, prompt, notebook_id=self._notebook_id
82	            )
83	        except Exception as exc:
84	            # Catch RPCError and other transport errors
85	            raise RuntimeError(f"NotebookLM RPC error: {exc}") from exc
86	
87	    def _parse_result(self, result: Any) -> ValidationResult:
88	        """Parse an AskResult into a structured ValidationResult."""
89	        raw_answer = getattr(result, "text", str(result))
90	        citations = self._parse_citations(result)
91	        score = self._extract_score(raw_answer)
92	        flags = self._extract_flags(raw_answer)
93	
94	        return ValidationResult(
95	            score=score,
96	            flags=flags,
97	            citations=citations,
98	            provider="notebooklm",
99	            raw_answer=raw_answer,
100	        )
101	
102	    def _parse_citations(self, result: Any) -> list[Citation]:
103	        """Extract citations from AskResult.references."""
104	        references = getattr(result, "references", None) or []
105	        citations: list[Citation] = []
106	        for idx, ref in enumerate(references, start=1):
107	            source_id = getattr(ref, "source_id", "") or str(idx)
108	            cited_text = getattr(ref, "text", "") or getattr(ref, "content", "") or ""
109	            citations.append(
110	                Citation(
111	                    citation_number=idx,
112	                    source_id=source_id,
113	                    cited_text=cited_text,
114	                )
115	            )
116	        return citations
117	
118	    @staticmethod
119	    def _extract_score(raw_answer: str) -> float:
120	        """Attempt to extract a numeric score (0-100) from the answer text."""
121	        import re
122	
123	        # Look for patterns like "score: 85", "pontuação: 90", "85/100", "85%"
124	        patterns = [
125	            r"(?:score|pontuação|precisão)[:\s]*(\d{1,3})",
126	            r"(\d{1,3})\s*/\s*100",
127	            r"(\d{1,3})\s*%",
128	        ]
129	        for pattern in patterns:
130	            match = re.search(pattern, raw_answer, re.IGNORECASE)
131	            if match:
132	                value = int(match.group(1))
133	                if 0 <= value <= 100:
134	                    return value / 100.0
135	        # Default to a moderate score if parsing fails
136	        return 0.5
137	
138	    @staticmethod
139	    def _extract_flags(raw_answer: str) -> list[str]:
140	        """Extract doctrinal flags/issues from the answer text."""
141	        flags: list[str] = []
142	        lower = raw_answer.lower()
143	        flag_keywords = [
144	            ("heresia", "Possível heresia detectada"),
145	            ("erro doutrinário", "Erro doutrinário identificado"),
146	            ("imprecisão", "Imprecisão teológica"),
147	            ("contradição", "Contradição com fontes oficiais"),
148	            ("problema", "Problema teológico identificado"),
149	        ]
150	        for keyword, flag_text in flag_keywords:
151	            if keyword in lower:
152	                flags.append(flag_text)
153	        return flags
154

49	@lru_cache
50	def get_validation_service() -> ValidationService:
51	    """Create and cache the ValidationService with primary and fallback providers."""
52	    settings = get_settings()
